chunks.text: The first campus of its kind in India University of Southampton Delhi is the first campus of its kind in India - and a proud part of University of Southampton, a global Top 100 university, and part of the Russell Group, the UK's top-tier of elite universities. We are regulated in India by University Grants Commission (UGC) to operate our campus and deliver programmes. Ranked top 100 in the world Source: QS World University Rankings 2026 Top 15 UK institution for employability Source: QS Graduate Employability Rankings, 2022 Founding member of the Russell Group A group of prestigious UK research-intensive universities, and equivalent to the US ‘Ivy League’
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi: Overview and Rankings

chunks.text: The spirit of festival took over the university in full swing as students, faculty and staff came together to celebrate Navratri. Each day, students and staff embraced a different colour to represent different colours of the Navratri palette. The entire university was covered in a bright canvas of hues, from passionate reds to bright yellows and soothing blues, showcasing the accord and enthusiasm of our university. This week was not just about the colours but a showcase that the university is all set for the coming festive season. From Colours to Celebration: Dandiya Night Was a Hit! The festive season is here, on October 1 , the campus came to life with its first ever celebration of the dandiya night. It was an event filled with passion, celebration with a twist of tradition. Students and faculty came together to celebrate this vibrant tradition. Organized by the Student Council in collaboration with the Cultural Club, the event was the perfect finale to a week of festivities and celebration. Dressed in dazzling traditional attire, students twirled and danced to the energeticbeat of dhol and dandiya creating an unforgettable night with a perfect blend ofculture, community and rhythm. We would like to express our gratitude to all those whoparticipated and made this event so special. Lights, Lehengas, and Beats: Diwali Celebration On October 17 , the campus buzzed with excitement with the celebration of Diwali. Thefestival of lights! The campus was transformed into a radiant spectacle of lightand colour. Strings of diyas, fairy lights, and rangolis adorned every corner, creating anenchanting atmosphere that reflected the essence of Diwali – the triumph of light overdarkness and good over evil. The evening featured different stalls, showcasing the creativity of our talentedstudents. From handmade accessories to gift cards to charming bracelets to gaming stalls;we had it all. And no celebration is complete without food! From spicy chaat and samosasto some refreshing iced teas. The combination of flavours, music, and joy madethe evening truly unforgettable. The night concluded with the rhythmic beatsof the dhols, energy and laughter, and with this the festive season came to an end. Wethank you all for making it a Diwali we all will remember.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi's Festive Navratri, Dandiya, and Diwali Celebrations

chunks.text:
We are the first university to receive approval by the University Grants Commission (UGC) to set up a campus in India under National Education Policy (NEP) 2020. We are the only global top 100 university with a campus in India and a UK Russell Group – research intensive - university. We are opening our doors to students in August 2025.

Ranked top 100 in the world
Source: QS World University Rankings 2025
Global 'Top 110' best university for employability
Source: QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022
UK's #15 university for graduate prospects
Source: Times and Sunday Times Good University Guide 2025

Supporting internationalisation in India
University of Southampton Delhi is the only Foreign Higher Educational Institution with a full licence approved by University Grants Commission (UGC) to run a campus in India. We are proud to be involved in a new chapter of internationalisation, as envisioned in the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, helping to underpin momentum for “internationalisation at home” by promoting India as a global study destination.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi: Rankings, Approval, and Global Vision

chunks.text:
You might also want to know
How can I meet your team?
You can meet us online, and in a selection of major cities in India. You can see when and where, by visiting the Events area of our website. When event tickets are available, you will be able to book a physical event, or to register for an on online session, from the event page. The best way to see campus is to book a pre-planned tour from our events page.

When will the next academic year start?
You can see the most up to date information about start dates on our page showing key information.

Should I wait to make my application to study?
We encourage students to make their application to study at University of Southampton Delhi as soon as they can. This means we can issue an early Conditional Offer and that students will have the best the chance to meet their offer conditions. If demand is high, we may choose to close applications earlier than mentioned, but students with Offers to study would receive preferential treatment.
chunks.title: Prospective Student FAQs: Meetings, Start Dates, and Application Advice

chunks.text: Our campus An innovative learning environment with versatile teaching spaces, including lecture theatres, seminar rooms, and collaborative workspaces, all designed to support diverse learning styles and encourage interdisciplinary exchange. A strong sense of place Design of our campus embraces University of Southampton's Triple Helix approach, incorporating education, research and knowledge exachange. This will enable collaboration between academia, industry, and government. This integration of research, innovation, and enterprise will create new opportunities for students and academics to engage with real-world challenges and drive forward advancements in their fields.Beyond its functional role, the campus is designed to foster a strong sense of place. Our designs honour the University of Southampton’s heritage while celebrating India’s rich cultural identity, blending local influences with a global outlook. Interiors meet international standards of comfort and refinement, combining natural materials and forms inspired by Southampton’s green landscapes with patterns and motifs drawn from Indian architectural and craft traditions. Built for teaching and learning Adaptability and efficiency are vital to the design of campus, with multi-purpose areas transitioning seamlessly between formal teaching, independent learning and research and workplace activities. This maximises resource use while promoting engagement between students and faculties.The campus is currently being outfitted for the first cohort of students in August 2025.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Campus Vision and Design

chunks.text: Our campus is located at International Tech Park Gurgaon, Near Sec 59, Off, Golf Course Ext Rd, Gurugram, Haryana 122101. The best way to see campus is to book a pre-planned tour from our events page. Study at your own speed Choose from study pods and tables located throughout campus, collaborate on larger group work in informal spaces, book a private meeting room or visit the centrally located quiet study and reading room. A range of teaching spaces Lessons are delivered in multi-functional spaces, featuring modular layouts adaptable to different teaching styles, and more traditional tiered lecture theatres. Information rich Our library is a rich vein of information to mine for students, staff and entrpreneurs. Readers have access to on-campus physical and digital resources as well as inter-library digital loans. Technology enabled learning Our modern campus features high speed wi-fi and charging points adjacent to learning and study locations. Video screens are used for teaching and we typically record lectures for on-demand playback. Truly accessible Our multi-floor campus is for all, with lifts to upper floors, ramped exterior access and interior passageways and room spaces designed for accessibility. A sustainable future The International Tech Park has USGBC LEED Platinum certification, offering rich landscape and Green Environment in urban settings. Our campus offers air conditioning and Higher MERV & UVGI for effectiveness of air filters.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Campus Facilities and Sustainability

chunks.text: Financial laboratory Home to our Bloomberg Terminals, a gateway to global financial data, news and analytics. Students gain experience of the identical technology and software used by major financial institutions, professional traders, government agencies and 325,000 of the world's most influential decision-makers. We expect that the 'FinTech Lab' could be used for private study, small tutorials, society events and trading competitions. Computing laboratory Our computing laboratory is a dedicated computer science facility for both individual and project work. It's a lively, communal space, where students will find support from both a programming help desk for programming-related questions, and IT help desk for other queries. The lab will be also be used for activities parallel to study, such as the Global Game Jam, as well as career events and workshops Electronics laboratory Our electronics lab is integral to course modules that require 'test and measure' activities. Bespoke lab benches hold standard test equipment such as a PSU, an oscilloscope/logic analyser, a digital bench multimeter, an arbitrary waveform generator, all linked to a control PC and screen.
chunks.title: Specialized Financial, Computing, and Electronics Laboratories

chunks.text: Premium shared facilities As part of an established technology park, our campus benefits from additional high-quality facilities. This is a great chance to get to know the neighbours and network with local business and entrpreneurs. Some facilities may need to be booked and may incur an additional access charge. Informal places to network Socialise and relax as you eat Collaborate and work together Where learning meets tech and business Our campus is at the heart of a 60 acre International Technology Park, home to 8 million square feet of premium business space. The 'new growth corridor', just off Golf Course Extension Road near Sector 59, is a bustling location supported by multiple amenities and well-planned infrastructure. University shuttle bus Students at the official student hostel can use the university's private shuttle bus to travel free of charge between their accommodation and campus. Private shuttle bus The dedicated International Tech Park shuttle connects staff, students and visitors to the local Rapid Metro Station at Sector 55/56 Gurgaon. Travelling by taxi Our campus is conveniently located off major routes and intersections in Sector 59 just to the south of Delhi, and is a 45 minute drive away from Indira Gandhi international airport. The wider region around campus hosts restaurants, hotels, a golf course, equestrian centre and the amenties you would expect from a bustling Technology Park.
chunks.title: International Tech Park Gurgaon Amenities and Transport

chunks.text: You might also want to know How can I travel between the student hostel and campus? Perhaps the best way to get to campus in the morning from our student hostel, or back there again at night, is the private shuttle bus. This free service will run at regular intervals every weekday. Can I visit the campus in person or book a tour? Our campus is located at International Tech Park Gurgaon, Near Sec 59, Off, Golf Course Ext Rd, Gurugram, Haryana 122101. The best way to see campus is to book a pre-planned tour from our events page. How can I meet your team? You can meet us online, and in a selection of major cities in India. You can see when and where, by visiting the Events area of our website. When event tickets are available, you will be able to book a physical event, or to register for an on online session, from the event page. The best way to see campus is to book a pre-planned tour from our events page.
chunks.title: FAQs: Campus Travel, Tours, and Team Meetings

chunks.text: Undergraduate admissions It is easy to make an application for a bachelor's undergraduate programme by following our advice. Our undergraduate admissions process At the undergraduate level, we accept results provided through the CBSE, CISCE and various State Boards as well as international boards such as the Cambridge Board and the International Baccalaureate (IB) Board. School leaving examinations including the Cambridge IGCSE, the American High School Diploma, the International Baccalaureate (The Diploma Programme and The Career-related Programme), and the 12-year General Secondary School Certificate Examination conducted in the Gulf countries specified by the Association of Indian Universities (AIU) may be submitted. Acceptable results Students appearing for Class 12 examinations conducted by various Boards are eligible to apply Entrance tests We do not conduct a standardised entrance test or require that applicants appear for an entrance test.
chunks.title: Undergraduate Admissions Process and Accepted Boards

chunks.text: Important admissions advice Applicants can use predicted scores to support an application to study until final grades are released by the relevant Board or school. Applicants should send these these final grades to us as soon as they are available. We cannot enrol new students using their predicted scores.Applicants must include a personal statement when they make an application to study.We use a letter of recommendation to get to know individual applicants better. Teachers and faculty will have formed a good picture of an applicant over many years, and we want to build on that strong relationship.Applicants should make sure that contact information for any referees is up to date and correct. We may need to get in touch with a referee when we consider an application to study. Refund policy Applicants are expected to pay a deposit of 10% of the tuition fee to secure a place. The first of three instalments of tuition fees is payable by September 8, 2025, the start of the academic term. For any amount paid prior to September 8, 2025, the Distance Selling regulations, applicable to UK universities, mandate us to compulsorily refund any amount paid within 14 days of the acceptance of an offer in full, without any deductions or processing fees as laid out in the Refund Policy. We never hold back an amount if the student has received a conditional offer and is not in a position to meet the academic conditions specified. Tuition Fee refund policy (pdf document)
chunks.title: Admissions Advice, Predicted Scores, and Refund Policy

chunks.text: Supporting documents Applicants will need to support an application to study with documents to prove who they are and what they have studied before. We suggest that applicants have digital copies of these documents easily available to upload before they start to fill in an application to study. Proof of identity An Aadhar Card or passport is a suitable proof of identity for an application to study. Letter of recommendation This must be on a letterhead from the applicant's school, signed and stamped. Class 10 certificate Or an equivalent to this certificate. Higher secondary Class 12 certificate Or an equivalent to this. Predicted Scores or pre-Board results may be submitted pending the announcement of final results. English Language proficiency results (optional) Valid proof of IELTS, TOEFL, PTE or Duolingo results in case we cannot offer an English language waiver.
chunks.title: Essential Supporting Documents for Undergraduate Applications

chunks.text: You might also want to know How much does an application to study cost? There is normally a 2,000 INR application assessment fee which students would need to pay in order for us to process their application for those starting in August 2026. Should I wait to make my application to study? We encourage students to make their application to study at University of Southampton Delhi as soon as they can. This means we can issue an early Conditional Offer and that students will have the best the chance to meet their offer conditions. If demand is high, we may choose to close applications earlier than mentioned, but students with Offers to study would receive preferential treatment. How can I meet your team? You can meet us online, and in a selection of major cities in India. You can see when and where, by visiting the Events area of our website. When event tickets are available, you will be able to book a physical event, or to register for an on online session, from the event page. The best way to see campus is to book a pre-planned tour from our events page.
chunks.title: Undergraduate Application Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

chunks.text: International admissions. International students bring diversity and vibrancy to our campus. We welcome applications from all parts of the world. The University of Southampton community welcomes international students from every part of the world. Our Delhi campus opened in August 2025 and houses an International Student Office to support students studying at our campus in India.
chunks.title: Welcome for International Students at University of Southampton Delhi

chunks.text: Application process. The entry requirements for each programme are stated on the programme web pages, with additional information for students holding other qualifications at University of Southampton international entry equivalencies. All programmes are taught in English, and we require students to meet English proficiency requirements. These requirements may be met through school-level English language studies, or by appearing for one of the tests that we accept as evidence of English language proficiency. There is a common application form for all students, and an application fee of Rs. 5,500/- is payable at the time of submitting your application form. Academic transcripts must be accompanied by a translation into English for us to consider your application. Additionally, we will require the accreditation status of the last institution attended. For detailed programme-specific eligibility, please refer to details on individual programme pages.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi International Application Process

chunks.text: Fee structure. International student fees are specified separately for each programme. They are applicable to those who do not hold an Indian passport and are not Overseas Citizens of India (OCI). Overseas Citizens of India (OCIs) are considered under the Indian student fee structure. For international fee information, please refer to 'Our fees' on individual programme pages.
chunks.title: International Student Fee Structure and OCI Policy

chunks.text: International Student Office. The International Student Office serves as a central support hub for students from diverse cultural backgrounds. Whether you need assistance settling into university life, navigating a new culture, or general academic guidance, our team is here to support you throughout your journey. The International Student Office offers guidance with: Pre-arrival and arrival support Cultural and academic transition Day-to-day student life. You can reach out to the office whenever you require support.
chunks.title: Support Services from the International Student Office

chunks.text: Your Student Visa. International students must obtain a visa as per Government of India’s regulations as specified from time to time. You must hold a valid Student Visa to study in India for the anticipated full duration of the programme of study. The student, or student’s guardian where applicable at any stage of the process, is responsible for applying for the Student Visa, payment of all visa and associated fees and expenses and compliance with visa requirements, documentation and processes required by the Government of India. International students must have a valid offer from University of Southampton Delhi before applying for a Student Visa. To apply for a visa: Hold an unconditional offer to study a programme with University of Southampton Delhi. Students need to confirm a place in student housing and provide proof of accommodation to obtain a Student visa. Our Delhi campus offers fully furnished student housing, integrated with support services for you to settle into a new country quickly and easily. After accepting your offer and paying the tuition deposit, apply for your student visa. You can find information on how to make your visa application using the Study In India Visa Guidelines or the Authorized Visa Portal. Register on the Study in India portal, as required for visa processing. Your visa must be endorsed to University of Southampton. Upon enrolment, the university will register your details on the Foreign Student Information System (FSIS) portal. On arrival in India, all international students must register at the Foreigners Registration Office (FRO) / Foreigners Regional Registration Office (FRRO) as required by regulations specified by the Government of India from time to time. Please start this process immediately on arrival in India, after registering yourself on the FRO/ FRRO portal. You are required to complete FRRO registration within 14 days of your arrival in India using these helpful Study In India FRRO Guidelines.
chunks.title: Indian Student Visa and FRRO Registration for International Students

chunks.text: You might also want to know. Who is considered an international student at University of Southampton Delhi? A student who does not hold an Indian passport is considered an international student. However, Overseas Citizens of India (OCI) are classified under the Indian fee structure. What level of English proficiency is required? All programmes are taught in English. You must meet the university’s English language requirements, either through school-level qualifications or by submitting a valid score from an accepted English proficiency test such as IELTS, TOEFL, or equivalent. How do I apply as an international student? You need to complete the application form, upload the required documents (including certified English translations of transcripts), and pay an application fee of INR 5,500. For programme-specific requirements, please check the relevant programme page. What are the tuition fees for international students? International tuition fees are listed separately for each programme. OCI cardholders are subject to the Indian student fee rates. Do I need a visa to study at University of Southampton Delhi? As an international student, you must apply for a Student Visa endorsed to University of Southampton after receiving your offer letter and completing your tuition deposit. Please apply through the official Indian visa portal. What is FRRO registration and how do I complete it? FRRO (Foreigners Regional Registration Office) registration is mandatory for international students and must be completed within 14 days of arrival in India. The university will assist you in initiating this process. Can international students work while studying in India? Currently, the Indian student visa does not permit part-time employment. You are advised to check the most recent government guidelines or speak to the International Student Office for updated information.
chunks.title: International Admissions Frequently Asked Questions

chunks.text:
It is easy to make an application for a master's postgraduate programme by following our advice. Our postgraduate admissions process. Our programmes are suited for those who are completing their first degree and for professionals carrying out work experience. Students appearing for final year bachelor’s examinations are eligible to apply.

Acceptable results: At the postgraduate level, we accept results from various Indian higher education institutions.

Entrance tests: We do not conduct a standardised entrance test or require that applicants appear for an entrance test.

Working professionals: If the applicant is a working professional, we request details of work experience and a resume.
chunks.title: Postgraduate Admissions Process and Eligibility Overview

chunks.text:
Important admissions advice:

Applicants can use predicted scores to support an application to study until final grades are released by the relevant higher education institution. Applicants should send final certificates or transcripts as soon as they are available. We cannot enrol new students using their predicted scores.

Applicants must include a personal statement when they make an application to study. Applicants should upload a letter of recommendation from their current higher education institution, college or university. Alternatively, they may provide a letter of reference from their current employer. Applicants should make sure that contact information for any referees is up to date and correct. We may need to get in touch with a referee when we consider an application to study.
chunks.title: Important Advice for Postgraduate Applications

chunks.text:
Refund policy:

Applicants are expected to pay a deposit of 10% of the tuition fee to secure a place. The first of three instalments of tuition fees is payable by September 8, 2025, the start of the academic term. For any amount paid prior to September 8, 2025, the Distance Selling regulations, applicable to UK universities, mandate us to compulsorily refund any amount paid within 14 days of the acceptance of an offer in full, without any deductions or processing fees as laid out in the Refund Policy. We never hold back an amount if the student has received a conditional offer and is not in a position to meet the academic conditions specified. Tuition Fee refund policy (pdf document)
chunks.title: Postgraduate Tuition Fee Refund Policy

chunks.text:
See our programmes. Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to that delivered to other University of Southampton students. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, postgraduates will study for one year. All programmes start in August, 2026.

Search programmes:

*   **BEng (Hons) Software Engineering**: Learn to develop reliable, complex and secure software systems. Duration: 3 years. Course Type: Full Time. Start Date: Aug, 2026.
*   **BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance**: A prestigious degree for those aiming to enter the fields of commerce, accountancy and finance. Duration: 3 years. Course Type: Full Time. Start Date: Aug, 2026.
*   **BSc (Hons) Business Management**: Learn to think differently about business and inspire change within organisations and society with an interdisciplinary approach. Duration: 3 years. Course Type: Full Time. Start Date: Aug, 2026.
*   **BSc (Hons) Computer Science**: Learn how to design and build software programs and systems, and find out about the latest advances. Duration: 3 years. Course Type: Full Time. Start Date: Aug, 2026.
*   **BSc (Hons) Creative Computing**: Build immersive and engaging digital experiences that bring art, and technology together. Duration: 3 years. Course Type: Full Time. Start Date: Aug, 2026.
*   **BSc (Hons) Economics**: Learn how our economic decisions affect others, and how business and financial institutions' choices can affect our society. Duration: 3 years. Course Type: Full Time. Start Date: Aug, 2026.

View all programmes.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Postgraduate Programmes

chunks.text:
Supporting documents:

Applicants will need to support their application to study with a range of simple documents. We suggest that applicants have digital copies of these documents easily available to upload before they start to fill in an application to study.

*   **Bachelor’s degree**: A final, or provisional degree, may be submitted as part of the application to study.
*   **Academic transcripts**: For all years or semesters as applicable.
*   **Higher secondary certificate**: Or an equivalent to this.
*   **Proof of identity**: An Aadhar Card or passport is a suitable proof of identity for an application to study.
*   **Letter of recommendation**: This must be on a letterhead from the applicant's educational institution or employer, signed and stamped.
*   **Updated resume**: Showing complete details of an applicant's work experience.
*   **English Language proficiency results (optional)**: Valid proof of IELTS, TOEFL, PTE or Duolingo results in case we cannot offer an English language waiver.

Applications are open now for postgraduate students to walk through the doors in August. Follow this link to start an application today.
chunks.title: Required Supporting Documents for Postgraduate Application

chunks.text:
You might also want to know:

*   **What documents do I need to apply for a Postgraduate Master's programme?**
    Academic Certificates (Certificates from previous study), Predicted Achievement (if still completing final year), Academic Transcripts, Proof of English Language proficiency (waiver possible if degree taught in English), Personal Statement (250-500 words), Letter of Reference, Photo-Identity Card (Adhaar card/passport), Resume or Curriculum Vitae (CV).

*   **Should I wait to make my application to study?**
    Students are encouraged to apply as soon as possible for an early Conditional Offer and the best chance to meet conditions, as high demand may close applications earlier.

*   **How much does an application to study cost?**
    There is normally a 2,000 INR application assessment fee for those starting in August 2026.

*   **How can I meet your team?**
    Meet online or in major Indian cities by visiting the Events area of our website to book physical events or register for online sessions. Campus tours can also be booked from the events page.
chunks.title: Postgraduate Application Frequently Asked Questions

chunks.text: You can use this page to make a payment to University of Southampton Delhi. We want to make sure that your payment is applied quickly and correctly. You can help us by following all the instructions on this page. If we don't have all correct information, payments may be lost or delayed.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Payment Overview

chunks.text: Step 1 - Understanding different types of payment. Application Assessment Fee. You must pay an non-refundable Application Assessment Fee after you have made an application to study at University of Southampton Delhi. When we have received your Application Assessment Fee we will process your application to study.Tuition Fee Deposit. You must pay a Tuition Fee Deposit after we make a Conditional Offer to you to confirm that offer. You should do this within 56 days of receiving a Conditional Offer, and no later than 14 days before the start of term.
chunks.title: Understanding University Payments: Assessment Fee & Deposit

chunks.text: Step 2 - Important information to read before you pay. University of Southampton partners with Oxford International Education Group, who are officially authorised to collect payments connected to Applications for study at University of Southampton Delhi. References to Oxford International Education Group, or OIEG, are fully accredited by University of Southampton.We only accept online payments. You must make all payments in Indian Rupees.You cannot pay using alternative methods such as a cheque, cash or banker's draft.You must quote the unique 7-figure Student Identification number when you make a payments. This is to make sure that we can apply any payment to your Application. If you do not quote the correct 7-figure Student Identification number, or you do not supply this information, we will not be able to match any money you send to us to your Application. Your unique 7-figure Student Identification number is on documents and emails that we send to you.
chunks.title: Crucial Guidelines for University Payment Transactions

chunks.text: Step 3 - Making a payment. Application Assessment Fee The non-refundable Application Assessment Fee is 5,500 INR. To make this payment you should:-Complete your application to study.Wait to receive confirmation by email that your application has been received. This email will contain your unique 7-figure Student Identification number which you will need to make a payment.Make a payment using this secure Application Assessment Fee payment link. The value of this payment is already set at 5,500 INR on the payment screen. You must quote your unique 7-figure Student Identification number when you make payment.Wait for confirmation by email that your Application Assessment Fee has been received and attached to your application to study.
chunks.title: Application Assessment Fee Payment Steps

chunks.text: Step 3 - Making a payment. Tuition Fee Deposit Your minimum Tuition Fee Deposit is equal to 10% of the published Tuition Fees for the first year of study. We will reduce the remaining balance of your Tuition Fees by the amount of your Deposit. To make this payment you should:-Wait to receive your Conditional Offer.Confirm your Conditional Offer.Make a payment using this secure Tuition Feed Deposit payment link. You must enter the value of your Deposit on the payment screen. You must quote your unique 7-figure Student Identification number when you make payment.Wait for confirmation by email that your Deposit has been received and attached to your Conditional Offer.
chunks.title: Tuition Fee Deposit Payment Instructions

chunks.text: University of Southampton Delhi and McGraw Hill India unveil second edition of International Relations for Civil Service Aspirants University of Southampton Delhi and McGraw Hill India unveil 2nd edition of International Relations for Civil Service Aspirants by Shri Ashok Kumar and Dr Rajesh Mohan. Governor of Haryana unveils the book authored by Shri Ashok Kumar, IPS (Retd.), and Dr. Rajesh Mohan, IPS National, 4th November 2025: University of Southampton Delhi and McGraw Hill India, hosted the release of the second edition of the book International Relations – For Civil Services and State Services Examinations, a comprehensive guide crafted to help aspirants strengthen their understanding of global affairs and excel in the international relations component of competitive exams. The book was unveiled by Hon’ble Governor of Haryana, Prof. Ashim Kumar Ghosh, in the presence of the authors Shri Ashok Kumar, IPS (Retd.), Former Director General of Police, Uttarakhand and Vice Chancellor, Sports University of Haryana, and Dr Rajesh Mohan, IPS, Deputy Commissioner of Police, Gurugram. The ceremony also witnessed the gracious presence of Shri Dammu Ravi, IFS, Secretary (Economic Relations), Ministry of External Affairs (MEA), Government of India, as Guest of Honour. Professor Eloise Phillips, Academic Provost, University of Southampton Delhi said: “We are delighted to celebrate the book launch with McGraw Hill India and the distinguished authors. International Relations is a valuable resource for civil service aspirants, equipping them with the knowledge and analytical perspective needed to interpret complex global developments and make informed decisions as future leaders in public services. University of Southampton Delhi plays a key role in nurturing such future leaders by providing them with the skills, global exposure, and academic foundation required to build impactful careers on the world stage.” Addressing the gathering, Hon’ble Governor Prof. Asim Kumar Ghosh said, “I am delighted to be part of this meaningful occasion that celebrates knowledge, dedication, and public service. I wholeheartedly laud the authors for their academic contribution. This book will serve as a valuable resource for young learners and civil service aspirants, offering them clarity and perspective on global affairs. International relations is a dynamic field, more relevant today than ever before. It enables us to understand the interconnected world we live in and to approach global challenges with balance and cooperation. India’s foreign policy today reflects confidence, balance, and strategic maturity, promoting global partnerships while safeguarding national interests. Diplomacy, in its truest sense, is not just about politics but about values, vision, and global responsibility. A strong understanding of international relations encourages young minds to think critically, act wisely, and serve society with integrity and purpose. I also appreciate McGraw Hill for publishing such an insightful and timely edition that will guide aspirants and educators alike. University of Southampton deserves appreciation for fostering such academic excellence and nurturing globally aware citizens.” The authors shared their reflections on the motivation behind the book and its second edition. They highlighted the book’s role in connecting theoretical understanding with practical insights from diplomacy and strategy, positioning it as a meaningful contribution to public service education and civil services preparation. This session also provided an engaging opportunity for University of Southampton Delhi students to interact directly with the authors through a Q&A session, allowing them to gain valuable first-hand insights into the complexities of international relations and its relevance to contemporary global affairs. The event also featured insightful discussions on leadership, collaboration, and the relevance of international relations in global management education.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi and McGraw Hill India Unveil International Relations Book

chunks.text: ~Expands Postgraduate Portfolio with the Launch of MSc EconomicsNational, 11th November 2025: University of Southampton Delhi, India’s first UGC-approved international university and the only global top 100 university with a campus in the country, has opened applications for its postgraduate programmes featuring MSc Finance, MSc International Management, and the newly added MSc Economics beginning in August 2026. Strengthening its commitment to advancing transnational education under India’s National Education Policy (NEP), the university continues to offer students the advantage of earning a globally recognised UK degree while studying in India. Commenting on the postgraduate offerings, Professor Eloise Phillips, Academic Provost, University of Southampton Delhi, said, “At University of Southampton Delhi, students receive the same level of academic quality and global recognition as those studying in the UK, without the need to relocate. Our programmes are tailored to address the changing dynamics of global industries and enable graduates to pursue leadership and specialist careers. We look forward to welcoming driven individuals who aspire to experience a Russell Group education in India.”
chunks.title: UoSD Opens Postgraduate Applications with New MSc Economics

chunks.text: Applicants must hold a recognised bachelor’s degree or equivalent professional qualification with a minimum overall score of 60%. UK First-Class and 2:1 degrees will be assessed based on institutional equivalency. Candidates must demonstrate English language proficiency. The postgraduate programmes reflect the university’s commitment to providing globally relevant and industry-aligned education. Each programme has been designed in consultation with industry leaders to balance academic excellence with practical application, equipping students for impactful roles across finance, policy, consulting, entrepreneurship, and international business. Further details on eligibility and applications are available at https://www.delhi.southampton.ac.uk/ At the heart of University of Southampton Delhi’s academic philosophy lies its Triple Helix Model, an integration of education, research, and industry collaboration. The campus offers world-class infrastructure featuring modern classrooms, digital labs, training facilities and a vibrant student hub that fosters innovation and community learning. Backed by over 150 years of academic legacy, University of Southampton stands among the world’s top universities for excellence in business, engineering, computer science, and social sciences. Upholding the same rigorous standards at its Delhi campus, the institution delivers career-focused programmes that prepare graduates to excel in the global job market. Ranked consistently among the top 15 UK universities for graduate employability, the University also features among the top 10 most targeted UK universities by leading employers, reflecting its strong industry connections and commitment to career-ready education.
chunks.title: UoSD Postgraduate Admissions: Eligibility, Curriculum, Campus, and Legacy

chunks.text: About University of Southampton DelhiUniversity of Southampton Delhi, the first UGC-approved international campus in India, drives original thinking, turns knowledge into action and impact, and creates solutions to the world’s challenges. We are ranked 17th in The Times Good University Guide 2026 (UK University Rankings). Our academics are leaders in their fields, forging links with high-profile international businesses and organisations, and inspiring a 25,000-strong community of exceptional students from over 135 countries worldwide. Through our high-quality education, the University helps students on a journey of discovery to realise their potential and join our global network of over 200,000 alumni. The University of Southampton’s Successful Futures Pathway is an award-winning programme designed to help students evaluate their skills, align them with employer expectations, and prepare for successful career pathways. The programmes also offer tailored support and access to a wide range of work experience opportunities both on and off campus. The University is globally recognised for its strong career outcomes, backed by a proven track record in graduate employability and industry engagement. According to the QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022, it ranks among the top 110 institutions worldwide for employability. The University is also listed among the top 15 universities in the UK by The Times and Sunday Times Good University Guide 2025, and features among the top 10 UK universities most targeted by leading employers, as reported in The Graduate Market 2023
chunks.title: About University of Southampton Delhi: Overview, Rankings, and Careers

chunks.text: Press Release: University of Southampton Delhi opens applications for undergraduate admissions 2026 ~ Strengthens STEM Offerings with two new programs - BSc (Hons) Creative Computing and B.Eng. (Hons) Software Engineering ~ Strengthens STEM Offerings with two new programs – BSc (Hons) Creative Computing and B.Eng. (Hons) Software EngineeringNational, ** October 2025: The University of Southampton Delhi, India’s first UGC-approved international university and the only global top 100 university with a campus in the country, has opened applications for its August 2026 undergraduate intake. The campus offers students an opportunity to pursue a prestigious UK degree while studying in India, a milestone moment in advancing transnational education under India’s National Education Policy (NEP). The University of Southampton’s leadership in global higher education was recently recognised as part of the UK government’s landmark visit to India. Professor Andrew Atherton, Vice-President International and Engagement, joined UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer on his first official visit to India as part of a trade delegation bringing together British and Indian business and education leaders to strengthen bilateral ties following the signing of the Free Trade Agreement. Professor Atherton said, “The University of Southampton was proud to become the very first international university approved by the University Grants Commission to establish a campus in India, thanks to our reputation for excellence in education, research and enterprise. Our first students are already benefiting from this flagship initiative, and last week’s UK delegation offered further opportunities for our university to further strengthen our links with India.”
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Opens 2026 Undergraduate Admissions

chunks.text: Students at the University of Southampton Delhi can choose from undergraduate programmes such as BSc (Hons) in Accounting & Finance, B.Sc. (Hons) Computer Science, B.Sc. (Hons) Business Management, and B.Sc. (Hons) Economics. For the August 2026 entry, the portfolio will be expanded to include BSc (Hons) in Creative Computing and B.Eng. (Hons) Software Engineering programmes, designed to meet the needs of today’s global workforce. Applications are officially open. To learn more and apply, visit – www.delhi.southampton.ac.uk/ Students completing Class XII (or equivalent) are eligible to apply. Applicants should have achieved at least 75% in their Standard XII examinations from boards such as CBSE, CISCE, or leading State Boards. Equivalent international qualifications are also accepted, including UK A-Levels and the International Baccalaureate (IB) Diploma with scores typically ranging between 28 and 38 points.Commenting on the launch of admissions for the August 2026 intake, Professor Eloise Phillips, Academic Provost at the University of Southampton Delhi, “After the overwhelming response to our first admissions cycle in 2025 and a warm welcome to our first student cohort, we are delighted to open applications for our second student intake. The success of our inaugural intake shows the strong demand for globally recognised UK education in India. With an expanded portfolio of undergraduate programmes for 2026, we are confident of offering students even more opportunities to build their future with a leading Russell Group university, here in India.”
chunks.title: New Undergraduate Programmes and Eligibility for 2026 Intake

chunks.text: Reflecting global standards, the programmes at the University of Southampton Delhi combine academic excellence with a strong career focus, equipping students with industry-relevant skills and practical exposure across finance, technology, consulting, and entrepreneurship. These three-year undergraduate courses give students the flexibility to study in India while experiencing the same academic rigour as in the UK, including the opportunity for a semester abroad. Following the University’s prestigious Triple Helix model, the campus seamlessly integrates education, research, and industry collaboration to prepare graduates for real-world impact.The new Gurugram campus fosters holistic growth with a student hub, social spaces, library, study zones, IT labs, and training suites. With over 150 years of academic excellence, the University of Southampton is globally renowned for producing outstanding graduates across a range of disciplines, including business, engineering, computer science, and social sciences. Reflecting the same standard of quality, the programmes at the University of Southampton Delhi are designed with a strong career focus, equipping students with industry-relevant skills and fostering meaningful connections that enhance employability in a global job market. The University consistently ranks among the world’s leading institutions, placed in the Global Top 100 and among the Top 15 UK universities for graduate employability. The University is also ranked among the Top 10 most targeted UK universities by leading employers, highlighting its strong reputation for both academic excellence and graduate employability.
chunks.title: UoSD Program Quality, Campus Facilities, and Employability Excellence

chunks.text: About the University of Southampton DelhiThe University of Southampton Delhi, the first UGC-approved international campus in India, drives original thinking, turns knowledge into action and impact, and creates solutions to the world’s challenges. We are ranked among the top 100 institutions globally (QS World University Rankings 2025); the only top 100 university with a campus in India. Our academics are leaders in their fields, forging links with high-profile international businesses and organisations, and inspiring a 25,000-strong community of exceptional students from over 135 countries worldwide. Through our high-quality education, the University helps students on a journey of discovery to realise their potential and join our global network of over 200,000 alumni. The University of Southampton’s Successful Futures Pathway is an award-winning programme designed to help students evaluate their skills, align them with employer expectations, and prepare for successful career pathways. The programmes also offer tailored support and access to a wide range of work experience opportunities both on and off campus. The University is globally recognised for its strong career outcomes, backed by a proven track record in graduate employability and industry engagement. According to the QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022, it ranks among the top 125 institutions worldwide for employability. The University is also listed among the top 15 universities in the UK by The Times and Sunday Times Good University Guide 2025, and features among the top 10 UK universities most targeted by leading employers, as reported in The Graduate Market 2024.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi: Global Rankings, Mission, and Career Support

chunks.text: The University of Southampton’s role as a global leader in higher education has been recognised as part of the UK government’s landmark visit to India. Professor Andrew Atherton, Vice-President International and Engagement at the University of Southampton, joined Prime Minister Keir Starmer as he made his first official visit to India. The trade delegation brought British and Indian business and industry leaders together with the aim of strengthening ties between the two countries following the signing of the free trade agreement. In July, the University of Southampton became the first international university to establish a campus in India under the country’s pioneering National Education Policy which aims to create world-class, work-ready, graduates. The University of Southampton Delhi provides students with an exciting opportunity to earn a prestigious degree from a world Top 100 university without leaving the country, marking a pivotal step in advancing transnational education in India. At a joint press conference with India’s Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Keir Starmer highlighted the global reputation of UK universities such as Southampton. Prime Minister Keir Starmer said: “Our great British universities are admired all over the world for their teaching excellence, high-quality research and commitment to innovation. “I’m delighted that more Indian students will be able to benefit from a world-class British education in the near future – strengthening the ties between our two countries while pumping millions back into our economy and supporting jobs at home.” Professor Atherton said : “The University of Southampton was proud to become the very first international university approved by the University Grants Commission to establish a campus in India thanks to our reputation for excellence in education, research and enterprise. “Our first students are already benefiting from this flagship initiative and this week’s UK delegation offers further opportunities for our university to further strengthen our links with India. “This visit is a powerful commitment to India-UK relations and demonstrates the importance of our higher education sector as we continue to deepen our engagement with the fastest growing country in the world.” Education Secretary Bridget Phillipson said : “British higher education is world-leading – and this expansion shows its global appeal. “Opening new campuses in India will give more young people the chance to benefit from a UK education, while delivering real returns for our universities at home. “As we prepare to set out our International Education Strategy, these new campuses are a marker of our ambition: building long-term partnerships that deliver growth, innovation and opportunity for both countries.” Alison Barrett MBE, Country Director India, British Council said : “The University of Southampton have successfully launched their campus in Delhi NCR and even welcomed their first batch of students. It’s been wonderful to see the first UK university campus being set up in India and congratulations to the university team for this achievement. This milestone underscores the impact of India–UK Vision 2035, showcasing how world-class academic excellence and globally connected opportunities are nurturing talent that will shape the future of both our nations.” The University of Southampton Delhi is located in the thriving business hub of Gurugram, with students enrolled on undergraduate and postgraduate programmes in business, management, computing, economics and finance. Plans to expand with courses in data science, AI and engineering are underway.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Joins UK Trade Delegation in India

chunks.text: National, September 2025 – New students at the University of Southampton Delhi enjoyed face-to-face sessions with industry leaders and entrepreneurs as part of their welcome to campus. The two-week induction and employability programme marked the beginning of an exciting academic journey at the world top 100 university. With a strong emphasis on employability, students were given early exposure to career advancement services, alumni success stories, and interactive sessions designed to help them define their vision of success and explore global career pathways. Sessions featured Dr. Ajay Kohli (Director & Cluster Head, Narayana Health), Sahil Gupta (Partner, Deloitte), Neelendra Singh (Former Managing Director/General Manager, Adidas India), Siddhartha Tandon (Consultant, L.E.K. Consulting), and Dr. Saurabh Agarwal (Chief Manager & Faculty, ULA – Strategy & Finance). Alumni such as Veenu Jain, Chief Operations Officer, Consulting Engineers Group (MSc Management, University of Southampton), and Ritesh Kaushik, Advocate & Partner, Kaushik and Associates (LLM Maritime Law, University of Southampton) also shared their professional journeys and insights. Students further explored the Careers & Employability ecosystem across Delhi and UK campuses, participated in resume clinics and internship pathway briefings, engaged in vision boarding with industry partners, and joined workshops on emotional intelligence, entrepreneurship, and leadership, equipping them with the skills and perspectives to thrive in their university journey and future careers. The first week of induction focused on fostering a sense of community and academic belonging through activities such as a grand welcome ceremony, interactive faculty introductions, campus tours, ice-breaking sessions, and student club showcases. Workshops on student support, wellbeing, and cultural activities provided insights into the inclusive and vibrant campus life. Professor Eloise Phillips, Academic Provost at the University of Southampton Delhi, said: “At University of Southampton Delhi, we are not just preparing students for degrees but for life. This induction programme set the foundation for academic excellence, employability, and personal growth – empowering our students to become future-ready leaders. We look forward to nurturing this cohort as they, while continuing to provide an enriching environment that blends high-quality education with a vibrant campus experience.” This dynamic induction programme not only introduced students to the academic rigour of the University of Southampton but also emphasised personal development, wellbeing, and networking among peers and faculty. The induction concluded with enthusiastic participation from students, academics, and industry guests, reinforcing the University’s commitment to offering a world-class educational experience in India.
chunks.title: UoSD Welcomes First Students with Induction and Employability Programme

chunks.text: About the University of Southampton Delhi The University of Southampton Delhi, the first UGC-approved international campus in India, drives original thinking, turns knowledge into action and impact, and creates solutions to the world’s challenges. We are ranked among the top 100 institutions globally (QS World University Rankings 2025); the only top 100 university with a campus in India. Our academics are leaders in their fields, forging links with high-profile international businesses and organisations, and inspiring a 25,000-strong community of exceptional students from over 135 countries worldwide. Through our high-quality education, the University helps students on a journey of discovery to realise their potential and join our global network of over 200,000 alumni. The University of Southampton’s Successful Futures Pathway is an award-winning programme designed to help students evaluate their skills, align them with employer expectations, and prepare for successful career pathways. The programmes also offer tailored support and access to a wide range of work experience opportunities both on and off campus. The University is globally recognised for its strong career outcomes, backed by a proven track record in graduate employability and industry engagement. According to the QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022, it ranks among the top 125 institutions worldwide for employability. The University is also listed among the top 15 universities in the UK by The Times and Sunday Times Good University Guide 2025, and features among the top 10 UK universities most targeted by leading employers, as reported in The Graduate Market 2024.
chunks.title: Overview of University of Southampton Delhi and Global Rankings
