chunks.text: UK's University of Southampton launched its Indian campus here on Wednesday, becoming the first foreign university to operationalise a full fledged campus in the country. Read article at Press Trust of India UK’s University of Southampton launched its Indian campus here on Wednesday, becoming the first foreign university to operationalise a full fledged campus in the country under UGC norms, officials said. Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, who inaugurated the campus along with Haryana Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini, termed it a significant milestone in India’s higher education landscape. The university received a Letter of Intent (LoI) last year under the University Grants Commission’s (Setting up and Operation of Campuses of Foreign Higher Educational Institutions in India) Regulations to set up a campus in India.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Campus Inaugurated in India

chunks.text: Press Release: Education Minister celebrates the opening of University of Southampton’s flagship Delhi campus India’s top Education Minister, Dharmendra Pradhan, celebrated opportunities University of Southampton Delhi will bring to Indian students at a ceremony held to open the new campus India’s top Education Minister, Dharmendra Pradhan, has celebrated the opportunities that the new University of Southampton Delhi will bring to Indian students at a ceremony held to open the new campus. The minister and other distinguished guests gathered to mark the official launch of the university, which will provide young people with the chance to earn a degree from a world Top 100 university without leaving the country. University of Southampton Delhi is the first campus of its kind in India and a proud part of the University of Southampton – a founding member of the UK’s prestigious Russell Group, celebrated for its research and academic excellence. The University was the first international institution to be granted a licence by India’s Universities Grants Commission under the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and will deliver Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s vision to create world-class, work-ready, graduates. The campus launch marks a pivotal step in advancing transnational education in India and recognises the exceptional academic potential of Indian students and the country’s emergence as a global leader in research, innovation, and enterprise. University of Southampton Vice-Chancellor, Mark E. Smith, said: “The University of Southampton Delhi offers Indian students the chance to earn a world-class degree at a university that is renowned for its excellence in teaching, global research and strong connections with business and industry. “Our Delhi campus builds on our long-standing relationship with India and fulfils our ambition to become a truly global institution, connecting students from around the world through Southampton’s ability to deliver outstanding education and world-leading research with the power to transform lives.” Lindy Cameron, British High Commissioner to India, said: “Congratulations to the University of Southampton on their launch, the first international university to open a comprehensive campus in India under UGC regulations. “The UK and India share a unique living bridge, and this significant announcement demonstrates our commitment to deepen our education partnerships – offering Indian and British students access to new world-class opportunities, and opening the door to new opportunities to increase research collaboration, drive innovation and growth, and inspire the next generation of global leaders.” Alison Barrett, Country Director India, British Council, said: “Congratulations to the University of Southampton on the opening of its campus in Delhi NCR. “This is a remarkable milestone in India–UK bilateral relations and a testament to the transformative impact of educational partnerships. By bringing world-class academic excellence, an industry-aligned curriculum, and globally renowned faculty, this campus will provide global exposure and opportunity for students right here in India.” Professor Andrew Atherton, the University of Southampton’s Vice-President International and Engagement, said: “The University of Southampton Delhi will support the development of a work-ready, globally competitive workforce as India continues to emerge as a global leader in research, innovation and enterprise. “Graduates will receive the same University of Southampton qualification as they do in the UK and that holds strong international recognition and is valued by employers and academic institutions alike.” Initial undergraduate and postgraduate degree programmes will be in Business Management, Accounting and Finance, Computer Science, Economics, Finance, and International Management, with the first cohort of around 150 students taking up their places in August. By 2035, the University plans to welcome up to 5,000 students every year. This will further reinforce India’s emergence as a hub for high-quality higher education while deepening the UK–India strategic partnership in knowledge and innovation. The University has delivered its state-of-the-art campus in Gurgaon alongside partners including Oxford International Education Group (OIEG). The location is close to influential businesses and features excellent facilities, including a student hub, library and IT laboratories. Students at the University of Southampton Delhi will also benefit from links to some of the country’s most influential and leading firms, including Deloitte India, Comviva and Investec, with internships and graduate placement schemes already in place.
chunks.title: Education Minister Celebrates University of Southampton Delhi Campus Opening

chunks.text:
Press Release: Comviva partners with University of Southampton Delhi to empower tech talent in India

Comviva, a global leader in customer experience and data monetization solutions, has signed a strategic Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the University of Southampton Delhi. With its Comviva 2.0 vision of driving innovation, fostering ecosystem partnerships, and empowering the workforce of the future, this collaboration marks a significant step in strengthening industry-academia ties and nurturing a robust pipeline of next-generation digital talent. Through this partnership, Comviva and the University of Southampton Delhi aim to co-develop strategic initiatives focused on talent development, collaborative innovation, and community impact. The engagement will create enriched learning opportunities for students at the University’s Delhi campus by enabling them to work on cutting-edge digital technologies and real-world business challenges. As part of the agreement, Comviva will lead a series of structured engagements throughout the academic year, including drop-in sessions for student interaction, employer talks to share insights on industry trends and career paths, skill development workshops to equip students with practical competencies.

The University of Southampton has established its Indian campus at the International Tech Park in Gurgaon, with the first cohort of students due to start in August. With this move, the University of Southampton has become the first international university to set up a campus in India under the country’s new higher education framework. The initial offerings will be undergraduate and postgraduate degrees in business, computing, economics and finance. Over the coming years, the course offering will expand significantly, to include engineering, healthcare, art and design, social sciences and science and technology. The partnership with Comviva was facilitated by the India Business Group, a leading strategic advisory consultancy that has been assisting the University of Southampton with stakeholder engagement for its India campus.

On this partnership, Rajesh Chandiramani, CEO of Comviva, said, “At Comviva, we believe the future of digital innovation lies in building meaningful partnerships that bridge academia and industry. This collaboration with the University of Southampton reflects our commitment to nurturing future-ready talent, fostering a culture of innovation, and supporting inclusive growth. It is a key pillar of our Comviva 2.0 vision to empower young minds and co-create solutions that address real-world challenges. Beyond talent development, this partnership will also serve as a platform for collaborative R&D, capacity building, and community-focused programs, thereby advancing our shared vision of innovation excellence and long-term societal impact.”

Professor Andrew Atherton, Vice-President International and Engagement at the University of Southampton, said, “This partnership with Comviva reflects our ongoing commitment to create meaningful, career-focused opportunities for our students in India. Through this collaboration, we aim to equip our graduates with the skills and experience required to succeed in a global workplace and competitive industry. We look forward to working with a global company like Comviva across talent development, innovation and broader community engagement.”

Comviva will also enable paid summer internships for University of Southampton Delhi students and defined opportunities for student placements during the academic year. The university students will also have the access to Comviva’s graduate assessment process for eligible students and Comviva has agreed for an intake of graduates for employment, contingent upon successful completion of the assessment centre.
chunks.title: Comviva Partners with University of Southampton Delhi for Tech Talent

chunks.text: The University of Southampton Delhi offers various ways to fund tuition fees and other expenses, including competitive scholarships for academically gifted students. Applicants must apply for and be awarded these scholarships. Specific competitive awards include: The Cambridge Scholarship Award provides support for up to 4 gifted undergraduate students who have successfully completed Cambridge board A levels for one academic year (2025-2026). The Undergraduate Delhi Presidential Scholarship is a full tuition fee scholarship for 3 academic years (2025-2028) of undergraduate study. The Postgraduate Delhi Presidential Scholarship covers full tuition fees for 1 academic year (2025-2026) of postgraduate study. The Future Talent Scholarship supports up to 12 gifted undergraduate or postgraduate students for one academic year (2025-2026).
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Competitive Scholarships Overview

chunks.text: The University of Southampton Delhi offers merit-based scholarships to academically outstanding applicants, such as the L.E.K. Scholar Award, which supports up to 12 undergraduate students enrolled in BSc (Hons) Economics or BSc (Hons) Accounting & Finance programmes for 3 academic years (2025-2028). Additionally, merit bursaries are available to financially support talented students based on assessed need and eligibility. The Future Talent Bursary, for example, is a merit bursary for up to 8 undergraduate students, supporting 3 academic years of study (2025-2028). Applicants must apply for and be awarded a merit bursary.
chunks.title: Merit-based Scholarships and Bursaries at UoSD

chunks.text: The University of Southampton Delhi offers funding support through partnerships with trusted financial institutions for education loans, covering the study period 2025-2028. Frequently Asked Questions address common queries: Self-funding students paying domestic tuition fees are welcome to apply for scholarships or bursaries, provided they meet additional programme-specific criteria. An application to study a specific programme must precede a scholarship or bursary application. Current terms and conditions are available for review. Scholarship or bursary recipients are encouraged and expected to act as Student Ambassadors to promote the University. Awards are disbursed as tuition fee waivers, reducing the payable amount directly, with no cash alternative.
chunks.title: Funding Support and Scholarship Application FAQs

chunks.text: Students at the University of Southampton’s flagship Delhi campus will get opportunities for internships, placements and graduate employment, thanks to an agreement with Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu. A Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) has been signed to strengthen industry-academia collaboration and foster work-ready skills among students, enhancing access to internships and industry placement opportunities. Through this collaboration, students will benefit from Deloitte India’s deep expertise across technology and transformation, strategy, finance, and other critical services. The partnership will facilitate guest lectures, practical learning sessions, and career-shaping work opportunities at the University’s state-of-the-art campus located at the International Tech Park in Gurgaon. The agreement aims to nurture future-ready graduates by connecting academic learning with real-world insights. In addition to student engagement, the partnership lays the foundation for long-term collaboration across innovation, civic and community initiatives, and joint research or knowledge-sharing platforms between Deloitte and the University. Professor Andrew Atherton, Vice-President International and Engagement at the University of Southampton, said: “Our University prides itself on our excellence in education, our world-leading research and our strong global connections with business and industry. This triple helix of education, research and engagement sits at the heart of our strategy and is a defining feature of our Delhi campus. “Our collaboration with Deloitte India will provide students at the University of Southampton Delhi with an exciting opportunity to connect with leaders in their fields to gain valuable real-life insights and experience, as well as work experience and employment opportunities.” Kamlesh Vyas, Partner, Deloitte India added: “Quality higher education is the cornerstone of unlocking India’s demographic dividend. Internationalisation at home with entry of leading foreign universities marks an inflexion point for higher education in India. “We are committed to supporting leading foreign universities entering India and Indian universities as they continue to strive for helping our students meet their aspirations. We believe that it will provide world class higher education and research opportunities to our students within the country. “Our collaboration with the University of Southampton Delhi reflects Deloitte’s continued commitment to our higher education and to nation-building.” Scheduled to open in August, the University of Southampton Delhi is the first international campus approved by the University Grants Commission (UGC) under India’s National Education Policy. The campus will initially offer undergraduate and postgraduate programmes in economics, finance, computing, and business—curricula specifically designed to prepare students for global careers. In addition to receiving support within the campus, students will have access to the wider University’s market-leading employability development offering, consisting of online resources, regular workshops, one-to-one guidance and support with qualified careers professionals. This partnership with Deloitte India adds to a growing list of strategic collaborations at the Delhi campus, including those with Investec and Comviva—further cementing the University’s commitment to integrating industry engagement into academic delivery.
chunks.title: UoSD and Deloitte India Partner for Future Talent

chunks.text:
The University of Southampton Delhi offers high-quality, affordable student accommodation in its hostel, designed as a home away from home. It provides a peaceful residential setting and strong community, ideal for focused study and personal growth. Room features, layout, and floor space vary, and students will be clearly informed upon booking. Due to limited supply, interested applicants should express interest without commitment after applying to study, to receive information on availability and booking.

Accommodation options include single, twin occupancy male, or twin occupancy female rooms, allowing students to share with a known individual or make new friends. All rooms are spacious, fully furnished with a bed, wardrobe, storage, study workstation, and a private en suite washroom with toilet and shower. Some rooms include cooking facilities, with access to larger communal cooking areas available for all. Rooms are air-conditioned, well-ventilated, and feature lockable windows or balcony doors, ensuring high standards of comfort and fit-out. Specific types include 'Twin deluxe with kitchen' for two students, 'Single standard with kitchen', and 'Single standard' rooms, some of which also offer a balcony.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Hostel Overview and Room Types

chunks.text:
The University of Southampton Delhi hostel provides professional housekeeping with daily visits and weekly linen changes, alongside laundry and ironing services. On-site support includes a resident warden available 24 hours for health and wellbeing, reception staff, and male and female security teams.

The hostel fosters a safe, respectful, and community-driven environment within a welcoming, gated community exclusively for university students. Male and female rooms are on separate, access-controlled floors to ensure privacy and dignity, supplemented by on-site security, a resident warden, and female guards stationed outside female quarters. A building curfew is in place for safety, with 24/7 CCTV surveillance in communal areas and security staff conducting regular patrols. The hostel is located in a quiet gated community ensuring true peace of mind.
chunks.title: Hostel Services, Security, and Community Life

chunks.text:
Healthy, convenient catering at the University of Southampton Delhi hostel offers breakfast and evening meals seven days a week, with a weekly menu catering to regional cuisines and dietary requirements. Communal cooking facilities are also available, alongside private options in some rooms. Social spaces in communal areas feature activities like pool, video games, board games, and widescreen televisions, along with shared refrigerator space, pot washing, and drinking water fountains. Scheduled celebration events are also organized throughout the year.

The student hostel offers excellent transport connections, being a short ride from the campus and well-connected to the National Capital Region. A complimentary private shuttle bus runs at regular intervals every weekday. Public transport is accessible via a 10-minute walk to the nearest Metro station. For car or taxi, the campus is approximately a 15-minute drive away, with limited on-street parking near the hostel.
chunks.title: Catering, Social Facilities and Campus Transport

chunks.text:
The University of Southampton Delhi offers premium accommodation with various rental periods at fair prices. Twin occupancy rooms with half board (breakfast and evening meals) cost around INR 4.0 lakh per student for a 40-week contract. Single occupancy rooms with half board for a 40-week contract are approximately INR 6.75 lakh per student. A refundable security deposit equivalent to two months' rent is required.

Standard contracts are for 40 weeks, aligning with the academic year, though some 50-week contracts are available. Students typically move in a few days before term starts in August. Payment options include a 7.5% discount for full rental period payment upfront, or an easy plan of three equal instalments throughout the academic year, with the first due before move-in.
chunks.title: Accommodation Rental Rates and Payment Details

chunks.text:
When can I book a room at the student hostel? You can apply for a room at the student hostel when you have completed your application to study. We expect demand for rooms to be high, but you can express interest in accommodation for free, which does not commit you to any contract. We will be in touch with those who have shown interest in accommodation to advise on availability and how to book a room.

When will the next academic year start? You can see the most up to date information about start dates on our page showing key information.

How can I travel between the student hostel and campus? Perhaps the best way to get to campus in the morning from our student hostel, or back there again at night, is the private shuttle bus. This free service will run at regular intervals every weekday.

Can I visit the campus in person or book a tour? Our campus is located at International Tech Park Gurgaon, Near Sec 59, Off, Golf Course Ext Rd, Gurugram, Haryana 122101. The best way to see campus is to book a pre-planned tour from our events page.
chunks.title: Booking and Campus Visit Frequently Asked Questions

chunks.text: University of Southampton Delhi has announced International Tech Park, Gurgaon, as the location of its state-of-the-art campus. New Delhi, November 2024: University of Southampton Delhi has announced International Tech Park, Gurgaon, as the location of its state-of-the-art campus. Set to be completed by April 2025 with the first intake of students starting in August 2025, the new campus will bring world-class education, research and enterprise opportunities to India through the excellent facilities offered at International Tech Park. The campus will offer several key benefits for students including a dedicated student hub, social and collaboration areas, large library, private study spaces, IT labs and training suites. One other striking feature will be the dedicated research space that is aimed at promoting academic and student collaboration. Professor Andrew Atherton, Vice-President of International and Engagement at the University of Southampton, said: “We chose Gurgaon and the International Tech Park for our new campus because of the city’s position as a thriving hub for business, technology, and innovation in India. “The International Tech Park offers an ideal location, surrounded by cutting-edge industries and dynamic career opportunities, ensuring our students are immersed in a forward-thinking environment. “The campus’s futuristic design will foster creativity and collaboration, with facilities that promote both academic excellence and student wellbeing. “This environment will empower students to not only receive world-class education but also gain the practical skills and industry connections needed to excel in their careers.” The new campus will implement the University of Southampton’s prestigious triple helix model, which combines education, research, and industry collaboration. This exciting opportunity represents the first chance to study in India with a world top 100 UK Russell Group university. Students will graduate with the same degree at University of Southampton Delhi as they will do when studying the same degree in the UK. University of Southampton Delhi will start by offering undergraduate programmes in Computer Science, Business Management, Economics, Accounts and Finance, as well as postgraduate programmes in International Management and Finance. The University will expand its portfolio every year by adding new and emerging programmes. More details on student accommodation will be unveiled soon. The International Tech Park provides students with access to fitness centres, food halls, table tennis zones, and sports facilities including basketball and tennis courts, with a cricket pitch under development. Nearby amenities include golf courses, football grounds, and equestrian centres, further enhancing the overall student experience. Oxford International will act as an operational partner, supplying the expertise needed to uphold the same exceptional standards of learning and employability that students experience in the UK. Set within the 60 acres of International Tech Park, the new campus will be IGBC LEED Platinum pre-certified. The campus is being designed by internationally acclaimed architect Morphogenesis and will be in an exclusive new building that will be accessible to all.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Announces New Gurgaon Campus Location and Programmes

chunks.text: About the University of Southampton The University of Southampton drives original thinking, turns knowledge into action and impact, and creates solutions to the world’s challenges. We are among the top 100 institutions globally (QS World University Rankings 2025). Our academics are leaders in their fields, forging links with high-profile international businesses and organisations, and inspiring a 25,000-strong community of exceptional students, from over 135 countries worldwide. Through our high-quality education, the University helps students on a journey of discovery to realise their potential and join our global network of over 200,000 alumni: www.southampton.ac.uk. Follow us on Instagram and Facebook for more University of Southampton Delhi updates.
chunks.title: Overview of the University of Southampton's Global Excellence

chunks.text: Meet our alumni University of Southampton alumni change lives and communities. Discover their inspiring journeys, see their achievements, and imagine your future as a University of Southampton graduate. Inspirational journeys Dibyayan Ghosh Audit Associate at Grant Thornton - 2022 graduate"The University of Southampton, being part of the prestigious Russell Group, has a strong academic foundation, and I knew it would provide the right platform to build a valuable professional network. That made my decision easier, but what truly convinced me was my interaction with the director for the Indian region at that time. She gave me a clear picture of what to expect in Southampton, and our conversation made me feel confident about my choice. Studying BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance with a placement year meant that I had the opportunity to gain real-world experience before graduating. In my third year, I went through the application process, secured a placement, and worked in a business environment. This experience helped me land a summer internship at Grant Thornton, which eventually led to a graduate role. Working in a multinational firm like Grant Thornton has been incredibly rewarding, especially since the company has a major presence in India and collaborates with some of the top businesses there. Looking back, my proudest achievement isn’t just my academic success, it’s the relationships I built. Four years flew by in what feels like an instant, and I walked away with not just a degree but lifelong friends and professional connections. That network is something I will carry with me forever. If there’s one piece of advice I could give to future students, it would be this: Go for it. Don’t be afraid of making mistakes. No one is waiting to catch you out—everyone is there to support you every step of the way."
chunks.title: University of Southampton Alumni Journeys and Testimonials

chunks.text: Avani Desai Finance Associate at Merck & Co - 2023 graduate"I felt an immediate personal connection with the University of Southampton after meeting their representative, during her visit to India. From the outset, the interaction made me feel genuinely valued, even before submitting my application. What truly stood out to me was the university's support for its students, both during their studies and after graduation.I chose the BSc (Hons) Business Management programme because of its strong practical focus. One of the key reasons was the opportunity to learn about taxes through hands-on simulations, which allowed me to apply theoretical knowledge in real-world scenarios. Adding to this, the support provided by the university was a major factor in my decision, as it gave me confidence in securing meaningful opportunities after graduation.I am thrilled to see the University of Southampton Delhi introducing the BSc (Hons) Business Management in its very first year. This is such a fantastic opportunity for students in India to experience world-class education. Having experienced the programme, myself I know how impactful it can be. It not only builds deep expertise in key management areas but also equips graduates with the skills and adaptability needed to thrive in complex business environments.The programme covers essential aspects of management, with specialised modules in accounting and finance, analytics, marketing, and business management—just like the experience I had in the UK. What is even more remarkable is that the content and learning experience remain consistent with what you would get at the University of Southampton."
chunks.title: Avani Desai's BSc Business Management Experience

chunks.text: Nikhil Sonkamble Senior Financial Analyst at Lytx - 2022 graduate"I had several options in mind when I began looking for master’s programmes in finance, but the University of Southampton stood out for multiple reasons. Being a part of the Russell Group, it has a strong academic reputation and is recognised globally for its research and teaching standards. The MSc Finance programme particularly appealed to me because it offered a solid mix of core finance theories along with practical tools and applications that are aligned with current industry trends. The programme modules, such as Portfolio Theory, Derivatives, Financial Modelling, and Corporate Finance, matched well with my interests and career goals. I also liked how the programme was structured – it wasn't just about textbook knowledge but also included hands-on experiences, access to Bloomberg terminals, and opportunities to apply theory in real-world contexts. That combination of academic depth and practical exposure was key in helping me make my decision.The degree played a significant role in helping me transition into the professional world. The technical skills I acquired, ranging from financial modelling to valuation techniques, are things I use regularly in my current role. Employers recognised the credibility that comes with a University of Southampton degree, especially since it combines strong academic content with real-world application. My dissertation focused on equity valuation, and I was able to apply that directly during interviews and in my job. Also, the way the programme is structured encouraged us to work on group assignments and presentations, which helped me become more confident in communicating financial concepts. Overall, my education laid the foundation for both the hard and soft skills required in my job and continues to give me an edge professionally.Having gone through the programme myself, I can say it is both rigorous and rewarding. What I appreciate most is the way it is designed with the finance industry in mind — there is a strong focus on practical learning and real-world application. I am sure students at the Delhi campus will gain the same valuable exposure and industry-relevant skills that I did."
chunks.title: Nikhil Sonkamble on MSc Finance and Career Transition

chunks.text: Bhavna Sharma Head of Member Retention at Quintessentially - 2020 graduate"The University of Southampton stood out to me as a leading Russell Group university, which was a key factor since I was taking a career break to pursue my Master’s. The programme content was highly relevant, and the university team made me feel supported from the very beginning. I felt valued not just as a prospective student but as someone with defined career goals. That level of personal attention and clarity about the programme’s benefits really helped me make the decision.I am eager to see how my experience in the UK will be mirrored for students at the University of Southampton Delhi. Students will enjoy the same level of preparation and support, thanks to initiatives like the award-winning Successful Futures Pathway. It is a unique university approach which empowers and arms students with careers and employability awareness and skills-building. Through this initiative, everything a student does and learns is assessed against 14 key skills that are important for the world of work and for employers. This ongoing focus helps students understand what employers are looking for, build the right skills, and gain hands-on work experience—all while studying on campus.I am sure students in India will experience the same world-class education and global perspective—without having to move abroad right away. While the Delhi campus doesn’t currently offer an MA in Luxury Brand Management, it does offer other master’s programmes like MSc International Management and MSc Finance. These will provide the same content and learning experience you would get in the UK. Plus, UK degrees are globally respected—so a one-year Master’s from the University of Southampton is equivalent to a two-year Master’s from many non-UK universities."
chunks.title: Bhavna Sharma on University Support and Employability

chunks.text: Remarkable achievements Yash Prakash Finalist Business and Entrepreneurship, 2024 NISAU India-UK Achievers Honours - 2014 graduate"My education in Southampton has been transformative for my life journey. The on-campus experience was invaluable, providing a platform to interact with fellow students from diverse cultures and backgrounds. This exposure broadened my perspectives and deepened my understanding of the world. Witnessing the varied struggles and battles that each person faces served as a humbling experience, reinforcing the importance of empathy and resilience in my personal and professional life." Kaushal Shah Winner Business and Entrepreneurship, 2023 NISAU India-UK Achievers Honours - 2015 graduate"I vividly recall my first day at Southampton, stepping onto the campus with a mix of nerves and anticipation. That experience shaped my academic journey and personal growth in ways I could never have imagined. By bringing world-class education to India, Southampton is expanding its reach and enriching its academic tapestry with diverse perspectives." Rohit Mukul Winner Business and Innovation, 2022 British Council Study UK Alumni Awards - 2017 graduate"Shuffling between (rigorous but rewarding) academics, countless hours at Hartley library and Jubilee sports centre and the vibrant social life taught the most fundamental but extremely critical skill of task prioritisation and goal setting. The diverse cohort at university developed in me interpersonal skills, which I believe are a necessity while building anything – an enterprise or relationships."
chunks.title: Remarkable Alumni Achievements and Insights

chunks.text: You might also want to know How many students will study on each programme? We expect to provide 25 seats on each undergraduate programme, and 20 seats for postgraduate programme, during academic year 25/26. We will provide seats number for academic year 26/27 soon. Should I wait to make my application to study? We encourage students to make their application to study at University of Southampton Delhi as soon as they can. This means we can issue an early Conditional Offer and that students will have the best the chance to meet their offer conditions. If demand is high, we may choose to close applications earlier than mentioned, but students with Offers to study would receive preferential treatment. How much does an application to study cost? There is normally a 2,000 INR application assessment fee which students would need to pay in order for us to process their application for those starting in August 2026.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Admissions Frequently Asked Questions

chunks.text: Watch "Computer science in the age of AI" An insightful conversation between Dr Vishal Talwar (COO at University of Southampton Delhi) and Professor Tim Norman (Head of the School of Electronics and Computer Science at University of Southampton). They explore the University’s distinctive approach to teaching, learning and research in computer science, and how AI (Artificial Intelligence) will shape future work and graduate careers. Viewers will hear about Southampton's innovative pedagogy that delivers world-class teaching methods, and how cutting-edge research translates into real-world impact. Professor Norman also highlights how major investment in technical facilities in Delhi, combined with cross-departmental collaboration with electronics and engineering academics, offers students hands-on experience with the specialist hardware and tools needed to design, develop, test, and refine their projects to industry standards. With the first intake in August 2025, this is a must-watch for anyone interested in University of Southampton Delhi’s BSc (Hons) Computer Science programme.
chunks.title: Computer Science and AI Education at Southampton Delhi

chunks.text: Watch "Economics at the cutting edge" Dr Vishal Talwar, Chief Operating Officer at University of Southampton Delhi, joins Daniel Cernin, Senior Teaching Fellow in Economics, to discuss Southampton’s innovative and globally connected approach to Economics education. This conversation explores how University of Southampton Delhi blends academic excellence with practical experience, giving students the opportunity to earn a world-class degree right here in India. The purpose-built Delhi campus promotes interactive learning through roundtable classrooms, seminar spaces, and a state-of-the-art Financial Laboratory (Fin Lab) featuring Bloomberg Terminals and advanced computing facilities. The university's distinctive Triple Helix model—uniting research, teaching, and enterprise—ensures a dynamic, career-focused learning experience that bridges theory and industry. Whether you are passionate about policy, finance, or economic innovation, this video offers valuable insights into the University of Southampton Delhi BSc (Hons) Economics programme, which equips students for success on the global stage.
chunks.title: Innovative Economics Education and Campus Facilities in Delhi

chunks.text: Watch "Career-ready Accounting and Finance" Explore the diverse career opportunities available at the Southampton Business School as Dr Vishal Talwar, Chief Operating Officer, University of Southampton Delhi and Dr Hesham Bassyouny, Lecturer in Accounting & Finance at Southampton Business School discuss how students are being prepared to thrive in various sectors. Learn how the curriculum integrates artificial intelligence (AI), business-related software, and modern techniques to equip students with practical skills. Discover how strong theoretical foundations, leadership training, and hands-on learning prepare graduates to excel in corporate environments and entrepreneurial ventures alike. The Business School also provides robust career support through initiatives suchs as the Talent Development Programme, offering students practical exposure and real-world business insights. For aspiring entrepreneurs, Dr Hesham shares key advice: take the time to observe your desired industry, understand market trends, and identify growth opportunities. This insightful conversation sheds light on how the University of Southampton is shaping the next generation of business leaders who might be interested in University of Southampton Delhi BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance programme.
chunks.title: Preparing Accounting and Finance Graduates for Career Success

chunks.text: Watch "Shaping global finance leaders" Hear Dr Hesham Bassyouny, Lecturer in Accounting & Finance at Southampton Business School, and Dr Vishal Talwar, Chief Operating Officer at University of Southampton Delhi, as they discuss how Southampton Business School is reshaping Accounting & Finance education through innovation and sustainability-focused learning. Discover how the integration of industry-based assessments, Bloomberg terminals, financial labs, and business-specific software is providing students with hands-on skills to excel in real-world financial environments. Sustainability is at the heart of our curriculum, aligning with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Our students have the unique opportunity to create sustainability reports for companies, supported by our Global Reporting Initiative (GRI) accreditation - equipping them with the expertise to lead in sustainable financial practices.Learn more about the University of Southampton Delhi BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance programme.
chunks.title: Sustainable Accounting and Finance Education at Southampton Business School

chunks.text:
Admissions are now open for a place at our campus in the Delhi NCR starting in August. A clear and fair process: We rely on academic scores, along with a personal statement and a reference letter to understand if an applicant can make the most of our programmes. Applicants with different interests and aspirations, and from different states, cultures and even nationalities are welcome at our campuses. Each one makes a unique contribution to the diversity that is our way of life. We accept applications in rounds (sometimes called windows). We reserve the right to extend or modify dates based on results from a number of Boards and Examining Bodies, and we may not accept new applications for a specific programme when it has reached capacity. We may take applications after the final date shown, but only on the basis of having a strictly limited number of spaces in the August 2026 intake. We will review applications, and contact individuals with a decision according to this schedule:

Application round | Applications open on | Applications close on
---|---|---
Early round 1 | Wednesday 01 October 2025 | Monday 15 December 2025
Early round 2 | Friday 19 December 2025 | Thursday 29 January 2026
Early round 3 | Monday 09 February 2026 | Friday 17 April 2026
Regular round | Saturday 18 April 2026 | Start of term
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Admissions Overview and Schedule

chunks.text:
Eligibility criteria: We want to make offers that fairly assess every applicant wherever they are based. The criteria to secure an offer is typically:
- An overall score in Grade 12 for undergraduate admissions, or a final university or college score for postgraduate programmes.
- One or more subjects may be specified as compulsory preparation for some degrees. Applicants should verify subject requirements for their programme if required, and whether an indicative mark in the subject or level of study is specified.
- Demonstrable and proven proficiency in English for the specific programme.

We do not require that applicants appear for admission tests as part of the application process. While our programmes do not compulsorily require test scores, you may volunteer a test result such as College Board SAT (DI Code: 41330) or ETS GRE, if you believe it strengthens your application. You may also provide contextual information. We are committed to ensuring that all applicants with the potential to succeed, regardless of their background, are encouraged to apply to study with us. The additional information gained through contextual data enables us to recognise an applicant's potential in the context of their background and experience. Proficiency in English may be indicated in a number of ways that include widely accepted tests. However, tests are not required if applicants can meet other conditions. Applicants should refer to specific details for each programme to know whether they are eligible to apply. Eligibility criteria do not entitle a candidate to admission - they only indicate the minimum requirement to be considered for admission. Admission and eligibility and requirements for undergraduate and postgraduate study varies, so it is important to check before making an application to study. Individual programmes may also have different criteria which can be seen on individual programme pages.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Eligibility Criteria for Applicants

chunks.text:
Need more help? We strongly suggest that applicants download and read this detailed guidance before starting to make an application. Applicants may also find it helpful to have this guidance to hand as they fill in an application. Applicant guidance (pdf document)

Making an application to study: All applications are made online. To make an application for more than one programme, applicants should complete a separate form for each programme that they are interested in. An application must be completed in a single session, which is likely to take about 45 minutes, because applicants cannot save a partially completed application form. Applicants should have digital copies of documents ready before starting an application. An application processing fee of INR 5,500 must be paid after an application form has been completed and submitted. We cannot process an application until this fee is paid. To make an application to study:
- Choose ‘Apply now’ to go to our online application form.
- Complete the application form and upload all supporting documents.
- After watching the explainer video, add a Personal Statement.
- Upload a letter of recommendation from a school or higher education institution, including up to date contact information of any referees.
- Submit your application along with the payment of the application assessment fee. An application assessment fee of 5,500 INR applies.
chunks.title: Guide to Making an Application to Study at UoSD

chunks.text:
Getting an offer to study: If your application is successful, you will typically receive a Conditional Offer, pending the submission of final grades, English language tests if required, and completion of any other conditions specified. Accept the Conditional Offer within 30 days to move ahead with your admission. Complete a payment of 10% of the first year’s fees within 56 days and secure your place. On receipt of your final grades and on meeting the other conditions specified, you will receive an Unconditional Offer.

Refund policy: Applicants are expected to pay a deposit of 10% of the tuition fee to secure a place. The first of three instalments of tuition fees is payable by September 8, 2025, the start of the academic term. For any amount paid prior to September 8, 2025, the Distance Selling regulations, applicable to UK universities, mandate us to compulsorily refund any amount paid within 14 days of the acceptance of an offer in full, without any deductions or processing fees as laid out in the Refund Policy. We never hold back an amount if the student has received a conditional offer and is not in a position to meet the academic conditions specified. Tuition Fee refund policy (pdf document)
chunks.title: University Offer and Tuition Fee Refund Policy

chunks.text:
Ready to apply now? Applications are open now for the first students to walk through the doors in August 2025. Follow this link to start an application today Apply now

You might also want to know:

Should I wait to make my application to study? We encourage students to make their application to study at University of Southampton Delhi as soon as they can. This means we can issue an early Conditional Offer and that students will have the best the chance to meet their offer conditions. If demand is high, we may choose to close applications earlier than mentioned, but students with Offers to study would receive preferential treatment.

How much does an application to study cost? There is normally a 2,000 INR application assessment fee which students would need to pay in order for us to process their application for those starting in August 2026.

Can I get an offer with predicted scores? We encourage students to apply as soon as possible so that they can receive a Conditional Offer based on predicted scores for their chosen programme. We can accept an application from a student using their predicted grades if we receive their final grades once they are available.

How can I meet your team? You can meet us online, and in a selection of major cities in India. You can see when and where, by visiting the Events area of our website. When event tickets are available, you will be able to book a physical event, or to register for an on online session, from the event page. The best way to see campus is to book a pre-planned tour from our events page.
chunks.title: Apply Now and Admissions FAQs

chunks.text: Frequently asked questions Discover more about the campus, student life and our programmes by seeing what detail others have asked us in the past. You might also want to know Type a word or phrase into the searchbox to filter the complete list of questions. When you see the question you want answered, just click the + (plus) icon to the left of the question to find out more. Search FAQs
chunks.title: Frequently Asked Questions Overview and Search Guide

chunks.text: Can I apply for a scholarship or bursary? We welcome applications for a scholarship or bursary from any self-funding student who will be paying domestic Tuition Fees. Additional criteria, such as programme eligibility, are shown clearly on individual award pages. You must first make an application to study a specific programme before you make an application for a scholarship or bursary. How can I contribute to the community as a scholarship student? We would encourage - and expect - that the recipient of a scholarship or bursary will act as a Student Ambassador to promote University of Southampton Delhi while they study with us. How would I receive my scholarship or bursary? There is no cash alternative to the waiver of Tuition Fees made under a scholarship or bursary. We will reduce the amount of Tuition Fees that you will pay by the value of the award. What are the terms and conditions for scholarships? You can see our current terms and conditions for scholarships and bursaries here. What is a merit bursary? A merit bursary is a form of financial aid for students who otherwise would not be able to fund their education. It is based on the financial ability of students to support educational aims as well, as merit criteria based on academic performance. Will I lose my deposit if I get a full scholarship? If you are awarded a full scholarship - which waives 100% of Tuition Fees - we will refund the value of any Deposit that you may have already paid.
chunks.title: General Scholarship and Bursary Application Guide

chunks.text: Is there an application fee waiver for Cambridge students applying to the University of Southampton Delhi? Yes, all Cambridge students applying to any undergraduate programme at University of Southampton Delhi between 01 May and 31 May 2025 will have their application fees waived. Who is eligible to apply for the Cambridge Scholarship Awards? The Cambridge Scholarship Awards are open to all Cambridge A-Level graduates in India who are applying to selected undergraduate programmes at University of Southampton Delhi. What additional benefits are available to L.E.K. Scholars? In addition to the scholarship, recipients of the L.E.K. Scholar Award will receive priority consideration for L.E.K. Consulting’s Summer Internship Programme. This provides an excellent opportunity for hands-on experience and professional growth, subject to performance and eligibility. Who is eligible to apply for an L.E.K. Scholar Award? Students with an unconditional offer to study for BSc (Hons) Economics or BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance can be considered for an L.E.K. Scholar award. Why should I apply for an L.E.K. Scholar Award? This scholarship will represent a highly prestigious accolade for our top students, aligned with one of the world’s most respected strategy consulting firms and offering an outstanding pathway into top-tier global career opportunities.
chunks.title: Specific Scholarship Awards: Cambridge and L.E.K.

chunks.text: Can I get an offer with predicted scores? We encourage students to apply as soon as possible so that they can receive a Conditional Offer based on predicted scores for their chosen programme. We can accept an application from a student using their predicted grades if we receive their final grades once they are available. How much does an application to study cost? There is normally a 2,000 INR application assessment fee which students would need to pay in order for us to process their application for those starting in August 2026. Should I wait to make my application to study? We encourage students to make their application to study at University of Southampton Delhi as soon as they can. This means we can issue an early Conditional Offer and that students will have the best the chance to meet their offer conditions. If demand is high, we may choose to close applications earlier than mentioned, but students with Offers to study would receive preferential treatment. When will the next academic year start? You can see the most up to date information about start dates on our page showing key information.
chunks.title: General Admissions Process and Application Information

chunks.text: How do I apply as an international student? You need to complete the application form, upload the required documents (including certified English translations of transcripts), and pay an application fee of INR 5,500. For programme-specific requirements, please check the relevant programme page. Who is considered an international student at University of Southampton Delhi? A student who does not hold an Indian passport is considered an international student. However, Overseas Citizens of India (OCI) are classified under the Indian fee structure. What are the tuition fees for international students? International tuition fees are listed separately for each programme. OCI cardholders are subject to the Indian student fee rates. Do I need a visa to study at University of Southampton Delhi? As an international student, you must apply for a Student Visa endorsed to University of Southampton after receiving your offer letter and completing your tuition deposit. Please apply through the official Indian visa portal. Can international students work while studying in India? Currently, the Indian student visa does not permit part-time employment. You are advised to check the most recent government guidelines or speak to the International Student Office for updated information. What is FRRO registration and how do I complete it? FRRO (Foreigners Regional Registration Office) registration is mandatory for international students and must be completed within 14 days of arrival in India. The university will assist you in initiating this process.
chunks.title: International Student Admissions and Visa Regulations

chunks.text: What documents do I need to apply for a Postgraduate Master's programme? Academic Certificates (Certificates from previous study) Students who are still to complete their final year in college, are required to provide Predicted Achievement i.e. the predicted score from your college. You will be required to provide the Final Certificates as soon as available. Academic Transcripts (transcripts from previous study) Proof of English Language proficiency You can see more guidance about English Language on our website. If you have successfully completed a degree taught and assessed in English up to two years preceding the date of first enrolment at the University of Southampton, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Personal Statement (250-500 words) You can see more guidance about Personal Statements on our website. We suggest you keep a draft version ready, explaining your motivation to study your subject of interest at Southampton, and how you intend to pursue a career in the area. Letter of Reference Photo-Identity Card (such as Adhaar card/passport) Resume or Curriculum Vitae (often known as a CV) What documents do I need to apply for an Undergraduate Bachelor's programme? Academic Certificates (certificates from Grade XII and Grade X) Students who are yet to complete their final year in school are required to provide Predicted Achievement i.e. the predicted score from your school. Please ensure that your predicted subject scores are specified in the document. Academic Transcripts (transcripts from Grade XII and Grade X) Proof of English Language proficiency You can see more guidance about English Language on our website. If you have been taught and assessed in English, including at a CBSE or CISCE school, for your final four years of high school, you may choose to use your English scores to meet the English Language requirement. Personal Statement (250-500 words) You can see more guidance about Personal Statements on our website. We suggest you keep a draft version ready, explaining your motivation to study your subject of interest at Southampton, and how you intend to pursue a career in the area. Letter of Reference Photo-Identity Card (such as Aadhaar card/passport) What level of English proficiency is required? All programmes are taught in English. You must meet the university’s English language requirements, either through school-level qualifications or by submitting a valid score from an accepted English proficiency test such as IELTS, TOEFL, or equivalent.
chunks.title: Required Documents for Postgraduate and Undergraduate Applications

chunks.text: How can I earn an Honours degree in just 3 years? The University Grants Commission (UGC) has mandated University of Southampton Delhi to make sure that programmes delivered in India are the same as those delivered at University of Southampton in the UK. Three years is the standard length of an undergraduate Honours degree in England where University of Southampton sets academic standards. This 3 year length reflects a trend towards accelerated learning where students see a return on their tuition fees as quickly as possible, whilst still maintaining high academic standards and the quality of the degree awarded. University of Southampton 3 year undergraduate degrees, and 1 year postgraduate degrees, are accepted widely internationally. Why is an Honours degree good for me? An Honours degree, shown with (Hons) in the programme title, mean that students will understand the fundamentals of their discipline and then build specialisation and perspective through optional modules. These fast-paced, intense programmes deliver essential learning in the first academic year and offer optional course modules, or electives, in the second year. Honours degrees are offered at University of Southampton campuses across the world, reflecting teaching and learning to a higher academic standard, meaning that an Honours degree would hold more value post graduation for future study and career development. What is an option? Our programmes are designed to make sure that students quickly understand the fundamentals of their discipline, and then build specialisation and perspective through electives, or optional, modules. These optional modules are typically offered in academic years 2 and 3, adding depth and breadth to core learning from previous years. As these options are often built around real-world trends, the specific choice of module available to students from year to year may vary. Why does a dissertation include research? In a dissertation module, students complete an independent research project. The objective is for students to take their learning, and build something of their own, demonstrating the skills that they have acquired, and the independence that they will need in their future career. The research orientation is an intrinsic part of an Honours degree. Why is my degree award BSc (Hons) or BEng (Hons)? Degrees with a BA and BSc award do not represent a stream, but a broad categorisation that reflects student choice and leaning. Our BSc (Hons) and BEng (Hons) undergraduate degrees are oriented towards specialist, technical roles in industry with an analytical approach, not science subjects. What is the academic pass mark? The pass mark for all programmes within University of Southampton Delhi is 40%. This is the same pass mark across all University of Southampton campuses and reflects the high and equivalent academic standards across all our campuses. How many students will study on each programme? We expect to provide 25 seats on each undergraduate programme, and 20 seats for postgraduate programme, during academic year 25/26. We will provide seats number for academic year 26/27 soon.
chunks.title: Programme Structure, Academic Standards and Student Capacity

chunks.text: Can I visit the campus in person or book a tour? Our campus is located at International Tech Park Gurgaon, Near Sec 59, Off, Golf Course Ext Rd, Gurugram, Haryana 122101. The best way to see campus is to book a pre-planned tour from our events page. How can I travel between the student hostel and campus? Perhaps the best way to get to campus in the morning from our student hostel, or back there again at night, is the private shuttle bus. This free service will run at regular intervals every weekday. When can I book a room at the student hostel? You can apply for a room at the student hostel when you have completed your application to study. We expect demand for rooms to be high, but you can express interest in accommodation for free, which which does not commit you to any contract. We will be in touch with those who have shown interest in accommodation to advise on availability and how to book a room.
chunks.title: Campus Visits, Accommodation, and Student Travel

chunks.text: What is an education loan? An education loan is a private commercial agreement between a student (or their family) with a financial institution to cover the costs of education. These independent organisations are not part of University of Southampton Delhi, any links are provided for information only and should not be taken as a recommendation. It is important to take financial and legal advice before entering into any agreement. Application for an education loan will not guarantee an offer to study, or enrolment on a programme, at University of Southampton Delhi.
chunks.title: Understanding Education Loans for Study Costs

chunks.text: How can I meet your team? You can meet us online, and in a selection of major cities in India. You can see when and where, by visiting the Events area of our website. When event tickets are available, you will be able to book a physical event, or to register for an on online session, from the event page. The best way to see campus is to book a pre-planned tour from our events page. Did you still want to know more? If you did not find the answer you wanted, get in touch and ask us your question. We will reply to you individually, so please make sure you leave an email and a phone number where we can contact you. Get in touch
chunks.title: Meeting the Team and General Contact Information

chunks.text: 12 L.E.K. Scholar Awards worth ₹1.5 lakh each per academic year to award academically meritorious students joining in August 2025. The University of Southampton Delhi, in association with global strategy consulting firm L.E.K. Consulting, introduces the L.E.K Scholar Awards, a new merit-based scholarship programme aimed at recognising and supporting academically outstanding students applying for the August 2025 intake. Through this collaboration, 12 scholarships of ₹1.5 lakh per academic year will be awarded to selected students pursuing the BSc (Hons) Economics or BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance programmes. These awards will contribute towards covering tuition fees and will be offered to students who meet and exceed the academic admission criteria of their chosen course. Recipients of the L.E.K. Scholar Award will also receive priority consideration for L.E.K. Consulting’s Summer Internship Programme (2026), offering a valuable opportunity for practical exposure and professional development, subject to performance and eligibility. To be eligible, students must hold an unconditional offer to study BSc (Hons) Economics, or BSc (Hons) Accounting & Finance at the University of Southampton Delhi, starting August 2025. Professor Eloise Phillips, Academic Provost and Associate Vice-President (International) University of Southampton Delhi, said: “This collaboration is more than a scholarship initiative; it’s an effort to shape future-ready professionals. We believe that today’s students need more than classroom education—they need opportunities to build confidence, interact with industry, and explore practical applications of their knowledge. Through this association, our students gain both financial encouragement and valuable professional insight.” Speaking about the collaboration, Ashwin Assomull, Partner and Head of the Global Education Practice at L.E.K. Consulting said: “The University of Southampton is globally recognised for academic excellence, and we are proud to support this next chapter in Delhi through the L.E.K. Scholar Awards. By supporting talented students early, we hope to foster a strong foundation for their careers. We see this as more than financial aid—it’s an investment in India’s brightest minds. We hope this collaboration sparks ambition, curiosity, and readiness in students as they take their first steps into the world of business and finance.” The merit scholarships are further evidence of a strong developing partnership between University of Southampton Delhi and L.E.K. Consulting. The partnership includes commitment by L.E.K. Consulting to a series of structured engagements throughout the academic year, from employer talks to skill development workshops for students and more.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi and L.E.K. Consulting Launch Merit Scholarships

chunks.text: Press Release: University of Southampton Delhi announces collaboration with Cambridge International. The University introduces the Cambridge Scholarship Awards – up to four scholarships exclusive to this year’s A-level students in India - as well as application fee waiver and other benefits for Cambridge students. National, 7 May 2025: The University of Southampton Delhi today announced a collaboration with the International Education group at Cambridge University Press & Assessment (Cambridge), the world’s largest provider of international education affiliated with the University of Cambridge and responsible for qualifications such as the Cambridge IGCSE, AS and A Level. Cambridge currently operates across 810 schools in India. As part of this collaboration, the University of Southampton Delhi announces the introduction of the Cambridge Scholarship Awards, up to four exclusive competitive scholarships for Cambridge A-Level graduates in India applying for the August 2025 intake. The University also announces that it will waive application fees for all Cambridge students applying between 1st May and 31st May 2025. Cambridge applicants will additionally benefit from a priority application review through a dedicated evaluation team, ensuring an efficient and streamlined admissions process. The Cambridge Scholarship Awards offer a flat contribution of INR 220,000 towards tuition fees for the academic year 2025–2026 at the University of Southampton Delhi. The University’s Scholarship Committee will select recipients based on outstanding academic performance and excellence in A-Level results. Commenting on this collaboration, Dr Vishal Talwar, Chief Operating Officer, University of Southampton Delhi said: “We’re excited about this collaboration because it brings together the best of both worlds – the world class education offered at University of Southampton Delhi and the academic excellence and global perspective of Cambridge. Cambridge A-Level graduates bring a solid academic foundation and international outlook, making them a perfect fit for the opportunities and career pathways at our university. Through this association, we aim to empower the next generation of leaders, equipping them with the skills and resources needed to thrive on the global stage.” Vinay Sharma, Sr. VP International Education, South Asia, Cambridge, said- “Cambridge qualifications are recognised by universities worldwide for their academic rigour and outstanding preparation for higher education, enabling students from diverse backgrounds to thrive in a range of educational settings. Our collaboration with University of Southampton Delhi reflects our global reach by providing Cambridge students in India with direct access to a world-class academic experience without leaving the country. Cambridge is opening new pathways to international education and ensuring that our students are ready for the world.”
chunks.title: UoSD Announces Collaboration with Cambridge International & Scholarship Awards

chunks.text: With over 150 years of academic excellence, the University of Southampton is globally renowned for producing outstanding graduates across a range of disciplines, including business, engineering, computer science, and social sciences. The University consistently ranks among the world’s leading institutions, placing in the Global Top 100 and among the Top 15 UK universities for graduate employability. The University is also ranked among the Top 10 most targeted UK universities by leading employers, highlighting its strong reputation for both academic excellence and graduate employability. About the University of Southampton The University of Southampton drives original thinking, turns knowledge into action and impact, and creates solutions to the world’s challenges. We are among the top 100 institutions globally (QS World University Rankings 2025). Our academics are leaders in their fields, forging links with high-profile international businesses and organisations, and inspiring a 25,000-strong community of exceptional students from over 135 countries worldwide. Through our high- quality education, the University helps students on a journey of discovery to realise their potential and join our global network of over 200,000 alumni: www.southampton.ac.uk The University of Southampton’s Successful Futures Pathway is an award-winning programme designed to help students evaluate their skills, align them with employer expectations, and prepare for successful career pathways. The programmes also offer tailored support and access to a wide range of work experience opportunities both on and off campus. The University is globally recognised for its strong career outcomes, backed by a proven track record in graduate employability and industry engagement. According to the QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022, it ranks among the Top 125 institutions worldwide for employability. The University is also listed among the Top 15 universities in the UK by the Times and Sunday Times Good University Guide 2025, and features among the Top 10 UK universities most targeted by leading employers, as reported in The Graduate Market 2024.
chunks.title: University of Southampton: Global Rankings, Employability, and Pathways

chunks.text: University of Southampton Delhi is proud to scholarships open to new students starting their undergraduate or postgraduate taught studies at University of Southampton Delhi in August 2025. Delhi Presidential Scholarships – offers one undergraduate scholarship covering full tuition fees for the three-year period of academic study of the degree, and one postgraduate competitive scholarship, covering full tuition fees for one academic year of the postgraduate degree with University of Southampton Delhi. Applications for this award are now closed for August 2025. Future Talent Scholarship – offers up to 12 competitive scholarships to undergraduate and postgraduate students, offering an INR 220,000 tuition fee reduction in the first academic year of study Applications for this award are now closed for August 2025. Cambridge Scholarship Award – offers up to 4 competitive scholarships to Cambridge A-Level graduates applying for undergraduate programmes, offering an INR 220,000 tuition fee reduction in the first academic year of study. Applications for this award are now closed for August 2025. Please refer to the scholarship summary page for information on deadlines, as well as eligibility criteria, terms and conditions, how we award the scholarship, and when you’ll find out if your application is successful. We strongly recommend that you keep a copy of answers to longer questions in a separate document. If your internet connection fails while you are part way through completing the form, or your web browser crashes, you can easily paste them from the copy you have kept elsewhere into a new form. To be considered for these scholarships you need to have already made a programme application for August 2025 entry and then submit the scholarship application form below. You will need your Application ID number, which you received when you submitted your programme application. Can I apply for a scholarship or bursary? We welcome applications for a scholarship or bursary from any self-funding student who will be paying domestic Tuition Fees. Additional criteria, such as programme eligibility, are shown clearly on individual award pages. You must first make an application to study a specific programme before you make an application for a scholarship or bursary. Will I lose my deposit if I get a full scholarship? If you are awarded a full scholarship - which waives 100% of Tuition Fees - we will refund the value of any Deposit that you may have already paid. How can I contribute to the community as a scholarship student? We would encourage - and expect - that the recipient of a scholarship or bursary will act as a Student Ambassador to promote University of Southampton Delhi while they study with us. How would I receive my scholarship or bursary? There is no cash alternative to the waiver of Tuition Fees made under a scholarship or bursary. We will reduce the amount of Tuition Fees that you will pay by the value of the award.
chunks.title: Competitive Scholarships and Application Details at University of Southampton Delhi

chunks.text: University of Southampton Delhi is committed to ensuring digital accessibility for all users, in compliance with the Public Sector Bodies (Websites and Mobile Applications) (No. 2) Accessibility Regulations 2018. We are dedicated to providing an online service that is usable and accessible to the widest possible audience, including those with disabilities. This accessibility statement applies to www.delhi.southampton.ac.uk , including the application form within this site. Compliance status: This website and application form are partially compliant with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) version 2.2 AA standard. The non-compliances and exemptions are detailed in this statement. How you can access our website and application form: You should be able to use most of www.delhi.southampton.ac.uk in the following ways: Navigate using a keyboard instead of a mouse. Use a screen reader or speech recognition software. Zoom in up to 300% without the text spilling off the screen. Change fonts, colours, or contrast for better readability. Access AbilityNet for free technology support: https://abilitynet.org.uk/. How you might find it hard to access our website: We are aware of the following issues that might make our website hard to access: General website issues including ARIA labelling on button icons not present, PDF buttons not clearly labelled, some videos lacking captions, and inconsistent accessibility of tables and maps. General application form issues include pages scrolling in two dimensions and not all radio button options being readable under the ‘How did you hear about us?’ question. What to do if you are having problems accessing our website: Request alternative formats by contacting protectingdata@oxfordinternational.com at Oxford International Education Group, 259 Greenwich High Road, London SE10 8NB. We will respond within 30 days with suitable formats like large print, easy read, audio recording, or Braille. Make a complaint: Email protectingdata@oxfordinternational.com for accessibility concerns. The Equality and Human Rights Commission (EHRC) enforces the accessibility regulations. If unsatisfied, contact the Equality Advisory and Support Service (EASS) [+44 (0)808 800 0082]. What we are doing to improve accessibility: We are continuously improving our website through regular reviews, updates, and accessibility training for our teams. We also work with third-party vendors to ensure their services are accessible. Preparation of this accessibility statement: This statement was prepared on 13/01/25, last reviewed on 20/03/25, and tested on 26/03/25 by Simon Brooker – Oxford International Education Group. Non-accessible content: Content outside the scope of regulations includes PDFs created before 23 September 2018 that are not essential, and live video streams without captions. Third-party content: We encourage vendors to provide Accessibility Conformance Reports (ACR) using the Voluntary Product Accessibility Template (VPAT) to improve accessibility of services like forms and timetables.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Website Accessibility Statement
