chunks.text: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy Dr Aparna Pasumarthy is an interdisciplinary researcher and educator, specializing in careers, leadership, and organizational behaviour. She previously taught at Queen Mary, University of London, and is an Associate Fellow of the Higher Education Academy. Her research focuses on the careers of professional men and women in India, especially within multinational corporations. Dr Pasumarthy holds PhD Business and Management from Queen Mary, University of London. She has presented her research at major international conferences and is committed to promoting social justice, inclusion, and sustainability in academia and organisational environments. Research Interests: Career Management & Sustainable Careers Diversity, Equality & Inclusion Ethical Decision Making Flexible Work Leaders and Leadership Development Organisational Behaviour View profile
chunks.title: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy: Interdisciplinary Researcher and Educator

chunks.text: Dr Chitrakalpa Sen Dr Chitrakalpa Sen is Head of Economics at the University of Southampton Delhi. He holds a PhD in Economics from the West Bengal University of Technology, following an MSc and BSc (H) in Economics from the University of Calcutta. His research lies at the intersection of sustainable finance, ESG investing, and financial market dynamics, with particular expertise in volatility modelling, regime switching, and convergence in emerging economies. Chitrakalpa has published widely in leading international journals such as Journal of Economic Surveys, Energy Economics, Resources Policy and the Journal of Cleaner Production, and his work continues to shape debates on the resilience of green investments and the role of financial markets in sustainable growth. Research Interests: Convergence and growth dynamics in emerging economies ESG finance and sustainable investing Financial market complexity and behavioural dynamics Volatility modelling, regime-switching, and higher-order moment connectedness View profile
chunks.title: Dr Chitrakalpa Sen: Head of Economics, Sustainable Finance Expert

chunks.text: Dr Mohammed Anam Akhtar Dr Mohammed Anam Akhtar, currently working as Head of Business Education at University of Southampton Delhi, brings over a decade of distinguished academic, leadership, and industry experience to the role. He has previously served as Assistant Professor of Accounting and Finance and BBA program chair at IMT Dubai, contributed to ACCA program accreditation, and held teaching and consultancy positions at Curtin University (Dubai). Dr Akhtar holds a PhD in Management from NIT India, is a Certified Management and Business Educator (CMBE, UK), and is UGC-NET qualified. His wide-ranging expertise ensures academic rigour and innovation in business programs at the University, enriching the student experience with world-class learning and strong industry engagement. Research Interests: Behavioural Finance Financial inclusion Financial Reporting Financial wellbeing & Financial capability View profile
chunks.title: Dr Mohammed Anam Akhtar: Head of Business Education

chunks.text: Dr Nalini Sharan Dr Nalini Sharan leads the Student Experience portfolio at University of Southampton Delhi, bringing nearly two decades of leadership in higher education, student affairs, and strategic engagement. She is responsible for shaping a student-first culture that fosters wellbeing, inclusion, global awareness, and a vibrant campus life. View profile
chunks.title: Dr Nalini Sharan: Student Experience Portfolio Lead

chunks.text: Dr Nitish Gupta Dr Nitish Gupta joined University of Southampton Delhi in August 2025 as Head of Business (Knowledge Exchange & Enterprise) and as a Lecturer in Business. He brings a proven track record in research, teaching, product innovation leadership and digital transformation to the campus. View profile
chunks.title: Dr Nitish Gupta: Head of Business, Knowledge Exchange & Enterprise

chunks.text: Dr Rajesh Yadav Dr Rajesh Yadav is a seasoned educator with over 20 years of experience in creating innovative and engaging learning environments. Specializing in Cybersecurity, Cyber Threat Intelligence, Digital Forensics, Malware Analysis, Threat Detection, and Ethical Hacking, he combines hands-on experiential teaching with curriculum design to enhance critical thinking and problem-solving skills. He holds a BE, MTech, and PhD in Computer Science and Engineering and has contributed to both national and international academic institutions. He serves as a Lecturer and Senior Tutor at the University of Southampton Delhi, having previously held diverse academic roles across multiple institutions. Dr Yadav’s teaching excellence and contributions to the cybersecurity domain have been recognized with the Excellence in Teaching Award from the University of Southampton Malaysia and the International Educational Achievements Award for his outstanding contributions to the Cybersecurity domain. Research Interests: Advanced Threat Detection & Response Cyber Threat intelligence Information Security & Privacy Malware Analysis Threat Hunting View profile
chunks.title: Dr Rajesh Yadav: Cybersecurity Expert, Lecturer, and Senior Tutor

chunks.text: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy is an interdisciplinary qualitative researcher and passionate educator. She enjoys making complex management theory accessible to students and adopts a student-centric approach to learning and teaching. Dr Pasumarthy previously taught postgraduate and undergraduate management students at Queen Mary, University of London in the UK. Among other professional affiliations, Dr Pasumarthy is an Associate Fellow of the Higher Education Academy, United Kingdom. Dr Pasumarthy’s research expertise spans the fields of careers, leadership, and organizational behaviour. Her on-going research projects focus on the (un)sustainable careers of professional men and women in India; careers of professional women in MNCs in India. She has presented her research at renowned international conferences including the Academy of Management Conference, European Group for Organisation Studies Colloquium, and British Sociological Association Work Employment Society Conference. Combining her expertise with her commitment to inclusion and social justice, Dr Pasumarthy aspires to help construct equitable organisational environments where all individuals can thrive. Prior to joining University of Southampton Delhi, Dr Pasumarthy pursued an MSc in International Management from the University of Bath and earned a PhD Business and Management (no corrections) from Queen Mary, University of London. Thereafter, she was the Garware Post-Doctoral Fellow at the University of Pennsylvania Institute for Advanced Study of India (UPIASI) and a Non-Resident Scholar at the Centre for the Advanced Study of India, University of Pennsylvania. Apart from her academic interests and professional endeavours, Dr Pasumarthy is passionate about living sustainably and conserving the environment.
chunks.title: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy's Academic Profile and Research Expertise

chunks.text: Stay active at our on-site sports facilities. Enjoy basketball, badminton, table tennis, or maintain your fitness routine. Our facilities support your wellbeing and social life, making it easy to stay healthy and active right here on campus. At University of Southampton Delhi, we believe that student life goes beyond the classroom. To support a balanced lifestyle, our campus offers a variety of sports and fitness facilities that encourage students to stay active, connect with peers, and nurture their overall well-being. Students can easily book facilities with on-ground support provided by the Student Experience team. Sports and recreation are at the heart of a vibrant student experience. Whether you’re looking to stay fit, take a break from studies, or simply have fun with friends. Our facilities include: Cricket playground (near Stryker Lab): A well-maintained ground for practice sessions and friendly matches, giving students the perfect space to host cricket matches. Indoor sports courts (Basement Level 1): Dedicated courts for both badminton and table tennis, ideal for quick games between classes or more competitive matches. Multipurpose sports court (behind Tower II): A flexible space designed for a range of sporting activities, perfect for sports (basketball, paddleball, lawn tennis) and group recreation. Gym (Ground Floor, Tower II): In partnership with on-site fitness providers, the University offers students access to a fully equipped gym. Memberships are available at a subsidized rate of Rs. 2,000 per month, making it easier for students to maintain their fitness goals.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi On-Campus Sports and Fitness Facilities

chunks.text: Campus infirmary: Open from 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM, staffed by a qualified nurse to assist with any medical needs. A professional counsellor is available on Mondays and Wednesdays (11:00 AM – 5:00 PM) to offer mental health and wellbeing support. A doctor is available for consultations on Mondays, Tuesdays, Thursdays, and Fridays (12:00 PM – 2:00 PM) to address health concerns and emergencies.
chunks.title: Campus Infirmary Medical and Counselling Services

chunks.text: University accommodation: The accommodation infirmary is staffed by a nurse from 8:00 PM to 8:00 AM, providing 24-hour medical assistance. A counsellor is available on Saturdays (11:00 AM – 5:00 PM) for mental health support. A doctor is available on Sundays (11:00 AM – 1:00 PM) for continued medical care and consultations for residents.

chunks.text: You might also want to know Can I visit the campus in person or book a tour? Our campus is located at International Tech Park Gurgaon, Near Sec 59, Off, Golf Course Ext Rd, Gurugram, Haryana 122101. The best way to see campus is to book a pre-planned tour from our events page. Should I wait to make my application to study? We encourage students to make their application to study at University of Southampton Delhi as soon as they can. This means we can issue an early Conditional Offer and that students will have the best the chance to meet their offer conditions. If demand is high, we may choose to close applications earlier than mentioned, but students with Offers to study would receive preferential treatment. How can I meet your team? You can meet us online, and in a selection of major cities in India. You can see when and where, by visiting the Events area of our website. When event tickets are available, you will be able to book a physical event, or to register for an on online session, from the event page. The best way to see campus is to book a pre-planned tour from our events page.
chunks.title: FAQs: Visiting Delhi Campus, Application Process, and Meeting University Staff

chunks.text: Our campus offers an innovative learning environment with versatile teaching spaces, including lecture theatres, seminar rooms, and collaborative workspaces, designed to support diverse learning styles and encourage interdisciplinary exchange. The campus design embraces the University of Southampton's Triple Helix approach, integrating education, research, and knowledge exchange to foster collaboration between academia, industry, and government. This creates opportunities for students and academics to engage with real-world challenges and drive advancements. The campus is designed to foster a strong sense of place, honouring Southampton’s heritage while celebrating India’s rich cultural identity. Interiors feature international standards of comfort, blending natural materials inspired by Southampton’s green landscapes with patterns and motifs from Indian architectural and craft traditions.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Campus Design and Ethos

chunks.text: The campus design prioritizes adaptability and efficiency with multi-purpose areas that transition between formal teaching, independent learning, research, and workplace activities, maximizing resource use and student-faculty engagement. The campus is being outfitted for its first student cohort in August 2025. It is located at International Tech Park Gurgaon, Near Sec 59, Off, Golf Course Ext Rd, Gurugram, Haryana 122101. Visitors can book a pre-planned tour via the events page. Students can choose from various study spaces like pods, collaborative informal areas, private meeting rooms, and a quiet reading room. Teaching occurs in multi-functional, modular spaces and traditional tiered lecture theatres. The library offers extensive on-campus physical and digital resources, plus inter-library digital loans. The modern campus features high-speed Wi-Fi, charging points, video screens for teaching, and recorded lectures for on-demand playback. Our multi-floor campus is truly accessible, with lifts, ramped exterior access, and interior spaces designed for accessibility.
chunks.title: Adaptable Learning Facilities and Campus Location

chunks.text: The campus promotes a sustainable future, located within the International Tech Park, which holds USGBC LEED Platinum certification, featuring a rich landscape and green urban environment. Our campus includes air conditioning with Higher MERV & UVGI for effective air filtration. Specialized laboratories enhance practical learning: the Financial Laboratory houses Bloomberg Terminals for global financial data, news, and analytics, providing students with industry-standard technology for private study, tutorials, society events, and trading competitions (FinTech Lab). The Computing Laboratory is a dedicated computer science facility for individual and project work, offering support from programming and IT help desks, and hosting events like the Global Game Jam and career workshops. The Electronics Laboratory is crucial for course modules requiring 'test and measure' activities, equipped with bespoke lab benches, standard test equipment (PSU, oscilloscope/logic analyser, digital bench multimeter, arbitrary waveform generator), and control PCs.
chunks.title: Sustainable Campus and Specialized Laboratories

chunks.text: As part of an established technology park, the campus benefits from additional high-quality shared facilities, offering networking opportunities with local businesses and entrepreneurs. The campus is located in the heart of a 60-acre International Technology Park, featuring 8 million square feet of premium business space. This 'new growth corridor' near Sector 59, off Golf Course Extension Road, is a bustling area with multiple amenities and well-planned infrastructure. For transportation, students at the official hostel can use the university's free private shuttle bus between accommodation and campus. A dedicated International Tech Park shuttle connects staff, students, and visitors to the Rapid Metro Station at Sector 55/56 Gurgaon. The campus is also conveniently located near major routes in Sector 59, south of Delhi, approximately a 45-minute drive from Indira Gandhi International Airport. The wider region around campus provides restaurants, hotels, a golf course, and an equestrian centre, along with other amenities expected in a thriving Technology Park.
chunks.title: Campus Integration with Tech Park and Transport Links

chunks.text: Regarding travel between the student hostel and campus, a private shuttle bus offers a free service at regular intervals every weekday. To visit the campus in person or book a tour, our campus is located at International Tech Park Gurgaon, Near Sec 59, Off, Golf Course Ext Rd, Gurugram, Haryana 122101. The best way to see the campus is to book a pre-planned tour from our events page. To meet the university team, you can connect online or at physical events in major cities across India. Event tickets and registration for online sessions are available on the Events area of our website.
chunks.title: Campus Visit and Team Meeting Information

chunks.text: Business Management BSc (Hons) 2025 Program Overview Award: BSc (Hons) Duration: 3 years Course Type: Full Time Start Date: 08 September 2025 Choosing this programme for 2025 This cutting-edge programme builds deep expertise in a specific management area, while developing skills and knowledge for agility and adaptability to navigate complex business environments. The programme covers foundational management through specialised modules in disciplines of accounting and finance, analytics, marketing, and business management. The programme is taught in English, and content is equivalent to a BSc (Hons) Business Management degree delivered to University of Southampton students in other locations. This programme stands out because:- It provides insights into emergent trends to help students thrive in an ever-changing business environment. The careers and employability focus is suited to a wide range of organisations, including start-ups, large corporations, social enterprises, and the not-for-profit sector. Students will investigate a business topic through a dissertation or a business project in part 3. It is accredited by the Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business (AACSB) signalling excellence in business education. What makes the programme unique? Optional modules begin from the second semester itself; students take a high number of optional modules in year 2 to follow their area of interest. The second year envisages that students select six optional modules, or three out of four modules in each semester. Guest lectures and talks are a regular part of the programme for students to network and meet potential employers. In part 3, a year-long dissertation or business project runs along with 6 modules to ensure the rigour of an Honours degree. Which students will benefit the most? Students who are keen to engage with the world of business can quickly cover management theory and practice. Teaching goes beyond the classroom towards more hands-on experiential learning. Students can work towards careers in areas as diverse as analytics and digital marketing through their choice of optional modules.
chunks.title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Programme Overview & Benefits

chunks.text: What you will study As part of this programme, you can: explore current business and management challenges gain an understanding of key business management principles and practices, including accounting, marketing, finance, strategy and entrepreneurship tailor the programme to your interests by choosing optional modules learn directly from people in inspiring jobs through regular guest speakers from industry Our research-focused academics are leaders in their field and keep the programme current. You’ll benefit from our Business School’s close links with industry. Modules with a difference Innovation, Technology and the Environment is an inter-disciplinary module that is offered at two levels in parts 1 and 2. Open modules integrate the latest developments in management and business into the curriculum. Corporate Social Responsibility and Sustainable Business is a compulsory module that mirrors the growing importance of sustainability in modern society and contemporary business. Course modules Students take a number of core and compulsory modules before selecting optional modules based on their interest. The programmes are designed to ensure that students gain the fundamentals of their discipline and then add specialisation and perspective through available optional modules. Optional modules may include modules related to the core discipline as well as options built around contemporary, real-world trends that change from time to time. Find out why, when and how we might make changes. In Year 1, students will take 6 fixed course modules, and 2 optional electives. In Year 2, students will take 2 fixed course modules, and 6 optional electives. In Year 3, students will take 3 fixed course modules, and 4 optional electives.
chunks.title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Curriculum Structure & Key Modules

chunks.text: In Year 1, students must take all these course modules:- Business in Society MANG1048 Digital Technologies in Business MANG1050 Introduction to Accounting and Finance MANG1055 Introduction to Management MANG1003 Introduction to Marketing for Business MANG1053 Management Analysis MANG1047 In Year 1, students must choose two of these optional electives:- Commercial Law MANG1014 Financial Accounting MANG1044 Introduction to Economics For Non-Economists ECON1009 Management Accounting MANG1045 In Year 2, students must take all these course modules:- Critical Perspectives on Organisation and Management MANG2088 Operations Management MANG2021 In Year 2, students must choose three of these optional electives:- Entrepreneurial Management MANG2094 Financial Accounting 2 MANG2003 Human Resource Management MANG2011 Management Ethics MANG2041 Company Law MANG2017 In Year 2, students must choose three of these optional electives:- Consulting: Context, Concepts and Practice MANG2104 Innovation, Technology and the Environment MANG2095 Making Successful Decisions MANG2069 Management Accounting 2 MANG2005 Philosophy of Management and Organisations MANG2057 In Year 3, students must take all these course modules:- Business Project MANG3100 Corporate Social Responsibility and Sustainable Business MANG3029 Strategic Management MANG3008 In Year 3, students must choose two of these optional electives:- Financial Accounting MANG3003 Information, Organisation and Accountability from a Historical Perspective MANG3065 Marketing in the Digital Age MANG3054 Technological Innovation MANG3072 In Year 3, students must choose two of these optional electives:- International Business MANG3105 Management Accounting 3 MANG3006 Management of Information Systems MANG3101 Project Management MANG3034
chunks.title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Core and Optional Modules

chunks.text: Your future career Graduating with a business management degree from a double-world top 100 university will open various career paths for you. You could choose to work in: the financial sector industry retail consultancy public sector Career opportunities Possible roles for Business Management graduates include: business advisor business analyst business development manager chartered management accountant corporate investment banker data analyst Companies you could work at University of Southampton graduates have secured roles in organisations such as: Deloitte Ernst & Young (EY) PricewaterhouseCoopers (PwC) Proctor & Gamble Times Group Careers services at University of Southampton Delhi As well as support from our team in Delhi, you’ll have access to our market-leading employability development offering. This includes: online resources regular workshops one-to-one guidance and support with qualified careers professionals through our MyCareer platform Through our e-mentoring scheme, you’ll also have access to: tailored career planning advice on employability skills industry insight We have incorporated our employability skills model, Successful Futures, into our curriculum. Our links with multi-national and industrial organisations mean that you can expect to engage with employers and explore potential career paths. You will receive a personalised employability programme that will provide excellent prospects for securing a premium placement and financial security on graduating. We also host regular alumni events hosted in person across major cities in India, allowing you to grow your own networks. Our support is ongoing, and once you graduate, you’ll be part of a global community of over 220,000 alumni that spans over 180 countries.
chunks.title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Career Paths & Employability Services

chunks.text: Key information To help plan your year we have published University of Southampton Delhi term and holiday dates in advance, but these may be subject to change. We expect to provide 25 seats on this programme for academic year 25/26. Eligibility criteria Students from diverse backgrounds with a keen interest in business are encouraged to apply. They would require a basic level of mathematics. For Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE): Minimum 78% in your India Senior School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) examination (overall) and a minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Secondary School Examination (Standard X) examination For Council Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (CICSE): Minimum 78% in your Indian School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) examination (overall) and minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (Standard X) examination For State Board: Minimum 83% in your Higher Secondary Certificate (Standard XII) examination (overall) and Minimum 75% in Mathematics in your Senior Secondary School Certificate (Standard X) examination International Baccalaureate: 34 points and above, including 17 points at Higher Level and 4 points in Maths and English at Standard level A levels: AAB, plus Grade B or above in GCSE Maths English language requirement We accept several ways of meeting our English language requirement. These include: IELTS Test, with scores of 6.5 overall (6.0 in reading, writing, speaking, and listening) other tests listed on our website an approved academic qualification having the required grade If you’ve been taught and assessed in English, including at a CBSE or CISCE school, for your final 4 years of high school, and have achieved at least 70% in English Language, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Refer to our English language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements. Our academic admissions criteria Typical academic admissions criteria represent the usual level of academic attainment an applicant is expected to achieve prior to being admitted to that programme. These criteria are not intended to provide a guarantee that all applicants who satisfy the criteria may be offered a place, nor that all applicants to be made an offer will receive an identical offer to one another.
chunks.title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Eligibility & English Language Criteria

chunks.text: Our fees You can pay your tuition fee in up to 4 instalments. The fees for a year’s study, starting in August 2025, are: 13,20,000 INR for India students 15,40,000 INR for Overseas students Your first payment will be a deposit of 10% of the programme fee. To confirm your offer, you must pay your deposit within 28 days of receiving a conditional offer, and no later than 14 days before the start of term. For students starting in August 2025, the deposit is: 132,000 INR for India students 154,000 INR for Overseas students You’ll then pay the balance of your yearly tuition fee as you study, in up to 3 instalments. If you’re an India student, you’ll have to make 3 separate payments of: 316,800 INR by Monday 8 September 2025 435,600 INR by Monday 26 January 2026 435,600 INR by Monday 20 April 2026 If you’re an Overseas student, you’ll have to make 3 separate payments of: 369,600 INR by Monday 8 September 2025 508,200 INR by Monday 26 January 2026 508,200 INR by Monday 20 April 2026 We may adjust the fees in the future to account for inflation and changes in other costs. Exact payment dates for future years will vary according to the academic calendar. Funding Scholarships We offer competitive scholarships to academically gifted students and talented students who would benefit from study financially through a merit bursary. Funding support Please email us about the partnerships we have developed with trusted and reputable financial institutions keen to support University of Southampton Delhi students with a range of education loans.
chunks.title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Tuition Fees & Scholarships

chunks.text: Our application process Admissions are now open for a place at our campus in the Delhi NCR. Our first cohort for undergraduate bachelor’s degrees starts in August 2025. Potential students can understand more about our application process by looking at helpful guidance and advice on university admissions pages. Download more details This downloadable document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, the programme structure, information on course modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. PDF BSc Hons Business Management 2025 Programme Description Document 106 KB How to apply Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to that delivered to other University of Southampton students. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, undergraduates study for three years, and postgraduates study for one year. You might also want to know Should I wait to make my application to study? We encourage students to make their application to study at University of Southampton Delhi as soon as they can. This means we can issue an early Conditional Offer and that students will have the best the chance to meet their offer conditions. If demand is high, we may choose to close applications earlier than mentioned, but students with Offers to study would receive preferential treatment. Can I get an offer with predicted scores? We encourage students to apply as soon as possible so that they can receive a Conditional Offer based on predicted scores for their chosen programme. We can accept an application from a student using their predicted grades if we receive their final grades once they are available. Why is an Honours degree good for me? An Honours degree, shown with (Hons) in the programme title, mean that students will understand the fundamentals of their discipline and then build specialisation and perspective through optional modules. These fast-paced, intense programmes deliver essential learning in the first academic year and offer optional course modules, or electives, in the second year. Honours degrees are offered at University of Southampton campuses across the world, reflecting teaching and learning to a higher academic standard, meaning that an Honours degree would hold more value post graduation for future study and career development. Why is my degree award BSc (Hons) or BEng (Hons)? Degrees with a BA and BSc award do not represent a stream, but a broad categorisation that reflects student choice and leaning. Our BSc (Hons) and BEng (Hons) undergraduate degrees are oriented towards specialist, technical roles in industry with an analytical approach, not science subjects. How can I earn an Honours degree in just 3 years? The University Grants Commission (UGC) has mandated University of Southampton Delhi to make sure that programmes delivered in India are the same as those delivered at University of Southampton in the UK. Three years is the standard length of an undergraduate Honours degree in England where University of Southampton sets academic standards. This 3 year length reflects a trend towards accelerated learning where students see a return on their tuition fees as quickly as possible, whilst still maintaining high academic standards and the quality of the degree awarded. University of Southampton 3 year undergraduate degrees, and 1 year postgraduate degrees, are accepted widely internationally. What is an option? Our programmes are designed to make sure that students quickly understand the fundamentals of their discipline, and then build specialisation and perspective through electives, or optional, modules. These optional modules are typically offered in academic years 2 and 3, adding depth and breadth to core learning from previous years. As these options are often built around real-world trends, the specific choice of module available to students from year to year may vary.
chunks.title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Application & Degree FAQs

chunks.text: Economics BSc (Hons) 2025 Program Overview Award: BSc (Hons) Duration: 3 years Course Type: Full Time Start Date: 08 September 2025 Choosing this programme for 2025 This is the degree of choice for careers and higher education focussed on finance, management and consulting. It has a real-life orientation and deep quantitative knowledge and skills which are in demand by employers. Students graduate with an internationally recognised Honours qualification at an accelerated pace within 3 years. The programme is taught in English, and content is equivalent to a BSc (Hons) Economics degree delivered to University of Southampton students in other locations. We expect that after graduating students will:- Have developed a solid grounding in quantitative methods. Have built an understanding of real-life economic decision-making. Possess highly desirable and employability boosting skills compared to other recent graduates.
chunks.title: Economics BSc (Hons) 2025 Programme Overview

chunks.text: What makes the programme unique? Financial laboratory (FinTech Lab), equipped with Bloomberg terminals, gives students hands-on experience of working with financial and economic data. The Bloomberg Market Concepts (BMC) course provides students with an opportunity to learn more than 150 Bloomberg functions to support them in a career in finance. The BMC completion certificate further adds value to the students’ resumes. The economics dissertation modules in year 3 are hands-on research projects that develop students’ ability to conduct independent analysis and quantitative skills as they enter the real work environment. The employability focus of the programme provides students with the opportunity to network and meet with employers to think about future career paths. There are regular engagements with and talks by industry practitioners/professionals for students in their final year. Which students will benefit the most? Bachelor of Science (Honours) Economics is the perfect degree for those inclined towards analytical and quantitative approaches to economics. Students should have an aptitude for, enjoy and feel confident with mathematics. It is relevant for students seeking a broad-based, rigorous education in economics. It has a real-life orientation and builds quantitative methods which are in demand by employers. Students graduate with an internationally recognised and rigorous Honours qualification at an accelerated pace within three years.
chunks.title: Unique Features and Ideal Candidates for Economics BSc

chunks.text: What you will study On this Economics degree, you can shape your studies by choosing optional modules to suit your interests or career plans. For example, you could choose international banking, risk management, or public economics. You’ll study with experts whose research has influenced international policy. You will also: develop your understanding in core areas of economics including microeconomics, macroeconomics and quantitative methods apply economic and statistical analysis to a range of policy problems use appropriate software tools to conduct quantitative data analysis address economic questions gain the knowledge to understand how the economy works learn how to use, develop and apply appropriate conceptual and analytic tools develop critical and evaluative thinking in economics Modules with a difference Experimental Economics: Covers real-life economic decision-making by connecting psychology and human behaviour to economics. Typically, experiments observe a group of participants to understand how people actually make decisions, and whether they act as rationally as economists assume in theory. The experience of studying experiments provides first-hand exposure to economic problems as modelled and understood by economists. Economic Skills and Employability: This module runs through each year to support students with information and skills relevant for their future academic and professional careers. Principles of Macroeconomics: This module in the second semester of Year 1 gives students the first opportunity for hands-on experience in the FinTech Lab. The BMC certification helps students while applying for internships and graduate jobs in later parts of the degree. Course modules Students take a number of core and compulsory modules before selecting optional modules based on their interest. The programmes are designed to ensure that students gain the fundamentals of their discipline and then add specialisation and perspective through available optional modules. Optional modules may include modules related to the core discipline as well as options built around contemporary, real-world trends that change from time to time. Find out why, when and how we might make changes.
chunks.title: Economics BSc Curriculum and Key Module Descriptions

chunks.text: In Year 1, students will take 9 fixed course modules. In Year 2, students will take 6 fixed course modules, and 4 optional electives. In Year 3, students will take 3 fixed course modules, and 6 optional electives. In Year 1, students must take all these course modules:- Economics with Data ECON1017 Economics with Experiments ECON1018 Economics Skills and Employability ECON1016 Exploring the Social World ECON1019 Foundations of Microeconomics ECON1020 Mathematics for Economics ECON1008 Principles of Macroeconomics ECON1021 Quantitative Modelling in Economics ECON1011 Statistics for Economics ECON1007 In Year 2, students must take all these course modules:- Applied Econometrics ECON2038 ECON Dissertation: Prelim Info ECON2034 Economics Skills and Employability 2 ECON2035 Intermediate Macroeconomics ECON2037 Intermediate Microeconomics ECON2039 Introduction to Econometrics ECON2043 In Year 2, students must choose one of these optional electives:- Company Law MANG2017 Management Ethics MANG2041 In Year 2, students must choose two or three of these optional electives:- Development Economics ECON2013 Dynamic Macroeconomics ECON2004 Industrial Economics ECON2005 In Year 2, students may choose one of these optional electives: Econometrics with Big Data ECON2042 Operations Management MANG2021 Portfolio Theory and Financial Markets MANG2004 In Year 3, students must take all these course modules:- Dissertation: Literature Review ECON3036 Dissertation: Research Project ECON3037 Economics Skills and Employability 3 ECON3033 In Year 3, students must two or three of these optional electives:- Labour Economics ECON3027 Principles of Finance ECON3015 Topics in Macroeconomics ECON3010 In Year 3, students may choose this optional elective:- Strategic Management MANG3008 In Year 3, students must choose two or three of these optional electives:- International Macroeconomics ECON3008 International Trade ECON3034 Public Economics ECON3004 In Year 3, students may choose one of these optional electives: International Banking MANG3009 Project Management MANG3034
chunks.title: Economics BSc (Hons) Specific Modules by Academic Year

chunks.text: Your future career We are a top 20 UK university for employability (QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022). Employability is an essential part of our economics courses. We make sure our graduates are prepared for a successful career or further postgraduate study. After completing this Economics degree, you’ll have vital transferable skills for a wide range of careers in economics. The programme is well-suited to students aiming at corporate careers in banking, financial services, insurance, consultancy, and management. Career opportunities Many of our Economics degree graduates have worked as: economists statisticians business and investment analysts financial advisers accountants management consultants marketing professionals Careers services at University of Southampton Delhi As well as support from our team in Delhi, you’ll have access to our market-leading employability development offering. This includes: online resources regular workshops one-to-one guidance and support with qualified careers professionals through our MyCareer platform Through our e-mentoring scheme, you’ll also have access to: tailored career planning advice on employability skills industry insight We’ve incorporated our employability skills model, Successful Futures, into our curriculum. With our links to multi-national and industrial organisations, you can expect to engage with employers and explore potential career paths. You’ll receive a personalised employability programme that will provide excellent prospects for securing a premium placement and financial security on graduating. We also host regular alumni events hosted in person across major cities in India, allowing you to grow your own networks. Our support is ongoing, and once you graduate, you’ll be part of a global community of over 220,000 alumni that spans over 180 countries.
chunks.title: Career Prospects and Employability Support for Economics Graduates

chunks.text: Key information To help plan your year we have published University of Southampton Delhi term and holiday dates in advance, but these may be subject to change. We expect to provide 25 seats on this programme for academic year 25/26. Eligibility criteria Students who meet the overall academic and mathematics scores for Grade 12 can apply. Those who have studied academic disciplines in the Humanities or Commerce or Sciences including subjects such as Political Science or Business Studies can apply for the Programme. Mathematics is a required subject. Students need not have studied economics before. For Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE): Minimum 78% in your India Senior School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) examination (overall) and a minimum of 75% in Mathematics (Standard XII) examination For Council Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (CICSE): Minimum 78% in your Indian School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) examination (overall) and minimum of 75% in Mathematics (Standard XII) examination For State Board: Minimum 82% in your Higher Secondary Certificate (Standard XII) examination (overall) and minimum of 85% in Mathematics (Standard XII) examination International Baccalaureate: 34 points and above, including 17 points at Higher level including 5 at Higher Level Mathematics (Analysis and Approaches, or Applications and Interpretation) A Levels: AAB, including Grade B in Mathematics English language requirement We accept several ways of meeting our English Language requirement. These include: IELTS Test, with scores of 6.5 overall (6.0 in reading, writing, speaking, and listening) other tests listed on our website an approved academic qualification having the required grade If you’ve been taught and assessed in English, including at a CBSE or CISCE school, for your final 4 years of high school, and have achieved at least 70% in English Language, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Refer to our English Language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements. Our academic admissions criteria Typical academic admissions criteria represent the usual level of academic attainment an applicant is expected to achieve prior to being admitted to that programme. These criteria are not intended to provide a guarantee that all applicants who satisfy the criteria may be offered a place, nor that all applicants to be made an offer will receive an identical offer to one another.

chunks.text: University of Southampton Delhi Campus Academic Session Commencing 2026. Thank you for your interest in studying at University of Southampton’s Delhi campus. Use the form below to apply for undergraduate and postgraduate programmes starting from August 2026. Important: You will not be able to save a partially completed application form, so ensure a strong internet connection and a quiet space. The application form is best experienced on a desktop computer and requires 30-45 minutes to complete. We encourage uploading documents to strengthen your application before an admission decision. There is a 2,000 INR application assessment fee for processing applications for August 2026. For help or status inquiries, email admissions@delhi.southampton.ac.uk. Our dedicated admissions team will reply promptly. We look forward to welcoming you to the campus in August 2026.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi 2026 Application Process Overview

chunks.text: What documents do I need to apply for an Undergraduate Bachelor's programme? Academic Certificates (certificates from Grade XII and Grade X) Students who are yet to complete their final year in school are required to provide Predicted Achievement i.e. the predicted score from your school. Please ensure that your predicted subject scores are specified in the document. Academic Transcripts (transcripts from Grade XII and Grade X) Proof of English Language proficiency You can see more guidance about English Language on our website. If you have been taught and assessed in English, including at a CBSE or CISCE school, for your final four years of high school, you may choose to use your English scores to meet the English Language requirement. Personal Statement (250-500 words) You can see more guidance about Personal Statements on our website. We suggest you keep a draft version ready, explaining your motivation to study your subject of interest at Southampton, and how you intend to pursue a career in the area. Letter of Reference Photo-Identity Card (such as Aadhaar card/passport)
chunks.title: Undergraduate Bachelor's Programme Application Document Requirements

chunks.text: What documents do I need to apply for a Postgraduate Master's programme? Academic Certificates (Certificates from previous study) Students who are still to complete their final year in college, are required to provide Predicted Achievement i.e. the predicted score from your college. You will be required to provide the Final Certificates as soon as available. Academic Transcripts (transcripts from previous study) Proof of English Language proficiency You can see more guidance about English Language on our website. If you have successfully completed a degree taught and assessed in English up to two years preceding the date of first enrolment at the University of Southampton, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Personal Statement (250-500 words) You can see more guidance about Personal Statements on our website. We suggest you keep a draft version ready, explaining your motivation to study your subject of interest at Southampton, and how you intend to pursue a career in the area. Letter of Reference Photo-Identity Card (such as Adhaar card/passport) Resume or Curriculum Vitae (often known as a CV)

chunks.text: Computer Science BSc (Hons) 2025 Program Overview Award: BSc (Hons) Duration: 3 years Course Type: Full Time Start Date: 08 September 2025 Choosing this programme for 2025 Computer science drives the fundamental technologies of today’s connected world, from healthcare to industrial applications, global trade, entertainment and security. Able to model and analyse challenges and provide solutions in every area of our lives, skilled computer scientists are highly sought after. Applications are welcomed for students who have followed the Arts, Commerce or Science streams. The programme is taught in English, and content is equivalent to a BSc (Hons) Computer Science degree delivered to University of Southampton students in other locations. What you will study On this programme, you’ll find out about the latest advances in areas like machine learning, cyber security and computer vision. Through a mix of theoretical, design and practical subjects, you’ll learn about: basic computer science theory the ethical and legal frameworks which computer scientists and software engineers work within the practicalities of working with computers key techniques and application areas You’ll gain the knowledge to develop technologies that can make a difference to people’s lives in topics, such as: algorithmics computer systems data management artificial intelligence networks and security programming
chunks.title: Computer Science BSc (Hons) 2025: Program Overview and Study Areas

chunks.text: Course modules Students take a number of core and compulsory modules before selecting optional modules based on their interest. The programmes are designed to ensure that students gain the fundamentals of their discipline and then add specialisation and perspective through available optional modules. Optional modules may include modules related to the core discipline as well as options built around contemporary, real-world trends that change from time to time. Find out why, when and how we might make changes. In Year 1, students will take 9 fixed course modules. In Year 2, students will take 7 fixed course modules. In Year 3, students will take 2 fixed course modules, and 5 optional electives. In Year 1, students must take all these course modules:- Algorithmics COMP1324 Computer Systems I COMP1313 COMP Part I Laboratory Programme COMP1300 Data Management COMP1314 Mathematics I COMP1311 Mathematics II COMP1321 Networks and Security COMP1323 Programming I COMP1312 Programming II COMP1322 In Year 2, students must take all these course modules:- Artificial Intelligence COMP2321 Computer Systems II COMP2323 Formal Specification and Verification COMP2313 Programming III COMP2312 Programming Language Concepts COMP2322 Software Design and Development Project COMP2300 Theory of Computing COMP2311 In Year 3, students must take all these course modules:- Part III Individual Project Phase 1 ECSP3000 Part III Individual Project Phase 2 ECSP3001 In Year 3, students must choose five of these optional electives:- Advanced Databases COMP3211 Cloud Application Development COMP3207 Computer Vision COMP3204 Game Design and Development COMP3218 Machine Learning Technologies COMP3222 Natural Language Processing COMP 3225 Security of Cyber Physical Systems COMP3217 Social Computing Techniques COMP3208 Web and Cloud Based Security COMP3226
chunks.title: Computer Science BSc (Hons): Detailed Course Modules and Electives

chunks.text: Your future career We are a top 20 UK university for employability (QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022). Graduating in computer science from a double-world top 100 university gives you a powerful advantage in the global job market. The digital sector is booming, with job creation outpacing other industries by double. Companies look to hire our graduates, confident they will make an immediate impact. Career opportunities With this Computer Science degree, you can pursue programming, software development, IT project management, or information systems oversight. Most of our graduates go straight into computer science jobs in high-tech companies. Potential roles include: software engineer app developer web developer systems analyst It’s also a great foundation for further study at master’s degree or PhD level. Companies you could work at University of Southampton computer science graduates have secured roles at organisations including: Apple Sony Cisco Systems Audi Microsoft Careers services at University of Southampton Delhi As well as support from our team in Delhi, you’ll have access to our market-leading employability development offering. This includes: online resources regular workshops one-to-one guidance and support with qualified careers professionals through our MyCareer platform Through our e-mentoring scheme, you’ll also have access to: tailored career planning advice on employability skills industry insight We have incorporated our employability skills model, Successful Futures, into our curriculum. Our links with multi-national and industrial organisations mean that you can expect to engage with employers and explore potential career paths. You will receive a personalised employability programme that will provide excellent prospects for securing a premium placement and financial security on graduating. We also host regular alumni events hosted in person across major cities in India, allowing you to grow your own networks. Our support is ongoing, and once you graduate, you’ll be part of a global community of over 220,000 alumni that spans over 180 countries.
chunks.title: Computer Science Graduate Careers and University Employability Support

chunks.text: Key information To help plan your year we have published University of Southampton Delhi term and holiday dates in advance, but these may be subject to change. We expect to provide 25 seats on this programme for academic year 25/26. Eligibility criteria For Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE): Minimum 82% in your India Senior School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) examination (overall) and a minimum of 85% in Mathematics (Standard X) examination For Council Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (CICSE): Minimum 82% in your Indian School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) examination (overall) and minimum of 85% in Mathematics (Standard X) examination For State Board: Minimum 87% in your Higher Secondary Certificate (Standard XII) examination (overall) and Minimum 85% in Mathematics in Mathematics (Standard X) examination International Baccalaureate: 38 points and above, including 19 points at Higher level, including 6 at Higher Level Mathematics (Analysis and Approaches) or 7 at Higher Level (Applications and Interpretations) A levels: A*AA including Grade A and above in Mathematics English language requirement We accept several ways of meeting our English language requirement. These include: IELTS Test, with scores of 6.5 overall (6.0 in reading, writing, speaking, and listening) other tests listed on our website an approved academic qualification having the required grade If you’ve been taught and assessed in English, including at a CBSE or CISCE school, for your final 4 years of high school, and have achieved at least 70% in English Language, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Refer to our English language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements. Our academic admissions criteria Typical academic admissions criteria represent the usual level of academic attainment an applicant is expected to achieve prior to being admitted to that programme. These criteria are not intended to provide a guarantee that all applicants who satisfy the criteria may be offered a place, nor that all applicants to be made an offer will receive an identical offer to one another.

chunks.text: Our fees You can pay your tuition fee in up to 4 instalments. The fees for a year’s study, starting in August 2025, are: 13,20,000 INR for India students 15,40,000 INR for Overseas students Your first payment will be a deposit of 10% of the programme fee. To confirm your offer, you must pay your deposit within 28 days of receiving a conditional offer, and no later than 14 days before the start of term. For students starting in August 2025, the deposit is: 132,000 INR for India students 154,000 INR for Overseas students You’ll then pay the balance of your yearly tuition fee as you study, in up to 3 instalments. If you’re an India student, you’ll have to make 3 separate payments of: 316,800 INR by Monday 8 September 2025 435,600 INR by Monday 26 January 2026 435,600 INR by Monday 20 April 2026 If you’re an Overseas student, you’ll have to make 3 separate payments of: 369,600 INR by Monday 8 September 2025 508,200 INR by Monday 26 January 2026 508,200 INR by Monday 20 April 2026 We may adjust the fees in the future to account for inflation and changes in other costs. Exact payment dates for future years will vary according to the academic calendar. Funding Scholarships We offer competitive scholarships to academically gifted students and talented students who would benefit from study financially through a merit bursary. Funding support Please email us about the partnerships we have developed with trusted and reputable financial institutions keen to support University of Southampton Delhi students with a range of education loans.

Apply now You might also want to know Can I apply for a scholarship or bursary? We welcome applications for a scholarship or bursary from any self-funding student who will be paying domestic Tuition Fees. chunks.title: Computer Science BSc (Hons): How to Apply and Admissions FAQs

chunks.text: Why is an Honours degree good for me? An Honours degree, shown with (Hons) in the programme title, mean that students will understand the fundamentals of their discipline and then build specialisation and perspective through optional modules. These fast-paced, intense programmes deliver essential learning in the first academic year and offer optional course modules, or electives, in the second year. Honours degrees are offered at University of Southampton campuses across the world, reflecting teaching and learning to a higher academic standard, meaning that an Honours degree would hold more value post graduation for future study and career development. How can I earn an Honours degree in just 3 years? The University Grants Commission (UGC) has mandated University of Southampton Delhi to make sure that programmes delivered in India are the same as those delivered at University of Southampton in the UK. Three years is the standard length of an undergraduate Honours degree in England where University of Southampton sets academic standards. This 3 year length reflects a trend towards accelerated learning where students see a return on their tuition fees as quickly as possible, whilst still maintaining high academic standards and the quality of the degree awarded. University of Southampton 3 year undergraduate degrees, and 1 year postgraduate degrees, are accepted widely internationally. Why is my degree award BSc (Hons) or BEng (Hons)? Degrees with a BA and BSc award do not represent a stream, but a broad categorisation that reflects student choice and leaning. Our BSc (Hons) and BEng (Hons) undergraduate degrees are oriented towards specialist, technical roles in industry with an analytical approach, not science subjects. What is an option? Our programmes are designed to make sure that students quickly understand the fundamentals of their discipline, and then build specialisation and perspective through electives, or optional, modules. These optional modules are typically offered in academic years 2 and 3, adding depth and breadth to core learning from previous years. As these options are often built around real-world trends, the specific choice of module available to students from year to year may vary.
chunks.title: Honours Degree Benefits and Computer Science Programme Structure FAQs

chunks.text: Accounting and Finance BSc (Hons) 2025 Program Overview Award: BSc (Hons) Duration: 3 years Course Type: Full Time Start Date: 08 September 2025 Choosing this programme for 2025 This is a prestigious degree that offers flexible and versatile career planning and professional certification. The degree is mapped to the latest developments in financial reporting, from sustainability reporting through to international reporting standards. The programme is taught in English, and content is equivalent to a BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance degree delivered to University of Southampton students in other locations. We expect that this programme will:- Take students towards a wide range of professional goals, while conveniently completing their undergraduate degree. Offer a window into International Financial Reporting Standards that are closely aligned to UK Financial Reporting Standards for a global picture, while being contextualised to India. Emphasise highly desirable and employability boosting skills compared to other recent graduates. Provide valuable accreditations that entitle students to earn specific exam exemptions for professional bodies including:- CIMA (Chartered Institute of Management Accountants) CIPFA (Chartered Institute of Public Finance and Accountancy) AACSB (Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business) We are currently applying for exemptions for ACCA (Association of Chartered Certified Accountants) and AIA (Association of International Accountants). Once approved, eligible students may receive exemptions from certain ACCA and AIA exams.
chunks.title: Accounting and Finance BSc (Hons) Programme Overview

chunks.text: What makes the programme unique? The School of Business is the only business school in Europe that holds a licence to impart training in Sustainability Reporting. Includes opportunities for gaining practical experience through client projects and activities with professional bodies. Allows students to keep pace with preparations for professional exams. As a BSc degree, it signals analytical rigour and is a strong asset in the employment market. Which students will benefit the most? Students who want flexibility to select their best-fit professional option will benefit from the programme. Students can work towards the requirements of global firms in terms of international reporting standards, professional preparation and certification, or research. The structure of the programme allows students to shape the degree towards their objectives.
chunks.title: Unique Features and Student Benefits of the Programme

chunks.text: What you will study These course modules provide examples of what you can expect to learn on this degree programme, based on recent academic teaching. As a research-led university, we review our programmes continuously to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. Modules with a difference Sustainability Reporting: Students work with a small and medium enterprise to prepare a sustainability report in terms of the Global Reporting initiative. The Sustainability Reporting course earns students a certification. Programme modules are aligned to accreditation norms: The course covers foundational concepts in year 1. From year 2, the programme is designed to support preparation either for professional qualification through subject exemptions for exams conducted by professional bodies, or for management roles in finance, or for entrepreneurship selecting of different combinations of core, compulsory and optional modules. The research dissertation module in year 3 is key to the development of critical thinking. Every student uniformly acquires a research background in the undergraduate degree, emerging as an independent thinker with an Honours degree in three years. The precise course modules available to you in future years may vary depending on staff availability and research interests, new study topics, timetabling, and student demand. Find out why, when and how we might make changes.
chunks.title: BSc Accounting and Finance Course Structure and General Modules

In Year 3, students will take 3 fixed course modules, and 4 optional electives. chunks.text: International Management MSc 2025 Program Overview Award: MSc Duration: 1 year Course Type: Full Time Start Date: 08 September 2025 Choosing this programme for 2025 Our MSc International Management degree programme will give you insight into becoming a successful global business manager. It will enhance your knowledge of how businesses operate and are managed. It also encourages you to explore different topics to help you discover what you want to specialise in. It’s open to graduates from different academic and professional backgrounds. Our one year postgraduate Master’s programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to programmes delivered to other University of Southampton students. The globally respected nature of UK awarded degrees means that the one year UK Master’s is equivalent to a two year Master’s degree awarded from a non-UK source. What you will study In your management studies, you’ll explore the traits of effective managers by studying essential concepts. You’ll put your learning into practice with practical exercises and simulations. You’ll also learn: how organisations operate and are managed how to resolve issues through your understanding of management concepts analytical, communication and self-management skills

We encourage students to make their application to study at University of Southampton Delhi as soon as they can. This means we can issue an early Conditional Offer and that students will have the best the chance to meet their offer conditions. If demand is high, we may choose to close applications earlier than mentioned, but students with Offers to study would receive preferential treatment. Can I get an offer with predicted scores? We encourage students to apply as soon as possible so that they can receive a Conditional Offer based on predicted scores for their chosen programme. We can accept an application from a student using their predicted grades if we receive their final grades once they are available.
chunks.title: MSc International Management Application Process and Frequently Asked Questions

chunks.text: University of Southampton Delhi has set aside a merit bursary pool (financial aid), called the Future Talent Bursary, for academically strong, but financially disadvantaged, undergraduate students. Bursary awards are for undergraduate students only, up to a maximum of INR 660,000 per student, for the 3 academic year duration of a University of Southampton Delhi undergraduate programme. We strongly recommend that you keep a copy of answers to longer questions in a separate document. If your internet connection fails while you are part way through completing the form, or your web browser crashes, you can easily paste them from the copy you have kept elsewhere into a new form. To be considered for a bursary you need to have already made a programme application for August 2025 entry, and then completed and submitted the bursary application form below. You will need your student identification (ID) number, which you received when you submitted your programme application.
chunks.title: Future Talent Bursary Details and Application Process

chunks.text: Can I apply for a scholarship or bursary? We welcome applications for a scholarship or bursary from any self-funding student who will be paying domestic Tuition Fees. Additional criteria, such as programme eligibility, are shown clearly on individual award pages. You must first make an application to study a specific programme before you make an application for a scholarship or bursary. How would I receive my scholarship or bursary? There is no cash alternative to the waiver of Tuition Fees made under a scholarship or bursary. We will reduce the amount of Tuition Fees that you will pay by the value of the award. How can I contribute to the community as a scholarship student? We would encourage - and expect - that the recipient of a scholarship or bursary will act as a Student Ambassador to promote University of Southampton Delhi while they study with us.
chunks.title: Scholarship Eligibility, Award Receipt, and Student Ambassador Role

chunks.text: Set up your university IT account. This page shows you how to set up your IT account and enable multi-factor authentication. You must set up your IT account to access the university's IT systems. You should set up your University IT account between 20-22 August 2025 after you have received your University of Southampton ID number. You should also set up multi-factor authentication as part of this process. You will not be able to upload your identity documents or enrol at the University until you've done this. It can take up to 24 hours for us to grant you access to our other systems and services, including: Blackboard, our online learning environment, your email, ID card service, enrolment services. If you still can’t access services after 24 hours, contact ServiceLine. You should check your email account regularly, as this is how we will contact you. What you will need: To set up your account, you’ll need your University of Southampton ID number, also known as your student ID number. You'll find this 8-digit number in your confirmation of place email from the University. If you can't find your ID number, email The Student Hub. Security questions: You’ll need to set up some security questions and answers. We’ll use these to check your identity if you ever forget your password. Back-up email address: You should add a personal email address when setting up your IT account. You can use this as a back-up email to access your account if you forget your password. You can also use your back-up email address to reset your multi-factor authentication settings if you change or lose your mobile phone. Multi-factor authentication: You'll be prompted to set up multi-factor authentication, also known as MFA, which adds an extra layer of security to your account. Please allow one hour for your IT account to synchronise before setting up MFA. You won't be able to access our IT services until you complete this step. We recommend using the Microsoft Authenticator app and setting SMS as a back-up. Once you’ve set up your account, you'll use the app to verify your identity when logging into your IT account. If you use WhatsApp with the same phone number you use to set up MFA, Microsoft will send you authentication codes using WhatsApp instead of SMS. How to set up your IT account and multi-factor authentication: Our slideshow on Thinglink will help you through each step of setting up your account. We recommend opening this page in a new tab. Getting ready for your studies: You may also want to read important information on getting ready for coursework, campus life, and your ongoing studies. You'll also find information on how to: access on-campus computers, borrow laptops, connect devices in halls, use our wi-fi service, get online security advice. What is your next step? Verify your documents: You should have original documents verified so that we can check you meet the requirements of your offer letter. See more about verification.
chunks.title: New Student IT Account Setup and Multi-Factor Authentication Guide

chunks.text: This page shows how we will verify original documents. You should have original documents verified so that we can check you meet the requirements of your offer letter. You should book an appointment to verify your original documents. Sessions take place on Friday 22 August, Saturday 23 August and Sunday 24 August, and start at either 10:00am or at 2:00pm. You will not be able to start your studies until you have your documents verified in person, and on campus, by one of our support staff. Check your emails to find a link to book your appointment. You should book only one document verification session as part of your Welcome activity. Document verification will take place individually, and should take around 30 minutes for each student. But we ask you to arrive on time at the start your chosen session to minimise the time that we ask all students to wait during the session. If you arrive after the published end time, we may ask you to come back on another day to verify your documents.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Document Verification Process and Booking

chunks.text: What you will need. You must bring the original versions of official certificates to your appointments. We are not able to accept digital scans, digital photos or photocopies of original documents. You must bring these items with you: Academic certificates and mark sheets. Photographic ID, such as a valid passport, Aadhaar, or a driver’s license. Your admissions Offer Letter, or the confirmation email of that Offer Letter. Four recent passport-style photographs. We recommend that you have: A notebook and pen, so that you can take notes. A water bottle. A laptop or tablet, if available, which will be useful for workshops. Chargers for any electronic devices. Proof you have paid your tuition fee Deposit, such as a payment receipt. Any prescription medications you need. A bank card or way to make payment for transactions such as at the food court. Some comfortable clothing and footwear, for interactive sessions and campus tours. An umbrella or raincoat. You may also find the following useful: Health insurance details, especially for international students. Details of your student hostel allocation, or room confirmation, if you have one. A small padlock or a locker key, if you are staying in a hostel. And remember to bring yourself just as you are. But don’t forget to pack an open mind, a curious spirit, and the courage to ask questions that matter to you.
chunks.title: Essential and Recommended Items for Document Verification and Campus Life

chunks.text: What is your next step? Check in to student hostel. If you have booked accommodation at the official student hostel you should check in and make sure payments are up to date. See more about check in.
chunks.title: Next Steps: Student Hostel Check-in and Payments

chunks.text: Check in to student hostel. This page shows you how to check in to the student hostel. You must check in before you travel to the accommodation or move into your room. Verify documents: You must have verified your documents before you are able to move into your room. Verification checks take place on campus, so you should come to campus with your documents rather than travelling to the student hostel. The official shuttlebus will take you from campus to the student hostel. You must also make accommodation fee payments before you move into your room. These payments are: a deposit to secure your room, and a first instalment towards your remaining accommodation fees. When you have completed both verification checks and made payments, you can check in to your room from 23 August 2025. What you will need: Original documents that need to be checked and verified. Any proof of payment or receipts that show that you have paid accommodation fees. Verify your documents: You should have original documents verified before you check in to the student hostel. See more about verification. Pay accommodation fees: You must pay a deposit and accommodation fee instalment before you check in to the student hostel. What is your next step? Enrol at the university: You must officially enrol to join the university as a student. See more about enrolment.
chunks.title: Student Hostel Check-in Process and Requirements

chunks.text: Enrol at the university. This page shows you how to enrol at the university. You must officially enrol to join the university as a student. You must enrol to officially join the university between 27-29 August. You may need to wait up to 12 hours after setting up your University IT account for the system to allow you to enrol. Enrolling is how you officially join the University. If you don't enrol, you can be withdrawn from your course. To enrol, you’ll need: your University IT account login details, your University ID number (also known as your student ID number, found on your offer letter or welcome email), your current permanent address, and emergency contact details. You'll also be asked for a term-time address and your mobile number, which can be added later. Questions for UK government statistical purposes and university support are also included. What is your next step? Collect your student ID card. You must have a student ID card to access campus and identify you as a member of the student community. See more about collection.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Enrolment and Student ID Card Guide

chunks.text: To collect your student ID card, which is essential for campus access and identification, ensure you have created your university IT account, supplied a passport-style photo for document verification, and enrolled as a student. ID cards should be collected on Friday 29 August 2025 from campus reception after receiving a confirmation message. Displaying your card grants access to campus facilities, the library, lecture and seminar rooms, and identifies you as a student. Replacement cards cost Rs. 500 if the original is lost. Additionally, prepare for the Student Induction and Support Week, offering support and advice from professional and academic teams on campus from 25-29 August 2025.
chunks.title: Student ID Card Collection and Induction Week Details

chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Careers and Employer Week

chunks.text:
The University of Southampton Delhi is hosting a Careers and Employer Week, offering students advice from employers and the careers team on being career-ready. Sessions will take place on campus from 01-05 September 2025. The week features a diverse schedule of events:

Monday 01 September 2025: Your future starts now: Employability & career readiness. Topics include a welcome & career advancement services keynote, 'Success at Southampton – Why employability starts now', an overview of the 'Careers & employability ecosystem – India & UK support', employer insights from Investec Global Services, and student club elections & campaigns.

Tuesday 02 September 2025: Culture collide: India × UK mashup. This day includes 'Bridging worlds, blending wisdoms', a self-mapping exercise for skills & aspirations, free time, a workshop on emotional intelligence in the world of AI, and an Indian traditional day & tastes of India celebration.

Wednesday 03 September 2025: Strong body, stronger bonds. Focusing on 'Building resilience, community & support systems', with employer insights & live briefs (SIP), 'Stranded! The ultimate survival challenge: teamwork & leadership', and reflection over tea & coffee.

Thursday 04 September 2025: Navigating the digital landscape. Themes include being 'Responsible, innovative, and future-ready', with alumni insights on 'Journeys & lessons from Southampton', and exploring opportunities as a student, followed by a movie evening.

Friday 05 September 2025: IGNITE: First light of the first batch. A celebration titled 'Celebrate your journey, your cohort, your spark', including dress rehearsals & campus setup, 'UoSD’s Got Talent – Shine on!', and free time before the big night.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Careers and Employer Week
