chunks.text: Meet our team. Our academic and professional team members combine local knowledge of Higher Education in India with international experience. University staff profiles can be searched or filtered by department or research interests (for academic faculty members). Departments include Accounting and Finance academics, Business academics, Computing academics, Economics academics, Professional staff, and Senior leadership. Research Interests encompass diverse areas such as Advanced Threat Detection & Response, Agricultural Economics, AI for social good, Behavioural Finance, Biomedical signal acquisition, processing, and analysis, Board gender diversity, Career Management & Sustainable Careers, Corporate finance, Cyber Threat intelligence, Digital health, Diversity, Equality & Inclusion, ESG finance, Ethical data science, Financial inclusion, Flexible Work, Information Security & Privacy, Malware Analysis, and Organisational Behaviour.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Team Overview and Expertise

chunks.text: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy is an interdisciplinary researcher and educator, specializing in careers, leadership, and organizational behaviour. She previously taught at Queen Mary, University of London, and is an Associate Fellow of the Higher Education Academy. Her research focuses on the careers of professional men and women in India, especially within multinational corporations. Dr Pasumarthy holds a PhD in Business and Management from Queen Mary, University of London. She has presented her research at major international conferences and is committed to promoting social justice, inclusion, and sustainability in academia and organisational environments. Research Interests: Career Management & Sustainable Careers Diversity, Equality & Inclusion Ethical Decision Making Flexible Work Leaders and Leadership Development Organisational Behaviour.
chunks.title: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy: Careers, Leadership, and Organisational Behaviour Expert

chunks.text: Dr Chitrakalpa Sen is Head of Economics at the University of Southampton Delhi. He holds a PhD in Economics from the West Bengal University of Technology, following an MSc and BSc (H) in Economics from the University of Calcutta. His research lies at the intersection of sustainable finance, ESG investing, and financial market dynamics, with particular expertise in volatility modelling, regime switching, and convergence in emerging economies. Chitrakalpa has published widely in leading international journals such as Journal of Economic Surveys, Energy Economics, Resources Policy and the Journal of Cleaner Production, and his work continues to shape debates on the resilience of green investments and the role of financial markets in sustainable growth. Research Interests: Convergence and growth dynamics in emerging economies ESG finance and sustainable investing Financial market complexity and behavioural dynamics Volatility modelling, regime-switching, and higher-order moment connectedness.
chunks.title: Dr Chitrakalpa Sen: Head of Economics, Sustainable Finance Expert

chunks.text: Dr Mohammed Anam Akhtar is Head of Business Education at University of Southampton Delhi, bringing over a decade of distinguished academic, leadership, and industry experience to the role. He has previously served as Assistant Professor of Accounting and Finance and BBA program chair at IMT Dubai, contributed to ACCA program accreditation, and held teaching and consultancy positions at Curtin University (Dubai). Dr Akhtar holds a PhD in Management from NIT India, is a Certified Management and Business Educator (CMBE, UK), and is UGC-NET qualified. His wide-ranging expertise ensures academic rigour and innovation in business programs at the University, enriching the student experience with world-class learning and strong industry engagement. Research Interests: Behavioural Finance Financial inclusion Financial Reporting Financial wellbeing & Financial capability.
chunks.title: Dr Mohammed Anam Akhtar: Head of Business Education and Finance Expert

chunks.text: Dr Nitish Gupta joined University of Southampton Delhi in August 2025 as Head of Business (Knowledge Exchange & Enterprise) and as a Lecturer in Business. He brings a proven track record in research, teaching, product innovation leadership and digital transformation to the campus.
chunks.title: Dr Nitish Gupta: Head of Business, Knowledge Exchange & Enterprise

chunks.text: Dr Rajesh Yadav is a seasoned educator with over 20 years of experience in creating innovative and engaging learning environments. Specializing in Cybersecurity, Cyber Threat Intelligence, Digital Forensics, Malware Analysis, Threat Detection, and Ethical Hacking, he combines hands-on experiential teaching with curriculum design to enhance critical thinking and problem-solving skills. He holds a BE, MTech, and PhD in Computer Science and Engineering and has contributed to both national and international academic institutions. He serves as a Lecturer and Senior Tutor at the University of Southampton Delhi, having previously held diverse academic roles across multiple institutions. Dr Yadav’s teaching excellence and contributions to the cybersecurity domain have been recognized with the Excellence in Teaching Award from the University of Southampton Malaysia and the International Educational Achievements Award for his outstanding contributions to the Cybersecurity domain. Research Interests: Advanced Threat Detection & Response Cyber Threat intelligence Information Security & Privacy Malware Analysis Threat Hunting.
chunks.title: Dr Rajesh Yadav: Cybersecurity Expert and Senior Tutor

chunks.text: Dr Nalini Sharan leads the Student Experience portfolio at University of Southampton Delhi, bringing nearly two decades of leadership in higher education, student affairs, and strategic engagement. She is responsible for shaping a student-first culture that fosters wellbeing, inclusion, global awareness, and a vibrant campus life. Role: Head of Student Experience Department: Professional staff
chunks.title: Dr Nalini Sharan: Head of Student Experience at UoSD

chunks.text: Prior to joining University of Southampton Delhi, Dr Sharan served as Director – Student Experience & Engagement (Pan India) at Pearl Academy, where she spearheaded transformation initiatives across five campuses. Her work focused on holistic student development, co-curricular innovation, mental health, and internationalisation. She has also held senior roles at Global University Systems and has experience leading large-scale operations across India, Canada, and the UAE. With a PhD in Indian History from Lucknow University, Dr Sharan combines academic depth with operational expertise. She has been instrumental in driving cross-functional strategies that integrate admissions, student support, wellbeing services, and community development. Dr Sharan said: “My vision is to create a nurturing, inclusive, and globally connected student ecosystem at the Delhi campus — one that equips students with the confidence, resilience, and skills they need to thrive”. “Student life is at the heart of the university experience. Through meaningful engagement, empathetic leadership, and a culture of care, I’m committed to ensuring every student at University of Southampton Delhi feels valued, supported, and inspired.”
chunks.title: Dr Nalini Sharan's Professional History and Student Vision

chunks.text: How can I travel between the student hostel and campus? Perhaps the best way to get to campus in the morning from our student hostel, or back there again at night, is the private shuttle bus. This free service will run at regular intervals every weekday. When can I book a room at the student hostel? You can apply for a room at the student hostel when you have completed your application to study. We expect demand for rooms to be high, but you can express interest in accommodation for free, which which does not commit you to any contract. We will be in touch with those who have shown interest in accommodation to advise on availability and how to book a room.

chunks.text: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy is a Lecturer and an interdisciplinary researcher and educator, specializing in careers, leadership, and organizational behaviour. She previously taught postgraduate and undergraduate management students at Queen Mary, University of London, and is an Associate Fellow of the Higher Education Academy. Her research expertise spans careers, leadership, and organizational behaviour, focusing on the (un)sustainable careers of professional men and women in India, especially within multinational corporations. Dr Pasumarthy holds a PhD in Business and Management (no corrections) from Queen Mary, University of London, and an MSc in International Management from the University of Bath. She was also a Garware Post-Doctoral Fellow at the University of Pennsylvania Institute for Advanced Study of India (UPIASI) and a Non-Resident Scholar at the Centre for the Advanced Study of India, University of Pennsylvania. She is committed to promoting social justice, inclusion, and sustainability in academia and organisational environments. Her research interests include Career Management & Sustainable Careers, Diversity, Equality & Inclusion, Ethical Decision Making, Flexible Work, Leaders and Leadership Development, and Organisational Behaviour. Apart from her academic interests and professional endeavours, Dr Pasumarthy is passionate about living sustainably and conserving the environment.
chunks.title: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy: Academic Profile, Research, and Teaching

chunks.text:
Dr Aparna Pasumarthy's publications and conference presentations include:

*   **Structure, Agency, and Career Management Strategies of Indian Professional Women** by Pasumarthy, A., Athanasopoulou, A. and Kirton, G. (2025) presented at The 85th Annual Meeting of Academy of Management, Careers Division (Copenhagen, Denmark).
*   **Striving to thrive?** by Pasumarthy, A., Kirton, G. and Athanasopoulou. A. (2023), a qualitative study exploring success, sustainable careers and navigation strategies of Indian professional/managerial women, presented at the 39th European Group for Organization Studies Colloquium (sub-theme 21).
*   **Moral-dilemmas-as-practice; Delineating the linkages between context, emotions, and cognitive processes** by A., Bao, L., Pasumarthy, A. and Stitilyte, G. (2023) presented at the 39th European Group for Organization Studies Colloquium (sub-theme 68).
*   **An exploratory qualitative study of the lived experiences, career trajectories and adaptive strategies of Indian professional women** by Pasumarthy, A. (2021) presented at the British Sociological Association Work, Employment and Society Conference 2021.
chunks.title: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy's Academic Publications and Presentations

chunks.text:
Dr Aparna Pasumarthy is involved with the Work Futures Research Centre, which conducts research on a broad range of topics. This includes digital labour, social care, work organisations across private, public, and third sectors, and the various statuses of employees, whether salaried, contract-based, or unemployed.

Her current research projects include:

*   Paternity leave perceptions and policy implications in India.
*   Structure, agency, and career management strategies of Indian women.
*   In pursuit of sustainable careers: Navigating careers and life.
*   Mentorship for professional success.
chunks.title: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy's Research Groups and Projects

chunks.text: Dr Chitrakalpa Sen MSc (Econ), PhD Dr Chitrakalpa Sen is Head of Economics at the University of Southampton Delhi. He holds a PhD in Economics from the West Bengal University of Technology, following an MSc and BSc (H) in Economics from the University of Calcutta. His research lies at the intersection of sustainable finance, ESG investing, and financial market dynamics, with particular expertise in volatility modelling, regime switching, and convergence in emerging economies. Chitrakalpa has published widely in leading international journals such as Journal of Economic Surveys, Energy Economics, Resources Policy and the Journal of Cleaner Production, and his work continues to shape debates on the resilience of green investments and the role of financial markets in sustainable growth. Role: Head of Economics Research Interests: Convergence and growth dynamics in emerging economies, ESG finance and sustainable investing, Financial market complexity and behavioural dynamics, Volatility modelling, regime-switching, and higher-order moment connectedness Department: Economics academics Academic profile Dr Chitrakalpa Sen is the founding Head of Economics at the University of Southampton Delhi. He joined UoSD in June 2025. He received his PhD from West Bengal University of Technology (now MAKAUT) in 2012. He has previously received a BSc (H) in Economics and MSc in Economics from the University of Calcutta. Chitrakalpa’s primary research interest includes sustainable finance, green investments, market volatility, and the role of financial markets in fostering long-term resilience, alongside recent work in growth economics. His research is regularly published in leading international peer-reviewed journals. Chitrakalpa has over 14 years of teaching and research experience. Prior to joining University of Southampton Delhi, he has held senior academic and leadership positions at OP Jindal Global University and other leading Indian institutions. At Jindal Global Business School, he served as the senior associate dean of internationalization and mobility, where he was instrumental in advancing international partnerships, building student exchange pathways, and strengthening global research collaborations.
chunks.title: Dr Chitrakalpa Sen: Head of Economics, Research, and Academic Profile

chunks.text: Discover our programmes Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to that delivered to other University of Southampton students. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, undergraduates study for three years, and postgraduates study for one year.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Programme Overview

chunks.text: BEng (Hons) Software Engineering Learn to develop reliable, complex and secure software systems. From mobile banking apps to aircraft autopilot controls, you’ll learn how to analyse software requirements, and then design, build and test a system that meets them. Duration: 3 years Course Type: Full Time Start Date: Aug, 2026
chunks.title: Programme: BEng (Hons) Software Engineering

chunks.text: BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance A prestigious degree for those aiming to enter the fields of commerce, accountancy and finance. Learn how to prepare and interpret financial and managerial information and gain a sound understanding of investment and finance, while shaping the degree towards your objectives. Duration: 3 years Course Type: Full Time Start Date: Aug, 2026
chunks.title: Programme: BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance

chunks.text: BSc (Hons) Business Management Learn to think differently about business and inspire change within organisations and society with an interdisciplinary approach Develop skills and knowledge to navigate complex business environments, covering foundational management through specialised modules in disciplines of accounting and finance, analytics, marketing, and business management. Duration: 3 years Course Type: Full Time Start Date: Aug, 2026
chunks.title: Programme: BSc (Hons) Business Management

chunks.text: Dr Mohammed Anam Akhtar MBA, PhD (NIT), CMBE (UK) Dr Mohammed Anam Akhtar, currently working as Head of Business Education at University of Southampton Delhi, brings over a decade of distinguished academic, leadership, and industry experience to the role. He has previously served as Assistant Professor of Accounting and Finance and BBA program chair at IMT Dubai, contributed to ACCA program accreditation, and held teaching and consultancy positions at Curtin University (Dubai). Dr Akhtar holds a PhD in Management from NIT India, is a Certified Management and Business Educator (CMBE, UK), and is UGC-NET qualified. His wide-ranging expertise ensures academic rigour and innovation in business programs at the University, enriching the student experience with world-class learning and strong industry engagement. Role: Head of Business Education Research Interests: Behavioural Finance Financial inclusion Financial Reporting Financial wellbeing & Financial capability Department: Business academics Dr Mohammed Anam Akhtar joined University of Southampton Delhi as the Head of Business (Education), bringing with him over a decade of academic excellence, leadership, and industry engagement. In his role, he will oversee the delivery and enhancement of business programs, ensuring academic rigour, innovation, and a strong student-centred experience. Prior to this role, Dr Akhtar served as an Assistant Professor of Accounting and Finance at the Institute of Management Technology (IMT) Dubai, where he also chaired the BBA program and actively contributed to the ACCA accreditation of the BBA program. His academic journey includes teaching engagements with globally recognized institutions such as Curtin University (Dubai campus) and consultancy roles for curriculum development and financial reporting standards. Dr Akhtar holds a PhD in Management from Motilal Nehru National Institute of Technology (NIT) India and is a Certified Management and Business Educator (CMBE) recognised by the Chartered Association of Business Schools (CABS), UK. He is also a UGC-NET qualified academic. Dr Akhtar has published extensively in leading international journals. He has presented his work at premier academic platforms such as IIMs, IITs, and international conferences in Germany, Romania, and Dubai. Dr Akhtar’s wide-ranging academic, research, and leadership experiences promise to greatly enrich the academic environment at University of Southampton Delhi, empowering students with world-class learning opportunities.
chunks.title: Dr. Mohammed Anam Akhtar: Head of Business Education and Academic Profile

chunks.text: Dr Nitish Gupta PhD, MBA Dr Nitish Gupta joined University of Southampton Delhi in August 2025 as Head of Business (Knowledge Exchange & Enterprise) and as a Lecturer in Business. He brings a proven track record in research, teaching, product innovation leadership and digital transformation to the campus. Role: Head of Business (Knowledge Exchange & Enterprise), Department: Business academics Academic profile Prior to this role, Dr Gupta served as Global Product Leader (Network) as a Service at IPC Systems in New York and London, where he led the launch of new services for capital markets and managed strategic cloud partnerships. He previously held positions as Global Product Manager at Bühler Group and London Medical Laboratory in London, where he spearheaded the development of innovative products, including high-tech optical sorters, solutions for food and plastic recycling, and advanced health check services. Over the course of his corporate career, he has worked with renowned organizations such as Tata and Merck and has collaborated with large corporations across various industries on research and innovation initiatives. Dr Gupta holds a PhD in Engineering from the University of Cambridge, specializing in Strategic Technology and Innovation Management. He also holds an MBA from BITS Pilani and a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science Engineering. He has served as a Visiting Researcher at the Tokyo Institute of Technology and has taught undergraduate, postgraduate, and executive-level courses at the University of Cambridge. He has also delivered guest lectures at institutions including the University of Liverpool and OP Jindal Global University. Dr Gupta has been invited to speak at several industry events and has served as a keynote speaker at the NYU Stern Innovation Conference. His primary areas of interest and research include strategy, innovation, and sustainability. Dr Gupta said: “I am excited to be part of the team at the University of Southampton Delhi, a Russell Group university that places research and teaching at its core while driving student experience through an innovative curriculum. I look forward to contributing to the university’s triple helix strategy—education, research, and knowledge exchange—particularly by fostering industrial engagement, advancing knowledge transfer, and supporting student success. My goal is to help prepare students to tackle complex global challenges by equipping them with world-class knowledge and the skills they need to thrive.”
chunks.title: Dr. Nitish Gupta: Head of Business, Academic Profile and Vision

chunks.text: Dr Rajesh Yadav BE, MTech, PhD Dr Rajesh Yadav is a seasoned educator with over 20 years of experience in creating innovative and engaging learning environments. Specializing in Cybersecurity, Cyber Threat Intelligence, Digital Forensics, Malware Analysis, Threat Detection, and Ethical Hacking, he combines hands-on experiential teaching with curriculum design to enhance critical thinking and problem-solving skills. He holds a BE, MTech, and PhD in Computer Science and Engineering and has contributed to both national and international academic institutions. He serves as a Lecturer and Senior Tutor at the University of Southampton Delhi, having previously held diverse academic roles across multiple institutions. Dr Yadav’s teaching excellence and contributions to the cybersecurity domain have been recognized with the Excellence in Teaching Award from the University of Southampton Malaysia and the International Educational Achievements Award for his outstanding contributions to the Cybersecurity domain. Role: Lecturer and Senior Tutor Research Interests: Advanced Threat Detection & Response Cyber Threat intelligence Information Security & Privacy Malware Analysis Threat Hunting Department: Computing academics
chunks.title: Dr Rajesh Yadav's Profile and Specializations

chunks.text: Dr Rajesh Yadav is a seasoned academician with two decades of progressive experience in the field of Computer Science, where he has consistently demonstrated excellence as an educator, researcher, and academic leader. Over the course of his career in Computer Science, he has made significant contributions to higher education through his work at reputed national and international institutions, shaping both undergraduate and postgraduate learning environments with a deep focus on innovation, quality, and relevance. Prior to his current role at the University of Southampton Delhi, Dr Yadav served as a faculty member at University of Southampton Malaysia, where he contributed significantly to curriculum design, program review, and institutional accreditation processes. His international teaching experience has provided him with a diverse and global perspective, enhancing his ability to address the educational needs of students from various academic and cultural backgrounds. His academic journey is marked by a strong commitment to excellence in teaching, an unwavering belief in the transformative power of education, and a forward-looking approach that integrates foundational computer science principles with emerging technologies. Dr Yadav’s teaching philosophy emphasizes not only the delivery of theoretical knowledge but also the cultivation of practical skills, critical thinking, problem-solving abilities, and independent learning—essential attributes for success in a rapidly evolving technological world. His academic frameworks are aligned with current industry trends and academic benchmarks, ensuring that the learning experience remains both contemporary and comprehensive. His courses are known for blending academic rigor with relevance, making them highly impactful for students aspiring to enter research, industry, or entrepreneurial domains. He holds a Bachelor of Engineering (BE) in Computer Science and Engineering from Maharishi Dayanand University, MTech in Computer Science and Engineering from Guru Jambheshwar University of Science and Technology, and a PhD in Computer Science and Engineering from Mewar University, India. Dr Yadav’s specialization is in the area of Cyber Security, and he has developed deep domain expertise across a wide spectrum of subfields, including Information Security and Privacy, Malware Analysis, Digital Forensics, Cyber Threat Intelligence, and Threat Hunting. His knowledge is not only rooted in research but also translated into practical applications, as he has played a key role in setting up and managing state-of-the-art cybersecurity laboratories. These labs have served as innovation hubs and hands-on learning centres for students, equipping them with the tools and techniques required to navigate real-world cyber threats. In addition to his teaching and institutional roles, Dr Yadav is an active researcher and thought leader in the domains of malware analysis, machine learning, and cyber threat intelligence – three areas that lie at the intersection of computer science theory and application. His research explores how intelligent systems can be leveraged to detect, analyze, and respond to cyber threats in real time. He has published numerous papers in peer-reviewed journals and has presented his findings at prestigious international conferences, earning recognition from academic peers and cybersecurity professionals alike
chunks.title: Detailed Academic Profile and Research Expertise

chunks.text:
Accounting and Finance BSc (Hons) Award BSc (Hons) Duration 3 years Course Type Full Time Start Date 24 August 2026 Apply now Download Prospectus Contact Us

Choosing this programme
Our BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance is a prestigious degree for students aiming to enter the fields of commerce, accountancy and finance. It offers flexibility in career planning. Students can work towards the requirements of global firms, professional preparation and certification, or research. Learn how to prepare and interpret financial and managerial information and gain a sound understanding of investment and finance, while shaping the degree towards your objectives. The degree is mapped to the latest developments in financial reporting, from sustainability reporting through to international reporting standards. The School of Business at University of Southampton is the only business school in Europe that holds a licence to impart training in Sustainability Reporting. You will graduate with an internationally recognised Honours qualification in 3 years, simultaneously gaining practical experience through client projects and keeping pace with preparations for professional certifications and qualifications. The content is equivalent to a BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance degree delivered to University of Southampton students in the UK. We expect that the programme will: Take students towards a wide range of professional goals, while conveniently completing a rigorous and globally recognised undergraduate degree. Offer a window into International Financial Reporting Standards that are closely aligned to UK Financial Reporting Standards for a global picture, while being contextualised to India. Provide valuable accreditations that entitle students to earn specific exam exemptions for professional bodies including: Association of Chartered Certified Accountants (ACCA) Association of International Accountants (AIA) Chartered Institute of Management Accountants (CIMA) Chartered Institute of Public Finance and Accountancy (CIPFA) Institute of Chartered Accountants of Scotland (ICAS) University of Southampton Business School is accredited to the Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business (AACSB) that signifies excellence in business education.
chunks.title: BSc Accounting and Finance Programme Overview and Accreditations

chunks.text:
What you will study
Students take a number of core and compulsory modules before selecting optional modules based on their interest. You are prepared for the rigour of professional qualifications or management roles in finance by selecting different combinations of core, compulsory and optional modules. Optional modules may include modules related to the core discipline as well as topics built around contemporary, real-world trends that change from time to time.

Year 1 overview
You will gain a vital understanding of the theories and techniques of successful management, management accounting and financial management.

Year 2 overview
You’ll gain a greater understanding of business essentials such as financial accounting, company law, portfolio theory, and the principles of audit and taxation. You can choose from module options including human resource management and management ethics.

Year 3 overview
The research dissertation module in year 3 is key to the development of critical thinking. Every student uniformly acquires a research background in the undergraduate degree, emerging as an independent thinker with an Honours degree in three years. Your optional module choices include auditing, strategic management, and futures and options.

Programme structure
The programme covers foundational concepts in year 1 before building specialisation and perspective through available optional modules in Year 2 and Year 3. The Programme Description Document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, information on programme modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. Examples of what you can expect to learn on this degree programme, based on recent academic teaching, include: Sustainability Reporting: Students work with a small and medium enterprise to prepare a sustainability report in terms of the Global Reporting initiative and earn a certification. Programme modules aligned to accreditation norms: Programme modules that are designed to support certification in accounting by professional bodies through exam exemptions. As a research-led University, we undertake a continuous review of our programmes to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. As a result, this programme may be revised during a student’s period of registration; however, any revision will be balanced against the requirement that the student should receive the educational service expected. Please read our Disclaimer to see why, when, and how changes may be made to a student’s programme.

Semester abroad
You will have the possibility to spend a semester at University of Southampton UK in Year 2, learning in a vibrant academic and cultural context. The selection of students for a semester abroad will depend on academic performance and a decision by programme lead.
chunks.title: Accounting and Finance BSc Curriculum and Semester Abroad

chunks.text:
Key information
We are mandated by the University Grants Commission (UGC) to deliver the equivalent programme and quality as we offer in the UK. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, the BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance degree has a duration of three years. View University of Southampton Delhi term dates. Dates mentioned are subject to change. Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to the BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance degree delivered at University of Southampton in the UK.

Your future career
We are a top 20 UK university for employability (QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022) and offer extensive support for today’s competitive and rapidly changing employment landscape. Career opportunities With an accounting and finance degree from a double-world top 100 university, you’ll have versatile analytical and problem-solving skills for many professional roles within the sector. You could choose to become: an actuary an auditor a chartered accountant a corporate finance executive a financial risk analyst a financial adviser an investment analyst a management accountant a management consultant a stockbroker a tax specialist or tax adviser

Careers services at University of Southampton Delhi
Our proprietary Southampton skills model is integrated into our curriculum. When you join us, you’ll be able to use our skills model to track, plan, and benefit your career development and progress. As well as support from the Careers and Employability team in Delhi, you’ll have access to our market-leading employability development offering. This includes: online resources regular workshops personalised career guidance and support through our MyCareer platform and e-mentoring With links to multinational and industrial organisations, we enable you to engage with employers and explore potential career paths. We also host regular alumni events in-person across major cities in India, allowing you to grow your own networks. Our support is ongoing, and once you graduate, you’ll be part of a global community of over 220,000 alumni that spans over 180 countries.
chunks.title: BSc Accounting and Finance Key Info and Career Services

chunks.text:
Eligibility criteria
Students must meet overall academic scores and subject-specific scores to be eligible for the programme. Students studying under CBSE, CISCE and State Boards require Mathematics up to Class X; Class XII Mathematics is not required. In order to be eligible for the programme, students must have:

Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE): A minimum of 78% (overall) in your All-India Senior School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) with a minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Secondary School Examination (Standard X)

Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (CISCE): A minimum of 78% (overall) in your Indian School Certificate examination (Standard XII) with a minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (Standard X)

State Board: A minimum of 83% (overall) in your Higher Secondary Certificate (Standard XII) examination with a minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Secondary Certificate Examination (Standard X)

International Baccalaureate: 34 points and above, including 17 points at Higher level Offers will be made on the individual Diploma Course subject(s) and the career-related study qualification. The CP core will not form part of the offer. Where there is a subject pre-requisite(s), applicants will be required to study the subject(s) at Higher Level in the Diploma course subject and/or take a specified unit in the career-related study qualification. Applicants may also be asked to achieve a specific grade in those elements. Please see the University of Southampton International Baccalaureate Career-Related Programme (IBCP) Statement for further information. Applicants are advised to contact the Admissions Office for more information.

A Levels: AAB, plus Grade B or above in GCSE Maths

English language requirement
We accept several ways of meeting our English Language requirement. These include: IELTS Test, with scores of 6.5 overall (6.0 in reading, writing, speaking, and listening) other tests listed on our website an approved academic qualification having the required grade If you’ve been taught and assessed in English, including at a CBSE or CISCE school, for your final 4 years of high school, and have achieved at least 70% in English Language, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Refer to our English Language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements.

Our academic admissions criteria
Typical academic admissions criteria represent the usual level of academic attainment an applicant is expected to achieve prior to being admitted to that programme. These criteria are not intended to provide a guarantee that all applicants who satisfy the criteria may be offered a place, nor that all applicants to be made an offer will receive an identical offer to one another.
chunks.title: BSc Accounting and Finance Eligibility and English Requirements

chunks.text:
Our fees
The fees for the year starting in August 2026 for Indian students are: 13,86,000 INR The tuition fee is paid in 4 instalments. Your first payment will be a deposit of 10% of the programme fee. To confirm your offer, you must pay your deposit as specified in your Offer. For students starting in August 2026, the deposit is: 1,38,600 INR for India students The balance of the yearly tuition fee is paid in 3 instalments. 3,32,640 INR by Monday 07 September 2026 4,57,380 INR by Monday 25 January 2027 4,57,380 INR by Monday 19 April 2027 Fees for international students The fees for the year starting in August 2026 for international students are: 16,17,000 INR The tuition fee is paid in 4 instalments. Your first payment will be a deposit of 10% of the programme fee. To confirm your offer, you must pay your deposit as specified in your Offer. For students starting in August 2026, the deposit is: 1,61,700 INR for international students If you are an international student, you’ll have to make 3 separate payments of: 3,88,080 INR by Monday 07 September 2026 5,33,610 INR by Monday 25 January 2027 5,33,610 INR by Monday 19 April 2027 We may adjust the fees in the future to account for inflation and changes in other costs. Exact payment dates for future years will vary according to the academic calendar.

Scholarships
We offer competitive scholarships to talented students.

Our application process
We are now accepting registrations for a place at our campus in the Delhi NCR for the academic year starting August 2026. Potential students can understand more about our application process by looking at helpful guidance and advice on university admissions pages. To start the application process, register yourself.

How to apply
Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to that delivered to other University of Southampton students. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, undergraduates study for three years, and postgraduates study for one year.

Apply now
chunks.title: BSc Accounting and Finance Fees, Scholarships, and Application

chunks.text:
You might also want to know
Can I apply for a scholarship or bursary? We welcome applications for a scholarship or bursary from any self-funding student who will be paying domestic Tuition Fees. Additional criteria, such as programme eligibility, are shown clearly on individual award pages. You must first make an application to study a specific programme before you make an application for a scholarship or bursary.

Can I get an offer with predicted scores? We encourage students to apply as soon as possible so that they can receive a Conditional Offer based on predicted scores for their chosen programme. We can accept an application from a student using their predicted grades if we receive their final grades once they are available.

Why is an Honours degree good for me? An Honours degree, shown with (Hons) in the programme title, mean that students will understand the fundamentals of their discipline and then build specialisation and perspective through optional modules. These fast-paced, intense programmes deliver essential learning in the first academic year and offer optional course modules, or electives, in the second year. Honours degrees are offered at University of Southampton campuses across the world, reflecting teaching and learning to a higher academic standard, meaning that an Honours degree would hold more value post graduation for future study and career development.

Why is my degree award BSc (Hons) or BEng (Hons)? Degrees with a BA and BSc award do not represent a stream, but a broad categorisation that reflects student choice and leaning. Our BSc (Hons) and BEng (Hons) undergraduate degrees are oriented towards specialist, technical roles in industry with an analytical approach, not science subjects.

How can I earn an Honours degree in just 3 years? The University Grants Commission (UGC) has mandated University of Southampton Delhi to make sure that programmes delivered in India are the same as those delivered at University of Southampton in the UK. Three years is the standard length of an undergraduate Honours degree in England where University of Southampton sets academic standards. This 3 year length reflects a trend towards accelerated learning where students see a return on their tuition fees as quickly as possible, whilst still maintaining high academic standards and the quality of the degree awarded. University of Southampton 3 year undergraduate degrees, and 1 year postgraduate degrees, are accepted widely internationally.

What is an option? Our programmes are designed to make sure that students quickly understand the fundamentals of their discipline, and then build specialisation and perspective through electives, or optional, modules. These optional modules are typically offered in academic years 2 and 3, adding depth and breadth to core learning from previous years. As these options are often built around real-world trends, the specific choice of module available to students from year to year may vary.
chunks.title: Frequently Asked Questions for Prospective Students

chunks.text: This page allows you to express interest and receive more information about the student hostel. You are not booking accommodation, and you are not committed to any contract or payment. We will be in touch to advise on availability and how to book a room.
chunks.title: Student Hostel Accommodation Interest at University of Southampton Delhi

chunks.text: Business Management BSc (Hons) Award BSc (Hons) Duration 3 years Course Type Full Time Start Date 24 August 2026. Learn to think differently about business and inspire change within organisations and society. Our BSc (Hons) Business Management degree offers an interdisciplinary approach, while developing skills and knowledge to navigate complex business environments. The programme covers foundational management through specialised modules in disciplines of accounting and finance, analytics, marketing, and business management. Our research-focused academics are leaders in their field and keep the programme current. Students graduate with an internationally recognised Honours qualification in 3 years. The content is equivalent to a BSc (Hons) Business Management degree delivered to University of Southampton students in the UK. We expect that after graduating students will: gain an understanding of key business management principles and practices, including accounting, marketing, finance, strategy and entrepreneurship tailor the programme to their interests by choosing optional modules learn to thrive in an ever-changing business environment explore sustainability in modern society and contemporary business. University of Southampton Business School is accredited to the Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business (AACSB) that signifies excellence in business education.
chunks.title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Programme Overview and Accreditation

chunks.text: What you will study This degree will suit you if you want to drive change, and make both business and society better. Each year is made up of core, compulsory and optional modules and optional modules begin from the second semester itself. Year 1 overview Compulsory modules will explore current management ideas from a variety of perspectives. You’ll study how commerce, industry and technology have developed, within a broader social and historical context. You’ll also learn about core management and financial accounting, and develop practical management skills. Year 2 overview Compulsory modules will develop your understanding of management and organisational theory, and business research. The second year envisages that students select six optional modules, or three out of four modules in a semester. You’ll choose from optional modules that cover topics such as: entrepreneurial management innovation, technology and the environment making successful decisions We’ll also teach you the research skills needed to complete your dissertation in year 3. Year 3 overview In part 3, a year-long dissertation or business project runs along with 6 modules to ensure the rigour of an Honours degree. You’ll also choose optional modules covering areas such as: marketing in the digital age international business information, organisation and accountability from a historical perspective Programme structure Our compulsory modules provide a solid grounding in the theory of business management. They show you how to apply your skills to real-world settings. You’ll also choose optional modules that reflect your personal interests or future career options. The Programme Description Document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, information on programme modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. As a research-led University, we undertake a continuous review of our programmes to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. As a result, this programme may be revised during a student’s period of registration; however, any revision will be balanced against the requirement that the student should receive the educational service expected. Please read our Disclaimer to see why, when, and how changes may be made to a student’s programme. Semester abroad You will have the possibility to spend a semester at University of Southampton UK in Year 2, learning in a vibrant academic and cultural context. The selection of students for a semester abroad will depend on academic performance and a decision by programme lead. Key information We are mandated by the University Grants Commission (UGC) to deliver the equivalent programme and quality as we offer in the UK. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, the BSc (Hons) Business Management degree has a duration of three years. View University of Southampton Delhi term dates. Dates mentioned are subject to change. Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to the BSc (Hons) Business Management degree delivered at University of Southampton in the UK.
chunks.title: Business Management BSc Curriculum and Study Abroad Options

chunks.text: Your future career We are a top 20 UK university for employability (QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022) and offer extensive support for today’s competitive and rapidly changing employment landscape. The careers and employability focus of this programme is suited to a wide range of organisations, including start-ups, large corporations, social enterprises, and the not-for-profit sector. Career opportunities Graduates commonly work in a range of organisations or sectors across the private, public and voluntary sectors. Careers directly related to this course: business advisor business analyst business development manager marketing data analyst management consultant marketing manager project manager risk manager supply chain manager Careers services at University of Southampton Delhi Our proprietary Southampton skills model is integrated into our curriculum. When you join us, you’ll be able to use our skills model to track, plan, and benefit your career development and progress. As well as support from the Careers and Employability team in Delhi, you’ll have access to our market-leading employability development offering. This includes: online resources regular workshops personalised career guidance and support through our MyCareer platform and e-mentoring With links to multinational and industrial organisations, we enable you to engage with employers and explore potential career paths. We also host regular alumni events in-person across major cities in India, allowing you to grow your own networks. Our support is ongoing, and once you graduate, you’ll be part of a global community of over 220,000 alumni that spans over 180 countries.
chunks.title: Career Opportunities and Employability Support

chunks.text: Eligibility criteria Students must meet overall academic scores and subject-specific scores to be eligible for our programmes. Students studying under CBSE, CISCE and State Boards require Mathematics up to Class X. Class XII Mathematics is not required. In order to be eligible for the programme, students must have: Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE): A minimum of 78% (overall) in your All-India Senior School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) with a minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Secondary School Examination (Standard X) Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (CISCE): A minimum of 78% (overall) in your Indian School Certificate examination (Standard XII) with a minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (Standard X) State Board: A minimum of 83% (overall) in your Higher Secondary Certificate (Standard XII) examination with a minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Secondary Certificate Examination (Standard X) International Baccalaureate: 34 points and above, including 17 points at Higher level Offers will be made on the individual Diploma Course subject(s) and the career-related study qualification. The CP core will not form part of the offer. Where there is a subject pre-requisite(s), applicants will be required to study the subject(s) at Higher Level in the Diploma course subject and/or take a specified unit in the career-related study qualification. Applicants may also be asked to achieve a specific grade in those elements. Please see the University of Southampton International Baccalaureate Career-Related Programme (IBCP) Statement for further information. Applicants are advised to contact the Admissions Office for more information. A Levels: AAB, plus Grade B or above in GCSE Maths English language requirement We accept several ways of meeting our English Language requirement. These include: IELTS Test, with scores of 6.5 overall (6.0 in reading, writing, speaking, and listening) other tests listed on our website an approved academic qualification having the required grade If you’ve been taught and assessed in English, including at a CBSE or CISCE school, for your final 4 years of high school, and have achieved at least 70% in English Language, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Refer to our English Language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements. Our academic admissions criteria Typical academic admissions criteria represent the usual level of academic attainment an applicant is expected to achieve prior to being admitted to that programme. These criteria are not intended to provide a guarantee that all applicants who satisfy the criteria may be offered a place, nor that all applicants to be made an offer will receive an identical offer to one another.
chunks.title: Business Management BSc Admission Eligibility Criteria

chunks.text: Computer Science BSc (Hons) Award BSc (Hons) Duration 3 years Course Type Full Time Start Date 24 August 2026 On this computer science degree, you’ll learn how to design and build software programs and systems. You’ll find out about the latest advances in areas like machine learning, cyber security and computer vision in this fast-moving industry and gain the knowledge to develop technologies that can make a difference to people’s lives. The curriculum ensures strong understanding of basics in mathematics, theory of computing and systems including computer architecture and computer networking. Pathways in Cyber Security, Data and Algorithms, Systems and Networks, Artificial Intelligence, and Applications continue from foundational levels into the third year, and potentially towards a specialist master’s degree. The world-leading undergraduate programme offers in-depth, comprehensive knowledge and aligns with the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) computing curriculum. You’ll study in state-of-the-art teaching labs with the latest equipment and high-quality displays. The content is equivalent to a BSc (Hons) Computer Science degree delivered to University of Southampton students in the UK.
chunks.title: Computer Science BSc (Hons) Programme Overview

chunks.text: What you will study A strong programming pathway runs through three years with Python as a first language, C as a second, Java as a third followed by Haskell as a fourth language. Through a mix of theoretical, design and practical subjects, you’ll learn about: basic computer science theory the ethical and legal frameworks which computer scientists and software engineers work within the practicalities of working with computers key techniques and application areas Year 1 overview You’ll study the logical and mathematical theory underpinning computer science. You’ll also gain an understanding of the fundamentals of computer hardware. As an introduction to software engineering, you’ll cover data structures and algorithms. You’ll also look at the principles of programming. Year 2 overview You’ll deepen your understanding of computer science by studying topics, such as artificial intelligence, communication protocols and the TCP/IP layered model. Year 3 overview A compulsory final year project is the capstone assessment for the three-year degree. Regular project work ensures teamwork, project planning, time management, presentation taking learning towards the successful completion of a brief. An individual project is a chance to conduct in-depth research in an area of computer science that interests you, under the supervision of an academic with expertise in your chosen field. Cybersecurity, applications and artificial intelligence pathways span diverse modules and you will get to choose optional modules on areas like: advanced databases machine learning technologies natural language processing security of cyber physical systems social computing techniques
chunks.title: Computer Science BSc Curriculum and Modules

chunks.text: Programme structure This programme builds a comprehensive foundation in the discipline through inclusion of various modules as compulsory that are otherwise considered elective. Optional modules are geared to employability: they are attractive to students and well-received by industry. It includes research-led, contemporary compulsory modules on areas like formal specification and verification, the design of programming languages and interpreters and on artificial intelligence. The Programme Description Document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, information on programme modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. As a research-led University, we undertake a continuous review of our programmes to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. As a result, this programme may be revised during a student’s period of registration; however, any revision will be balanced against the requirement that the student should receive the educational service expected. Please read our Disclaimer to see why, when, and how changes may be made to a student’s programme. Semester abroad You will have the possibility to spend a semester at University of Southampton UK in Year 2, learning in a vibrant academic and cultural context. The selection of students for a semester abroad will depend on academic performance and a decision by programme lead. Key information We are mandated by the University Grants Commission (UGC) to deliver the equivalent programme and quality as we offer in the UK. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, the BSc (Hons) Computer Science degree has a duration of three years. View University of Southampton Delhi term dates. Dates mentioned are subject to change. Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to the BSc (Hons) Computer Science degree delivered at University of Southampton in the UK.

chunks.text: Your future career We are a top 20 UK university for employability (QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022) and offer extensive support for today’s competitive and rapidly changing employment landscape. With this Computer Science degree, you can pursue programming, software development, IT project management, or information systems oversight. Recommendations of University of Southampton Industrial Advisory Board in the UK and partner companies feed into this course curriculum. It is a foundation for further study at master’s degree or PhD level anywhere in the world. Most of our graduates opt for computer science jobs in high-tech companies. Potential roles include: software engineer app developer web developer systems analyst Careers services at University of Southampton Delhi Our proprietary Southampton skills model is integrated into our curriculum. When you join us, you’ll be able to use our skills model to track, plan, and benefit your career development and progress. chunks.text: Eligibility criteria Students must meet the overall academic and mathematics scores for Grade 12 to be eligible for our programmes. Those who have studied academic disciplines in the Humanities or Commerce or Sciences including subjects such as Political Science or Business Studies can apply for the Programme. Mathematics in Class XII is a required subject. In order to be eligible for the programme, students must have: Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE): Minimum 82% (overall) in your All-India Senior School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) with 85% in Standard XII Mathematics Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (CISCE): Minimum 82% (overall) in your Indian School Certificate examination (Standard XII) with 85% in Standard XII Mathematics State Board: Minimum 87% (overall) in your Higher Secondary Certificate (Standard XII) examination with 85% in Standard XII Mathematics International Baccalaureate: 38 points and above, including 19 points at Higher Level, including 6 at Higher Level Mathematics (Analysis and Approaches) or 7 at Higher Level (Applications and Interpretations). Offers will be made on the individual Diploma Course subject(s) and the career-related study qualification. The CP core will not form part of the offer. Where there is a subject pre-requisite(s), applicants will be required to study the subject(s) at Higher Level in the Diploma course subject and/or take a specified unit in the career-related study qualification. Applicants may also be asked to achieve a specific grade in those elements. Please see the University of Southampton International Baccalaureate Career-Related Programme (IBCP) Statement for further information. Applicants are advised to contact the Admissions Office for more information. A Levels: A*AA including mathematics (minimum grade A). English language requirement We accept several ways of meeting our English Language requirement. These include: IELTS Test, with scores of 6.5 overall (6.0 in reading, writing, speaking, and listening) other tests listed on our website an approved academic qualification having the required grade If you’ve been taught and assessed in English, including at a CBSE or CISCE school, for your final 4 years of high school, and have achieved at least 70% in English Language, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Refer to our English Language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements. Our academic admissions criteria Typical academic admissions criteria represent the usual level of academic attainment an applicant is expected to achieve prior to being admitted to that programme. These criteria are not intended to provide a guarantee that all applicants who satisfy the criteria may be offered a place, nor that all applicants to be made an offer will receive an identical offer to one another.

chunks.text: Funding Explore different ways to fund your tuition fees and other expenses with us. Competitive scholarships We offer competitive scholarships to academically gifted students keen to study at University of Southampton Delhi. To benefit you must apply for, and then be awarded, a competitive scholarship. Cambridge Scholarship Award A scholarship for up to 4 gifted undergraduate students who have completed successfully Cambridge board A levels. Award duration: 1 academic year Study period covered: 2025 - 2026 Funding type: Competitive scholarship Undergraduate Delhi Presidential Scholarship A full scholarship covering tuition fees for 3 academic years of undergraduate study. Award duration: 3 academic years Study period covered: 2025 - 2028 Funding type: Competitive scholarship Postgraduate Delhi Presidential Scholarship A full scholarship covering tuition fees for 1 academic year of postgraduate study. Award duration: 1 academic year Study period covered: 2025 - 2026 Funding type: Competitive scholarship Future Talent Scholarship A scholarship for up to 12 gifted undergraduate or postgraduate students. Award duration: 1 academic year Study period covered: 2025 - 2026 Funding type: Competitive scholarship

Scholar Award A scholarship for up to 12 undergraduate students on BSc (Hons) Economics or BSc (Hons) Accounting & Finance programmes. Award duration: 3 academic years Study period covered: 2025 - 2028 Funding type: Merit based scholarship Merit bursaries We support talented students who would benefit from study at University of Southampton Delhi financially through the bursary. We will select students using an assessment of need and eligibility, treating applicants with dignity and respect throughout. To benefit you must apply for, and then be awarded, a merit bursary. You can see our current terms and conditions for scholarships and bursaries here. How can I contribute to the community as a scholarship student? We would encourage - and expect - that the recipient of a scholarship or bursary will act as a Student Ambassador to promote University of Southampton Delhi while they study with us. How would I receive my scholarship or bursary? There is no cash alternative to the waiver of Tuition Fees made under a scholarship or bursary. chunks.text: Dr Sagaya Amalathas joined University of Southampton Delhi as Director of Learning and Teaching from University of Southampton Malaysia. She is responsible for delivering outstanding education and a distinctively Southampton student experience at the University’s Delhi campus. Her role is Director of Learning and Teaching within Senior leadership.
chunks.title: Dr Sagaya Amalathas: Director of Learning and Teaching

chunks.text:
Dr Amalathas was previously Head of Computer Science and an Associate Professor at the University of Southampton’s Malaysia campus, where she successfully led the graduation of its first Computer Science cohort. She has a strong background in higher education initiatives, research, and contributions to the computing industry. Her expertise spans designing, developing, and implementing applications for Malaysian agriculture, medicine, and telecommunications.

Dr Amalathas expressed her passion for curriculum innovation, aiming to foster a creative and inclusive learning environment at the Delhi campus. Leveraging her experience in launching academic programmes in Malaysia, she is committed to the success of the Delhi campus through her dedication to education, equality, diversity, and inclusion. She believes education is transformative and seeks to inspire both students and staff to achieve excellence in her role as Director of Learning and Teaching.
chunks.title: Dr Amalathas's Professional Profile and Educational Vision

chunks.text: Dr Chitrakalpa Sen MSc (Econ), PhD is Head of Economics at the University of Southampton Delhi. He holds a PhD in Economics from the West Bengal University of Technology, following an MSc and BSc (H) in Economics from the University of Calcutta. His research lies at the intersection of sustainable finance, ESG investing, and financial market dynamics, with expertise in volatility modelling, regime switching, and convergence in emerging economies. Chitrakalpa has published widely in leading international journals such as Journal of Economic Surveys, Energy Economics, Resources Policy and the Journal of Cleaner Production, and his work shapes debates on green investments and financial markets in sustainable growth. Role: Head of Economics. Research Interests include convergence and growth dynamics in emerging economies, ESG finance, sustainable investing, financial market complexity, behavioural dynamics, volatility modelling, regime-switching, and higher-order moment connectedness. Department: Economics academics. Dr Chitrakalpa Sen is the founding Head of Economics at the University of Southampton Delhi, joining in June 2025. He received his PhD from West Bengal University of Technology (now MAKAUT) in 2012, and BSc (H) and MSc in Economics from the University of Calcutta. His primary research interest spans sustainable finance, green investments, market volatility, fostering long-term resilience, and growth economics. He has over 14 years of teaching and research experience, previously holding senior academic and leadership positions at OP Jindal Global University, where he was instrumental in advancing international partnerships and research collaborations. Current research projects include “Examining the Impact of Firm Attributes on ESG Disclosure Scores” with Komal Khatri.
chunks.title: Dr. Chitrakalpa Sen: Head of Economics, Profile, and Research

chunks.text: Publications include: Bamel, N., Khatri, K. & Sen, C. (2025). “Towards a Sustainable Future: A Comprehensive Review and Future Directions in Sustainability Reporting Research”. Journal of Economic Surveys. Forthcoming. Roy, S. & Sen, C. (2025). “Does Distance Matter in Convergence Among Indian States?”. Asian Development Review. Sen, C., & Chakrabarti, G. chunks.text: Accounting and Finance BSc (Hons) Award BSc (Hons) Duration 3 years Course Type Full Time Start Date 24 August 2026 Choosing this programme Our BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance is a prestigious degree for students aiming to enter the fields of commerce, accountancy and finance. It offers flexibility in career planning. Students can work towards the requirements of global firms, professional preparation and certification, or research. Learn how to prepare and interpret financial and managerial information and gain a sound understanding of investment and finance, while shaping the degree towards your objectives. The degree is mapped to the latest developments in financial reporting, from sustainability reporting through to international reporting standards. The School of Business at University of Southampton is the only business school in Europe that holds a licence to impart training in Sustainability Reporting. You will graduate with an internationally recognised Honours qualification in 3 years, simultaneously gaining practical experience through client projects and keeping pace with preparations for professional certifications and qualifications. The content is equivalent to a BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance degree delivered to University of Southampton students in the UK. We expect that the programme will: Take students towards a wide range of professional goals, while conveniently completing a rigorous and globally recognised undergraduate degree. Offer a window into International Financial Reporting Standards that are closely aligned to UK Financial Reporting Standards for a global picture, while being contextualised to India. Provide valuable accreditations that entitle students to earn specific exam exemptions for professional bodies including: Association of Chartered Certified Accountants (ACCA) Association of International Accountants (AIA) Chartered Institute of Management Accountants (CIMA) Chartered Institute of Public Finance and Accountancy (CIPFA) Institute of Chartered Accountants of Scotland (ICAS) University of Southampton Business School is accredited to the Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business (AACSB) that signifies excellence in business education.
chunks.title: Overview of Accounting and Finance BSc (Hons) Programme

chunks.text: What you will study Students take a number of core and compulsory modules before selecting optional modules based on their interest. You are prepared for the rigour of professional qualifications or management roles in finance by selecting different combinations of core, compulsory and optional modules. Optional modules may include modules related to the core discipline as well as topics built around contemporary, real-world trends that change from time to time. Year 1 overview You will gain a vital understanding of the theories and techniques of successful management, management accounting and financial management. Year 2 overview You’ll gain a greater understanding of business essentials such as financial accounting, company law, portfolio theory, and the principles of audit and taxation. You can choose from module options including human resource management and management ethics. Year 3 overview The research dissertation module in year 3 is key to the development of critical thinking. Every student uniformly acquires a research background in the undergraduate degree, emerging as an independent thinker with an Honours degree in three years. Your optional module choices include auditing, strategic management, and futures and options.
chunks.title: Accounting and Finance BSc (Hons) Module Structure and Learning

chunks.text: Programme structure The programme covers foundational concepts in year 1 before building specialisation and perspective through available optional modules in Year 2 and Year 3. The Programme Description Document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, information on programme modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. Examples of what you can expect to learn on this degree programme, based on recent academic teaching, include: Sustainability Reporting: Students work with a small and medium enterprise to prepare a sustainability report in terms of the Global Reporting initiative and earn a certification. Programme modules aligned to accreditation norms: Programme modules that are designed to support certification in accounting by professional bodies through exam exemptions. As a research-led University, we undertake a continuous review of our programmes to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. As a result, this programme may be revised during a student’s period of registration; however, any revision will be balanced against the requirement that the student should receive the educational service expected. Please read our Disclaimer to see why, when, and how changes may be made to a student’s programme. Semester abroad You will have the possibility to spend a semester at University of Southampton UK in Year 2, learning in a vibrant academic and cultural context. The selection of students for a semester abroad will depend on academic performance and a decision by programme lead. Key information We are mandated by the University Grants Commission (UGC) to deliver the equivalent programme and quality as we offer in the UK. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, the BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance degree has a duration of three years. View University of Southampton Delhi term dates. Dates mentioned are subject to change. Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to the BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance degree delivered at University of Southampton in the UK.
chunks.title: Programme Structure, Accreditations, Study Abroad, and Key Information

chunks.text: Your future career We are a top 20 UK university for employability (QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022) and offer extensive support for today’s competitive and rapidly changing employment landscape. Career opportunities With an accounting and finance degree from a double-world top 100 university, you’ll have versatile analytical and problem-solving skills for many professional roles within the sector. You could choose to become: an actuary an auditor a chartered accountant a corporate finance executive a financial risk analyst a financial adviser an investment analyst a management accountant a management consultant a stockbroker a tax specialist or tax adviser Careers services at University of Southampton Delhi Our proprietary Southampton skills model is integrated into our curriculum. When you join us, you’ll be able to use our skills model to track, plan, and benefit your career development and progress. As well as support from the Careers and Employability team in Delhi, you’ll have access to our market-leading employability development offering. This includes: online resources regular workshops personalised career guidance and support through our MyCareer platform and e-mentoring With links to multinational and industrial organisations, we enable you to engage with employers and explore potential career paths. We also host regular alumni events in-person across major cities in India, allowing you to grow your own networks. Our support is ongoing, and once you graduate, you’ll be part of a global community of over 220,000 alumni that spans over 180 countries.
chunks.title: Career Prospects for Accounting and Finance Graduates

chunks.text: Eligibility criteria Students must meet overall academic scores and subject-specific scores to be eligible for the programme. Students studying under CBSE, CISCE and State Boards require Mathematics up to Class X; Class XII Mathematics is not required. In order to be eligible for the programme, students must have: Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE): A minimum of 78% (overall) in your All-India Senior School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) with a minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Secondary School Examination (Standard X) Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (CISCE): A minimum of 78% (overall) in your Indian School Certificate examination (Standard XII) with a minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (Standard X) State Board: A minimum of 83% (overall) in your Higher Secondary Certificate (Standard XII) examination with a minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Secondary Certificate Examination (Standard X) International Baccalaureate: 34 points and above, including 17 points at Higher level Offers will be made on the individual Diploma Course subject(s) and the career-related study qualification. The CP core will not form part of the offer. Where there is a subject pre-requisite(s), applicants will be required to study the subject(s) at Higher Level in the Diploma course subject and/or take a specified unit in the career-related study qualification. Applicants may also be asked to achieve a specific grade in those elements. Please see the University of Southampton International Baccalaureate Career-Related Programme (IBCP) Statement for further information. Applicants are advised to contact the Admissions Office for more information. A Levels: AAB, plus Grade B or above in GCSE Maths English language requirement We accept several ways of meeting our English Language requirement. These include: IELTS Test, with scores of 6.5 overall (6.0 in reading, writing, speaking, and listening) other tests listed on our website an approved academic qualification having the required grade If you’ve been taught and assessed in English, including at a CBSE or CISCE school, for your final 4 years of high school, and have achieved at least 70% in English Language, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Refer to our English Language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements. Our academic admissions criteria Typical academic admissions criteria represent the usual level of academic attainment an applicant is expected to achieve prior to being admitted to that programme. These criteria are not intended to provide a guarantee that all applicants who satisfy the criteria may be offered a place, nor that all applicants to be made an offer will receive an identical offer to one another.
chunks.title: Accounting and Finance BSc (Hons) Eligibility and English Language Requirements
