chunks.text: Our application process We are now accepting registrations for a place at our campus in the Delhi NCR for the academic year starting August 2026. Potential students can understand more about our application process by looking at helpful guidance and advice on university admissions pages. To start the application process, register yourself. How to apply Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to that delivered to other University of Southampton students. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, undergraduates study for three years, and postgraduates study for one year.
chunks.title: Accounting and Finance Programme Application Process

chunks.text: You might also want to know Can I apply for a scholarship or bursary? We welcome applications for a scholarship or bursary from any self-funding student who will be paying domestic Tuition Fees. Additional criteria, such as programme eligibility, are shown clearly on individual award pages. You must first make an application to study a specific programme before you make an application for a scholarship or bursary. Can I get an offer with predicted scores? We encourage students to apply as soon as possible so that they can receive a Conditional Offer based on predicted scores for their chosen programme. We can accept an application from a student using their predicted grades if we receive their final grades once they are available. Why is an Honours degree good for me? An Honours degree, shown with (Hons) in the programme title, mean that students will understand the fundamentals of their discipline and then build specialisation and perspective through optional modules. These fast-paced, intense programmes deliver essential learning in the first academic year and offer optional course modules, or electives, in the second year. Honours degrees are offered at University of Southampton campuses across the world, reflecting teaching and learning to a higher academic standard, meaning that an Honours degree would hold more value post graduation for future study and career development. Why is my degree award BSc (Hons) or BEng (Hons)? Degrees with a BA and BSc award do not represent a stream, but a broad categorisation that reflects student choice and leaning. Our BSc (Hons) and BEng (Hons) undergraduate degrees are oriented towards specialist, technical roles in industry with an analytical approach, not science subjects. How can I earn an Honours degree in just 3 years? The University Grants Commission (UGC) has mandated University of Southampton Delhi to make sure that programmes delivered in India are the same as those delivered at University of Southampton in the UK. Three years is the standard length of an undergraduate Honours degree in England where University of Southampton sets academic standards. This 3 year length reflects a trend towards accelerated learning where students see a return on their tuition fees as quickly as possible, whilst still maintaining high academic standards and the quality of the degree awarded. University of Southampton 3 year undergraduate degrees, and 1 year postgraduate degrees, are accepted widely internationally. What is an option? Our programmes are designed to make sure that students quickly understand the fundamentals of their discipline, and then build specialisation and perspective through electives, or optional, modules. These optional modules are typically offered in academic years 2 and 3, adding depth and breadth to core learning from previous years. As these options are often built around real-world trends, the specific choice of module available to students from year to year may vary.
chunks.title: Frequently Asked Questions about Admissions and Programme Details

chunks.text: Dr Mohammed Anam Akhtar MBA, PhD (NIT), CMBE (UK) Dr Mohammed Anam Akhtar, currently working as Head of Business Education at University of Southampton Delhi, brings over a decade of distinguished academic, leadership, and industry experience to the role. He has previously served as Assistant Professor of Accounting and Finance and BBA program chair at IMT Dubai, contributed to ACCA program accreditation, and held teaching and consultancy positions at Curtin University (Dubai). Dr Akhtar holds a PhD in Management from NIT India, is a Certified Management and Business Educator (CMBE, UK), and is UGC-NET qualified. His wide-ranging expertise ensures academic rigour and innovation in business programs at the University, enriching the student experience with world-class learning and strong industry engagement. Role: Head of Business Education Research Interests: Behavioural Finance Financial inclusion Financial Reporting Financial wellbeing & Financial capability Department: Business academics Dr Mohammed Anam Akhtar joined University of Southampton Delhi as the Head of Business (Education), bringing with him over a decade of academic excellence, leadership, and industry engagement. In his role, he will oversee the delivery and enhancement of business programs, ensuring academic rigour, innovation, and a strong student-centred experience. Prior to this role, Dr Akhtar served as an Assistant Professor of Accounting and Finance at the Institute of Management Technology (IMT) Dubai, where he also chaired the BBA program and actively contributed to the ACCA accreditation of the BBA program. His academic journey includes teaching engagements with globally recognized institutions such as Curtin University (Dubai campus) and consultancy roles for curriculum development and financial reporting standards. Dr Akhtar holds a PhD in Management from Motilal Nehru National Institute of Technology (NIT) India and is a Certified Management and Business Educator (CMBE) recognised by the Chartered Association of Business Schools (CABS), UK. He is also a UGC-NET qualified academic. Dr Akhtar has published extensively in leading international journals. He has presented his work at premier academic platforms such as IIMs, IITs, and international conferences in Germany, Romania, and Dubai. Dr Akhtar’s wide-ranging academic, research, and leadership experiences promise to greatly enrich the academic environment at University of Southampton Delhi, empowering students with world-class learning opportunities.
chunks.title: Dr Mohammed Anam Akhtar's Biography and Academic Profile

chunks.text: Dr Nalini Sharan leads the Student Experience portfolio at University of Southampton Delhi, bringing nearly two decades of leadership in higher education, student affairs, and strategic engagement. She is responsible for shaping a student-first culture that fosters wellbeing, inclusion, global awareness, and a vibrant campus life. Role: Head of Student Experience Department: Professional staff. Prior to joining University of Southampton Delhi, Dr Sharan served as Director – Student Experience & Engagement (Pan India) at Pearl Academy, where she spearheaded transformation initiatives across five campuses. Her work focused on holistic student development, co-curricular innovation, mental health, and internationalisation. She has also held senior roles at Global University Systems and has experience leading large-scale operations across India, Canada, and the UAE. With a PhD in Indian History from Lucknow University, Dr Sharan combines academic depth with operational expertise. She has been instrumental in driving cross-functional strategies that integrate admissions, student support, wellbeing services, and community development. Dr Sharan said: “My vision is to create a nurturing, inclusive, and globally connected student ecosystem at the Delhi campus — one that equips students with the confidence, resilience, and skills they need to thrive”. “Student life is at the heart of the university experience. Through meaningful engagement, empathetic leadership, and a culture of care, I’m committed to ensuring every student at University of Southampton Delhi feels valued, supported, and inspired.”
chunks.title: Dr Nalini Sharan: Head of Student Experience, University of Southampton Delhi

chunks.text: Dr Samiya Khan, Lecturer in Computer Science at the University of Southampton, Delhi, specializes in data science, ethical AI, and interdisciplinary research. A PhD holder and HEA Fellow, she has published widely on digital health, green computing, and responsible AI, and serves as a Women in Data Science Ambassador promoting diversity in technology. Her role is Lecturer in the Department of Computing academics. Research Interests include: AI for social good, Digital health, Ethical data science, Green computing, Nature-inspired computing, and Responsible AI.
chunks.title: Dr Samiya Khan: Lecturer, Computer Science, and Research Interests

chunks.text: Dr Samiya Khan is a Lecturer in Computer Science at University of Southampton, Delhi, India. She brings deep expertise in data science, ethical AI, and interdisciplinary research, underpinned by substantial international experience in both teaching and research. A strong advocate of responsible innovation, Dr Khan’s work explores emerging domains including AI for social good, data feminism, and Indigenous data governance. She has delivered keynote talks and guest lectures at international conferences and leading universities and has authored and edited books with top academic publishers such as Elsevier and Springer. Prior to joining the University of Southampton Delhi, she served as a Lecturer in Computer Science at the University of Greenwich, United Kingdom. In this role, she played a pivotal role in curriculum development, student mentorship, and the supervision of award-winning undergraduate research projects. She holds a PhD in Computer Science and has contributed to several funded research initiatives at the intersection of artificial intelligence, healthcare analytics, and sustainability. Dr Khan is a Fellow of HEA and is widely published in peer-reviewed journals and has authored numerous book chapters and conference papers on topics such as data science for digital health, green computing, and responsible AI. She is also an active ambassador for Women in Data Science (WiDS), championing diversity and equity in technology through workshops, community engagement, and outreach events.
chunks.title: Dr Samiya Khan's Academic Profile, Expertise, and Contributions

chunks.text: Dr Samridhi Suman, holding a PhD from IIT D, is a Lecturer in Accounting and Finance at the University of Southampton Delhi. She is dedicated to student learning through mentorship and delivering complex finance and accounting concepts engagingly. Her research interests primarily lie at the intersection of corporate governance and corporate finance, specifically focusing on the financial implications of board structures, board gender diversity, and promoter ownership. Her doctoral research investigated the impact of various corporate governance mechanisms on corporate finance decisions. Dr Suman's work has been published in peer-reviewed journals and presented at numerous conferences, including receiving the Best Paper Award at the Academy for Global Business Advancement 16th World Congress. She is also a recipient of the UGC JRF fellowship and IIT Delhi’s Research Scholars Travel Award. Prior to joining the University of Southampton Delhi, Dr Suman served as an Assistant Professor at OP Jindal Global University. She completed her PhD in 2024 from the Department of Management Studies, Indian Institute of Technology Delhi (IITD), and holds undergraduate and postgraduate degrees in Commerce from the University of Delhi.
chunks.title: Dr Samridhi Suman: Accounting and Finance Lecturer Profile

(2025), published by Springer. "The Role of Multiple Large Shareholders in Dividend Payouts: Evidence from India" by Suman, S. & Singh, S. (2022), featured in American Business Review, 25(1), 120-151. Additionally, "Corporate governance mechanisms and corporate investments: evidence from India" by Suman, S., & Singh, S. (2021) appeared in International Journal of Productivity and Performance Management, 70(3), 635-656.
chunks.title: Dr Samridhi Suman's Academic Publications in Finance

chunks.text: Dr Tanu Gupta is Lecturer in Economics at University of Southampton Delhi. Previously, she worked as Visiting Assistant Professor at Indian Statistical Institute (ISI), New Delhi. Her research focuses on labor, environment, identity and agriculture. She holds a PhD in Economics from the Indira Gandhi Institute of Development Research (IGIDR), Mumbai and was a Visiting PhD Fellow at UNU-WIDER, Helsinki. She completed her master’s in Economics from Delhi School of Economics, Delhi. Her research has been published in peer-reviewed journals of international standing and has received competitive research grants. She is affiliated with the Centre for Research on the Economics of Climate, Food, Energy and Environment (CECFEE) and is a member of Women in Environmental Economics for Development (WinEED). Role: Lecturer Research Interests: Agricultural Economics Environment Economics Gender and identity Labor Economics Department: Economics academics
chunks.title: Dr. Tanu Gupta: Lecturer in Economics, Research & Affiliations

chunks.text: She holds a PhD in Economics from the Indira Gandhi Institute of Development Research (IGIDR) in Mumbai and was a Visiting PhD Scholar at UNU-WIDER in Helsinki. She earned her Master’s degree in Economics from the Delhi School of Economics. Her work uses applied econometric techniques alongside experimental and quasi-experimental methods, drawing on both primary field experiments and large-scale household survey data. Her research interests span labour economics, environmental economics, gender and identity, and agriculture, examining how social norms, infrastructure, and environmental shocks shape gendered outcomes in labour markets, time use, and human capital investment. She has received competitive research grants from international organisations like J-PAL at MIT and ICIMOD in support of her ongoing work. Her research has been published in peer-reviewed journals of international standing, and she serves as a referee for several leading journals. She regularly presents her work at international conferences and is affiliated with the Centre for Research on the Economics of Climate, Food, Energy and Environment (CECFEE) and is a member of Women in Environmental Economics for Development (WinEED).
chunks.title: Dr. Tanu Gupta's Academic Profile, Research Methods & Grants

chunks.text: Dr Vaibhav Gandhi BEng, MEng, PhD, MBA, SF HEA, M CMI, LM ISTE Dr Vaibhav Gandhi is an accomplished academic leader and researcher with over two decades of experience spanning higher education, research, and professional service in the UK and India. Currently serving as Head of Computer Science at University of Southampton Delhi, he provides high-level academic leadership in education and research. Previously, as Director of Programmes for Product Design & Engineering at Middlesex University London, he spearheaded programme design, accreditation, curriculum innovation, and student engagement while strengthening industry and professional body collaborations. His research journey includes pioneering work in cognitive robotics, brain-computer interfacing, and intelligent systems, with successful supervision of doctoral and postgraduate students. A Senior Fellow of the Higher Education Academy (UK) and active member of professional bodies, Dr Gandhi has contributed extensively to global academic standards through roles with the QAA, external examinations, and journal/conference reviewing. With a proven record of leadership, innovation in pedagogy, and commitment to advancing engineering and computer science education, he continues to shape academic excellence and impactful research. Role: Head of Computer Science Research Interests: Biomedical signal acquisition, processing, and analysis (EEG and related modalities) Brain–computer interfaces (BCIs) for assistive technologies Deep learning, neural networks, and quantum-inspired computational models Detection for speech disfluency and communication disorders Human–robot interaction and adaptive intelligent systems Inclusive, user-centred design for emerging and accessible technologies Department: Computing academics
chunks.title: Dr Vaibhav Gandhi: Overview, Role, and Research Interests

chunks.text: Dr Vaibhav Gandhi is an academic leader and researcher with expertise in brain–computer interfaces (BCIs), assistive robotics, biomedical signal processing, and emerging technologies. He is the founding Head of Computer Science at University of Southampton Delhi, where he leads academic strategy and research development. Prior to this, he held leadership roles at Middlesex University London, most notably as Director of Programmes for Product Design & Engineering, where he spearheaded programme design, accreditation, and practice-based learning initiatives that enhanced both teaching excellence and student employability. With two decades of experience in computing and engineering education, Dr Gandhi has made significant contributions to research and pedagogy, particularly in areas such as BCIs, assistive robotics, speech disfluency detection, and adaptive human-computer interaction systems. His research includes pioneering the use of quantum mechanics-inspired models for physiological signal processing and developing user-centric interfaces that improve information transfer for BCI users. He champions a project-driven and experiential learning approach, embedding real-world applications into the curriculum to equip students with both technical depth and transferable skills. Alongside his teaching and research, he has supervised doctoral, postgraduate, and undergraduate students, underscoring his commitment to academic mentorship. Dr Gandhi holds a PhD in Computing and Engineering, jointly completed at Ulster University (UK) and IIT Kanpur (India) under the prestigious UKIERI scholarship. He also holds an MEng in Electrical Engineering, a BEng in Instrumentation & Control, and an MBA in Senior Leadership. Recognized as a Senior Fellow of the UK Higher Education Academy, he has also served as Deputy Chair for the QAA (The UK’s Quality body for Higher Education) to lead the review of Subject Benchmark Statements (SBS) for Engineering (2021-23) thereby contributing to the development of UK higher education standards. A member of the Chartered Management Institute (UK) and a life member of the Indian Society for Technical Education, Dr Gandhi continues to advance interdisciplinary innovation at the intersection of technology, education, and society.
chunks.title: Dr Vaibhav Gandhi: Detailed Academic Profile and Qualifications

chunks.text: BCI for Natural Communication Designing EEG-controlled robotic systems for individuals with severe motor disabilities. Speech Disfluency Detection Develop and validate a robust, automated system capable of accurately detecting and classifying a comprehensive range of speech disfluencies using machine learning. Assistive Robotics & Exoskeletons Creating sEMG-driven robotic devices to support mobility (UG and PG student projects). Neuron-Based Control Mechanisms for a Robotic Arm and Hand A robotic arm and hand, driven by a simulated neural network organized as a finite state automaton, demonstrating a proof-of-concept pick-and-place task that bridges neuroscience, robotics, and psychology with the dual aim of advancing flexible robot design and deepening understanding of biological motor control. (PhD thesis supervision of N Singh: Neuron based control mechanisms for robot arm movement) Developing Traffic Predictions from Source to Destination using Stochastic Modelling Presents a novel Markovian framework, including the Zero Server Markov Chain, to analyze delays at junctions and along roads for more accurate journey time estimation. Validated through SUMO simulations and real-world routes, the work demonstrates clear improvements over existing methods and offers strong potential for advancing intelligent traffic management. (PhD thesis supervision of V Mehta: Developing Traffic Predictions from Source to Destination using Stochastic Modelling)

chunks.text: Dr Vishal Talwar is Chief Operating Officer for the university’s Delhi campus with responsibility for growth strategy and delivery, as well as operations from marketing, recruitment and admissions, to campus life and student experience. Role: Chief Operating Officer Department: Senior leadership
chunks.title: Dr Vishal Talwar: Chief Operating Officer, University of Southampton Delhi

chunks.text: Dr Vishal Talwar brings a wealth of experience in higher education leadership, innovation, and academic excellence. Prior to this role, he served as Director & Head of Campus and Professor of Marketing at the Institute of Management Technology, Ghaziabad. He has also held senior leadership positions at BML Munjal University (BMU), including Dean of the School of Management and Director of Innovation & Entrepreneurship. His academic journey spans globally recognised institutions such as SP Jain School of Global Management and the London School of Economics. A prolific academic, Dr Talwar has made substantial contributions to global research, publishing in various journals including the prestigious FT 50 list. His scholarly contributions extend to books, book chapters, and presentations at top-tier international conferences such as the Academy of Management Conference, American Marketing Association Summer Conference, British Academy of Management, and INFORMS Marketing Sciences. Dr Talwar is a PhD from Manchester Business School, UK.
chunks.title: Dr Vishal Talwar's Extensive Academic and Leadership Background

chunks.text: Dr Talwar said: “I am delighted to join the University of Southampton Delhi at this exciting juncture. University of Southampton Delhi promises to be an enriched global learning experience for students. Set within a purpose-built, safe and technologically advanced campus in Gurgaon, it will have a strong focus on academic rigour, as well as holistic development. The Delhi NCR region provides a great platform for networking, industry engagement and career transformation. A carefully curated approach will provide our students with hands-on experience and real-world insights, thus enabling more robust career readiness.”
chunks.title: Dr Vishal Talwar's Vision for University of Southampton Delhi

chunks.text: Mr Hemant Raj BSc PhD FRSC SFHEA NTF is a Lab Teaching Associate in Computer Science at the University of Southampton, Delhi. He has experience in Data Science and Robotics, having previously worked as a Coding Instructor. He has mentored undergraduate students in AI/ML and Robotics and conducted several AI/ML workshops across institutes. Hemant holds a B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering and a PG Diploma in Data Science from IIT Madras, India. He actively participated in various technical competitions and served as a Lead for the Google Developer Students Club, where he mentored over 200 students, organized impactful technical events, and contributed to open-source projects. His technical contributions include developing a FaceNet-based CCTV Auto Attendance System deployed on Jetson nano and a Voice Assistant with Computer Vision for a Humanoid Robot. Hemant is passionate about integrating real-world applications into the curriculum and fostering student growth through project-based learning, technical clubs, and open-source collaboration.
chunks.title: Mr Hemant Raj's Academic Profile and Contributions

chunks.text: The Department of Computing academics at the University of Southampton, Delhi is associated with several research groups. Agents, Interaction and Complexity focuses on the science and engineering of complex systems, including human-machine teams and human-in-the-loop interaction. Vision, Learning and Control explores the theory behind technological areas such as control, machine learning, and computer vision. The Centre for Robotics aims to bridge the gap between robot perception, control, and interaction to create robotic systems that seamlessly integrate into society, transforming industries and lives.
chunks.title: Computing Department's Research Groups and Expertise

chunks.text: Ms Anupama Saini joins University of Southampton Delhi as Site Librarian, bringing with her a dynamic background in library and information science, community engagement, and human-centred project leadership. Role: Site Librarian Department: Professional staff She has nearly a decade of diverse experience across academic, legal, and civic sectors. Anupama holds a Gold Medal in her Master’s degree in Library and Information Science from the University of Delhi and is qualified for the Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) through the UGC-NET. Her work has consistently integrated digital literacy, equity, and ethical information practices into inclusive learning environments. Prior to this appointment, Anupama served as the Head of the British Council Library and Cultural Centre in New Delhi, where she led the transformation of one of India’s most established international libraries. Her portfolio included strategic planning, operational leadership, digital integration, and audience development. She also spearheaded large-scale public programming, including collaborations with UK universities, embassies, and advocacy organisations, resulting in significant growth in reach and impact. In earlier roles, she managed legal reference services and acquisitions at the High Court of Delhi and worked in assurance and audit at EY, where she developed a strong foundation in analytical thinking and stakeholder communication. Anupama is a strong advocate for equitable access to information and has been instrumental in designing initiatives around climate justice, animal ethics, and gender rights. Commenting on her new role, Anupama said: “I am grateful and excited to be joining University of Southampton Delhi. Libraries are evolving into powerful spaces of curiosity, agency, and collaboration, and I look forward to co-creating a vibrant, student-focused learning ecosystem that bridges information services with global challenges and critical thinking.” Her affiliations with IFLA, CILIP (UK), and ILA reflect her international engagement and professional standing. She also holds certifications in project management and digital leadership, underscoring her commitment to continuous learning. At Southampton, Anupama aims to build a progressive library environment that supports academic research, inquiry-based learning, and intellectual growth, in alignment with the university’s international standards and values.
chunks.title: Ms Anupama Saini: Site Librarian Profile and Vision

chunks.text: Finance MSc: Award MSc, Duration 1 year, Course Type Full Time, Start Date 24 August 2026. Choosing this programme: Develop skills sought by employers in rapidly changing and complex financial environments, and advance your career in industry, government or research. On our Master of Finance in India, we will teach you advanced stock market analysis, corporate finance, and portfolio management. You will also study behavioural finance and explore the behavioural biases that can affect financial decision making in uncertain environments. The MSc is intended for those who aim to work in core finance jobs in the banking and financial industry. The programme follows the market and professional bodies closely and is mapped to skills that employers demand. It builds the ability to use and critically evaluate models and formal processes within finance and financial management. University of Southampton Business School is accredited to the Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business (AACSB) that signifies excellence in business education.
chunks.title: Finance MSc Programme Overview and Accreditation

chunks.text: What you will study: This is a full-time 1-year degree programme. The first 9 months comprise taught study (2 semesters), followed by a 3-month individual research project for your dissertation. Topics covered include: complex financial products, assessing and managing financial risk, management decisions and value creation, econometric modelling, forecasting, and leading econometric and statistical software. Programme structure: Core finance modules such as quantitative finance, asset pricing, and portfolio management provide solid fundamentals for investment decisions and financial asset valuation. Optional modules in semester 2 allow specialisation in areas like computational finance, derivative securities analysis, and fixed income securities analysis. The Programme Description Document details aims, modules, and assessment. As a research-led University, continuous review ensures quality; programmes may be revised, but educational service expectations will be met. Refer to our Disclaimer for revision details.
chunks.title: Finance MSc Curriculum and Programme Structure

chunks.text: Key information: The University Grants Commission (UGC) mandates that the Finance MSc programme delivers equivalent quality to UK offerings. This one-year MSc Finance degree aligns with UK awarded degrees. Programmes are taught in English, with content equivalent to the University of Southampton's UK MSc Finance degree. Term dates are available from University of Southampton Delhi and are subject to change.
chunks.title: Key Information for Finance MSc at UoSD

chunks.text: Your future career: The University is a top 20 UK university for employability (QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022), offering extensive support for the financial landscape. Students gain industry experience through trade simulation workshops, industry software, live company data, and weekly research seminars. Career opportunities span banking, finance, accountancy, business consultancy, and the public sector, with roles such as financial analyst, business analyst, and investment analyst. Careers services at University of Southampton Delhi include the Southampton skills model, online resources, workshops, personalised guidance via MyCareer, and e-mentoring. Links to multinational organizations and alumni events in India help students engage with employers and build networks, joining a global alumni community of over 220,000 across 180 countries.
chunks.title: Finance MSc Career Prospects and Employability Support

chunks.text: Eligibility criteria for the Finance MSc programme require an upper second-class degree (2:1 or international equivalent) in a quantitative subject like finance, sciences, maths, engineering, computer science, economics, or quantitative business/management. Professional ACCA/ACA/CIMA/CA qualifications are considered case-by-case, and work experience can compensate for unrelated degrees. English language requirement: Accepted methods include an IELTS Test (6.5 overall, 6.0 in all components), other listed tests, or an approved academic qualification. A degree taught and assessed in English, completed within two years of enrolment at the University of Southampton, may also be accepted. Refer to the English Language proficiency guide for full details. Academic admissions criteria represent expected attainment but do not guarantee an offer.
chunks.title: Finance MSc Eligibility and English Language Requirements

chunks.text: Our fees: For Indian students starting August 2026, the Finance MSc tuition fee is 23,10,000 INR, paid in 4 instalments. A 10% deposit (2,31,000 INR) confirms the offer. Prospective students can find guidance on university admissions pages and register to start their application. chunks.text: Ms Monisha Tandon joined University of Southampton Delhi in July 2025 as Head of Career Advancement Services. She aims to bring transformative impact by staying focused on establishing high-impact academic-industry linkages, advancing global best practices in career development services, and creating a future-forward student experience that reflects the university's academic excellence and international legacy. Role: Head of Career Advancement Services Department: Professional staff. Monisha Tandon has played pivotal roles in over two decades of leadership experience in higher education, with expertise spanning student admissions, career services, institutional branding, and strategic industry partnerships. Her professional journey includes impactful roles at reputed institutions such as NIIT University and Shiv Nadar University, where she has consistently driven growth, built high-performing teams, and forged impactful industry and academic collaborations. Known for her ability to blend data-driven decision-making with empathetic leadership, she has delivered measurable outcomes in student success, institutional branding, and employer engagement. A strong believer in the power of education as a catalyst for change to transform lives, Monisha is committed to combine strategic vision with operational execution. Her approach is rooted in collaboration, data-driven planning, and an unwavering commitment to student success to shape institutional strategy, driving student-centric initiatives, and building sustainable stakeholder relationships.
chunks.title: Ms Monisha Tandon: Head of Career Advancement Services Profile

chunks.text: Professor Eloise Phillips is the Academic Provost at the University of Southampton Delhi, responsible for academic quality and delivery. Previously, she was instrumental in establishing the University of Birmingham’s Dubai campus as Head of Health, Wellbeing and Human Science programmes, and served as an Associate Dean at Aston University. A registered nurse and highly experienced advanced clinical practitioner, Professor Phillips has worked globally in clinical practice, healthcare leadership and management, research, and academia. She was drawn to the University of Southampton for its mission to impact and transform students’ lives and the region. As Academic Provost and Associate Vice-President (International) at the University of Southampton Delhi, she is committed to leading the international campus towards excellence in education, research, and knowledge exchange, fostering an inclusive and innovative environment within India’s dynamic education landscape.
chunks.title: Professor Eloise Phillips: Academic Provost and International Vice-President

chunks.text: Business Management BSc (Hons) Award BSc (Hons) Duration 3 years Course Type Full Time Start Date 24 August 2026 Choosing this programme Learn to think differently about business and inspire change within organisations and society. Our BSc (Hons) Business Management degree offers an interdisciplinary approach, while developing skills and knowledge to navigate complex business environments. The programme covers foundational management through specialised modules in disciplines of accounting and finance, analytics, marketing, and business management. Our research-focused academics are leaders in their field and keep the programme current. Students graduate with an internationally recognised Honours qualification in 3 years. The content is equivalent to a BSc (Hons) Business Management degree delivered to University of Southampton students in the UK. We expect that after graduating students will: gain an understanding of key business management principles and practices, including accounting, marketing, finance, strategy and entrepreneurship tailor the programme to their interests by choosing optional modules learn to thrive in an ever-changing business environment explore sustainability in modern society and contemporary business. University of Southampton Business School is accredited to the Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business (AACSB) that signifies excellence in business education. Key information We are mandated by the University Grants Commission (UGC) to deliver the equivalent programme and quality as we offer in the UK. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, the BSc (Hons) Business Management degree has a duration of three years. Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to the BSc (Hons) Business Management degree delivered at University of Southampton in the UK. As a research-led University, we undertake a continuous review of our programmes to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. As a result, this programme may be revised during a student’s period of registration; however, any revision will be balanced against the requirement that the student should receive the educational service expected.
chunks.title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Programme Overview and Key Information

chunks.text: What you will study This degree will suit you if you want to drive change, and make both business and society better. Each year is made up of core, compulsory and optional modules and optional modules begin from the second semester itself. Year 1 overview Compulsory modules will explore current management ideas from a variety of perspectives. You’ll study how commerce, industry and technology have developed, within a broader social and historical context. You’ll also learn about core management and financial accounting, and develop practical management skills. Year 2 overview Compulsory modules will develop your understanding of management and organisational theory, and business research. The second year envisages that students select six optional modules, or three out of four modules in a semester. You’ll choose from optional modules that cover topics such as: entrepreneurial management innovation, technology and the environment making successful decisions We’ll also teach you the research skills needed to complete your dissertation in year 3. Year 3 overview In part 3, a year-long dissertation or business project runs along with 6 modules to ensure the rigour of an Honours degree. You’ll also choose optional modules covering areas such as: marketing in the digital age international business information, organisation and accountability from a historical perspective Programme structure Our compulsory modules provide a solid grounding in the theory of business management. They show you how to apply your skills to real-world settings. You’ll also choose optional modules that reflect your personal interests or future career options. The Programme Description Document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, information on programme modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. Semester abroad You will have the possibility to spend a semester at University of Southampton UK in Year 2, learning in a vibrant academic and cultural context. The selection of students for a semester abroad will depend on academic performance and a decision by programme lead.
chunks.title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Curriculum and Modules

chunks.text: Your future career We are a top 20 UK university for employability (QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022) and offer extensive support for today’s competitive and rapidly changing employment landscape. The careers and employability focus of this programme is suited to a wide range of organisations, including start-ups, large corporations, social enterprises, and the not-for-profit sector. Career opportunities Graduates commonly work in a range of organisations or sectors across the private, public and voluntary sectors. Careers directly related to this course: business advisor business analyst business development manager marketing data analyst management consultant marketing manager project manager risk manager supply chain manager Careers services at University of Southampton Delhi Our proprietary Southampton skills model is integrated into our curriculum. When you join us, you’ll be able to use our skills model to track, plan, and benefit your career development and progress. As well as support from the Careers and Employability team in Delhi, you’ll have access to our market-leading employability development offering. This includes: online resources regular workshops personalised career guidance and support through our MyCareer platform and e-mentoring With links to multinational and industrial organisations, we enable you to engage with employers and explore potential career paths. We also host regular alumni events in-person across major cities in India, allowing you to grow your own networks. Our support is ongoing, and once you graduate, you’ll be part of a global community of over 220,000 alumni that spans over 180 countries.
chunks.title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Career Opportunities

chunks.text: Eligibility criteria Students must meet overall academic scores and subject-specific scores to be eligible for our programmes. Students studying under CBSE, CISCE and State Boards require Mathematics up to Class X. Class XII Mathematics is not required. In order to be eligible for the programme, students must have: Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE): A minimum of 78% (overall) in your All-India Senior School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) with a minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Secondary School Examination (Standard X) Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (CISCE): A minimum of 78% (overall) in your Indian School Certificate examination (Standard XII) with a minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (Standard X) State Board: A minimum of 83% (overall) in your Higher Secondary Certificate (Standard XII) examination with a minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Secondary Certificate Examination (Standard X) International Baccalaureate: 34 points and above, including 17 points at Higher level Offers will be made on the individual Diploma Course subject(s) and the career-related study qualification. The CP core will not form part of the offer. Where there is a subject pre-requisite(s), applicants will be required to study the subject(s) at Higher Level in the Diploma course subject and/or take a specified unit in the career-related study qualification. Applicants may also be asked to achieve a specific grade in those elements. Please see the University of Southampton International Baccalaureate Career-Related Programme (IBCP) Statement for further information. Applicants are advised to contact the Admissions Office for more information. A Levels: AAB, plus Grade B or above in GCSE Maths English language requirement We accept several ways of meeting our English Language requirement. These include: IELTS Test, with scores of 6.5 overall (6.0 in reading, writing, speaking, and listening) other tests listed on our website an approved academic qualification having the required grade If you’ve been taught and assessed in English, including at a CBSE or CISCE school, for your final 4 years of high school, and have achieved at least 70% in English Language, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Refer to our English Language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements. Our academic admissions criteria Typical academic admissions criteria represent the usual level of academic attainment an applicant is expected to achieve prior to being admitted to that programme. These criteria are not intended to provide a guarantee that all applicants who satisfy the criteria may be offered a place, nor that all applicants to be made an offer will receive an identical offer to one another.
chunks.title: Business Management BSc (Hons) Admissions Criteria

For students starting in August 2026, the deposit is: 1,38,600 INR for India students The balance of the yearly tuition fee is paid in 3 instalments. 3,32,640 INR by Monday 07 September 2026 4,57,380 INR by Monday 25 January 2027 4,57,380 INR by Monday 19 April 2027 Fees for international students The fees for the year starting in August 2026 for international students are: 16,17,000 INR The tuition fee is paid in 4 instalments. For students starting in August 2026, the deposit is: 1,61,700 INR for international students If you are an international student, you’ll have to make 3 separate payments of: 3,88,080 INR by Monday 07 September 2026 5,33,610 INR by Monday 25 January 2027 5,33,610 INR by Monday 19 April 2027 We may adjust the fees in the future to account for inflation and changes in other costs. chunks.text: Our application process We are now accepting registrations for a place at our campus in the Delhi NCR for the academic year starting August 2026. Potential students can understand more about our application process by looking at helpful guidance and advice on university admissions pages. To start the application process, register yourself. How to apply Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to that delivered to other University of Southampton students. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, undergraduates study for three years, and postgraduates study for one year. You might also want to know Should I wait to make my application to study? We encourage students to make their application to study at University of Southampton Delhi as soon as they can. This means we can issue an early Conditional Offer and that students will have the best the chance to meet their offer conditions. If demand is high, we may choose to close applications earlier than mentioned, but students with Offers to study would receive preferential treatment. Can I get an offer with predicted scores? We encourage students to apply as soon as possible so that they can receive a Conditional Offer based on predicted scores for their chosen programme. We can accept an application from a student using their predicted grades if we receive their final grades once they are available. Why is an Honours degree good for me? An Honours degree, shown with (Hons) in the programme title, mean that students will understand the fundamentals of their discipline and then build specialisation and perspective through optional modules. These fast-paced, intense programmes deliver essential learning in the first academic year and offer optional course modules, or electives, in the second year. Honours degrees are offered at University of Southampton campuses across the world, reflecting teaching and learning to a higher academic standard, meaning that an Honours degree would hold more value post graduation for future study and career development. Why is my degree award BSc (Hons) or BEng (Hons)? Degrees with a BA and BSc award do not represent a stream, but a broad categorisation that reflects student choice and leaning. Our BSc (Hons) and BEng (Hons) undergraduate degrees are oriented towards specialist, technical roles in industry with an analytical approach, not science subjects. How can I earn an Honours degree in just 3 years? The University Grants Commission (UGC) has mandated University of Southampton Delhi to make sure that programmes delivered in India are the same as those delivered at University of Southampton in the UK. Three years is the standard length of an undergraduate Honours degree in England where University of Southampton sets academic standards. This 3 year length reflects a trend towards accelerated learning where students see a return on their tuition fees as quickly as possible, whilst still maintaining high academic standards and the quality of the degree awarded. University of Southampton 3 year undergraduate degrees, and 1 year postgraduate degrees, are accepted widely internationally. What is an option? Our programmes are designed to make sure that students quickly understand the fundamentals of their discipline, and then build specialisation and perspective through electives, or optional, modules. These optional modules are typically offered in academic years 2 and 3, adding depth and breadth to core learning from previous years. As these options are often built around real-world trends, the specific choice of module available to students from year to year may vary.
chunks.title: Applying for Business Management BSc (Hons) Programme and FAQs

chunks.text: Economics BSc (Hons) Award BSc (Hons) Duration 3 years Course Type Full Time Start Date 24 August 2026 Apply now Download Prospectus Contact Us On this page Choosing this programme What you will study Key information Your future career Eligibility criteria Our fees Our application process Apply now Choosing this programme Learn how our economic decisions affect others, and how business and financial institutions’ choices can affect our society. Our BSc (Hons) Economics gives you an in-depth understanding of economic theory, and the skills you need to work in sectors like finance, management and industry. This is the degree of choice for careers and higher education focussed on economics. finance, management and consulting. It is grounded in quantitative knowledge and skills and a real-life orientation. Students graduate with an internationally recognised Honours qualification in 3 years, gaining theoretical depth and a practical orientation. The content is equivalent to a BSc (Hons) Economics degree delivered to University of Southampton students in the UK. We expect that after graduating students will:- Develop a solid grounding in quantitative methods. Build an understanding of real-life economic decision-making. Possess highly transferable skills and knowledge that enhance employability.
chunks.title: Economics BSc (Hons) Programme Introduction and Overview

chunks.text: What you will study You will have a parallel focus on theory and practice. Each year is made up of core, compulsory and optional modules and you can shape your studies by choosing optional modules to suit your interests or career plans. Our facilities include the Financial Laboratory (FinTech Lab), equipped with Bloomberg terminals, to gives you hands-on experience of working with financial and economic data. You also have the opportunity to network and meet with employers to explore future career paths. Interdisciplinary modules include modules like Experimental Economics on real-life economic decision-making that connects psychology and human behaviour. Year 1 overview Core modules will provide a foundation in economic topics, including mathematics, statistics, microeconomics and macroeconomics and exploring the social world. You’ll also take an employability module to help you plan your next steps. Year 2 overview You will continue to study core modules in applied and theoretical economics, macroeconomics and microeconomics, adding optional modules for specialisation. Year 3 overview The economics dissertation module in Year 3 is a hands-on research project that develops your ability to work independently in the real work environment. You’ll run your own research project and prepare for postgraduate study and a work environment, depending on your aims. A research supervisor will support you throughout. Programme structure The programmes are designed to ensure that students gain the fundamentals of their discipline and then add specialisation and perspective through available optional modules. The Programme Description Document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, information on programme modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. As a research-led University, we undertake a continuous review of our programmes to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. As a result, this programme may be revised during a student’s period of registration; however, any revision will be balanced against the requirement that the student should receive the educational service expected. Please read our Disclaimer to see why, when, and how changes may be made to a student’s programme. Semester abroad You will have the possibility to spend a semester at University of Southampton UK in Year 2, learning in a vibrant academic and cultural context. The selection of students for a semester abroad will depend on academic performance and a decision by programme lead.
chunks.title: Economics BSc (Hons) Curriculum and Learning Experience

chunks.text: Key information We are mandated by the University Grants Commission (UGC) to deliver the equivalent programme and quality as we offer in the UK. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, the BSc (Hons) Economics degree has a duration of three years. View University of Southampton Delhi term dates. Dates mentioned are subject to change. Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to the BSc (Hons) Economics degree delivered at University of Southampton in the UK. Your future career We are a top 20 UK university for employability (QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022) and offer extensive support for today’s competitive and rapidly changing employment landscape. After completing this Economics degree, you’ll have vital transferable skills for a wide range of careers in economics. The programme is well-suited to students aiming at corporate careers in banking, financial services, insurance, consultancy, and management. Career opportunities Many of our Economics degree graduates have worked as: economists statisticians business and investment analysts management executives financial advisers management consultants Careers services at University of Southampton Delhi Our proprietary Southampton skills model is integrated into our curriculum. When you join us, you’ll be able to use our skills model to track, plan, and benefit your career development and progress. As well as support from the Careers and Employability team in Delhi, you’ll have access to our market-leading employability development offering. This includes: online resources regular workshops personalised career guidance and support through our MyCareer platform and e-mentoring With links to multinational and industrial organisations, we enable you to engage with employers and explore potential career paths. We also host regular alumni events in-person across major cities in India, allowing you to grow your own networks. Our support is ongoing, and once you graduate, you’ll be part of a global community of over 220,000 alumni that spans over 180 countries.

chunks.text: Eligibility criteria Students must meet the overall academic and mathematics scores for Grade 12 to be eligible for the programme. Those who have studied academic disciplines in the Humanities or Commerce or Sciences including subjects such as Political Science or Business Studies can apply for the Programme. Mathematics in Class XII is a required subject. Students need not have studied economics before. In order to be eligible for the programme, students must have: Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE): Minimum 78% (overall) in your All-India Senior School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) with 80% in Mathematics in Standard XII. Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (CISCE): Minimum 78% (overall) in your Indian School Certificate examination (Standard XII) with 80% in Mathematics in Standard XII. State Board: Minimum 83% (overall) in your Higher Secondary Certificate (Standard XII) examination with 80% in Mathematics in Standard XII. International Baccalaureate: 34 points and above, including 17 points at Higher level including 5 at Higher Level Mathematics (Analysis and Approaches, or Applications and Interpretation). Offers will be made on the individual Diploma Course subject(s) and the career-related study qualification. The CP core will not form part of the offer. Where there is a subject pre-requisite(s), applicants will be required to study the subject(s) at Higher Level in the Diploma course subject and/or take a specified unit in the career-related study qualification. Applicants may also be asked to achieve a specific grade in those elements. Please see the University of Southampton International Baccalaureate Career-Related Programme (IBCP) Statement for further information. Applicants are advised to contact the Admissions Office for more information. A Levels: AAB, including Grade B in Mathematics English language requirement We accept several ways of meeting our English Language requirement. These include: IELTS Test, with scores of 6.5 overall (6.0 in reading, writing, speaking, and listening) other tests listed on our website an approved academic qualification having the required grade If you’ve been taught and assessed in English, including at a CBSE or CISCE school, for your final 4 years of high school, and have achieved at least 70% in English Language, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Refer to our English Language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements. Our academic admissions criteria Typical academic admissions criteria represent the usual level of academic attainment an applicant is expected to achieve prior to being admitted to that programme. These criteria are not intended to provide a guarantee that all applicants who satisfy the criteria may be offered a place, nor that all applicants to be made an offer will receive an identical offer to one another.

chunks.text: Creative Computing BSc (Hons) Award BSc (Hons) Duration 3 years Course Type Full Time Start Date 24 August 2026 Download Prospectus Contact Us Build immersive and engaging digital experiences that bring art and technology together in our creative computing degree. The programme develops creative, technical and intellectual strengths drawing on industry-standard technology and philosophical and ethical approaches. You will take the construction of digital projects to the screen and beyond using sculpture and fabrication. The programme will enhance strengths and skills in design and systems thinking, to bring focus on programming skills, user experience design, and building digital projects. You will research the aesthetic, sustainable, and political aspects of computer media formats including networks, social media, digital games and open source and activist initiatives. As a creative computing student, you will benefit from: virtual reality, augmented reality, robot design and programming systems access to fabrication and construction equipment exposure to social and collaborative computing guest speakers and industry events chances to showcase your work optional study trips to museums, galleries, and industry events
chunks.title: Creative Computing BSc (Hons) Programme Overview and Benefits

chunks.text: The programme structure includes the study of creative computing through coding, user experience design, making and research. Year 1 overview You will learn principles of programming for creative technological applications such as visual coding, AI, augmented reality, robotics and pervasive computing. You will study relevant theories and test out your skills. Year 2 overview Workshops and lectures will help you build on your technical expertise and follow your interests through a project. You will engage with user experience research and interactive systems, extending your knowledge and critical understanding of the creative industries, contexts for creative production, you gain employability skills. Year 3 overview A major project helps you to discover your creative insights and technical skills in depth – in an area that correlates with your own interests and career ambitions. Programme structure Students take a number of core and compulsory modules before selecting optional modules based on their interest. The programmes are designed to ensure that students gain the fundamentals of their discipline and then add specialisation and perspective through available optional modules. The 2025 Programme Description Document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, information on programme modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. As a research-led University, we undertake a continuous review of our programmes to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. As a result, this programme may be revised during a student’s period of registration; however, any revision will be balanced against the requirement that the student should receive the educational service expected. Please read our Disclaimer to see why, when, and how changes may be made to a student’s programme. Semester abroad You will have the possibility to spend a semester at University of Southampton UK in Year 2, learning in a vibrant academic and cultural context. The selection of students for a semester abroad will depend on academic performance and a decision by programme lead.

chunks.text: We are a top 20 UK university for employability (QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022) and offer extensive support for today’s competitive and rapidly changing financial landscape. BSc (Hons) Creative Computing students will graduate with industry-ready programming skills, and will have focussed on user experience design, physical fabrication or project management. All will have extensive experience in team production and hence of working within and towards computational-based projects. Career opportunities The Creative Computing degree offers career-focused study for employment in a wide range of industries. Graduates commonly work in a range of organisations or sectors including: Games studios Production companies Advertising and broadcasting companies Creative agencies PR and communications firms Education providers Software developers Careers services at University of Southampton Delhi Our proprietary Southampton skills model is integrated into our curriculum. When you join us, you’ll be able to use our skills model to track, plan, and benefit your career development and progress. As well as support from the Careers and Employability team in Delhi, you’ll have access to our market-leading employability development offering. This includes: online resources regular workshops personalised career guidance and support through our MyCareer platform and e-mentoring With links to multinational and industrial organisations, we enable you to engage with employers and explore potential career paths. We also host regular alumni events in-person across major cities in India, allowing you to grow your own networks. Our support is ongoing, and once you graduate, you’ll be part of a global community of over 220,000 alumni that spans over 180 countries.
chunks.title: Career Prospects for Creative Computing Graduates

chunks.text: Students with overall academic scores for Grade 12 and including a creative, humanities, and/or computing subject can apply. Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE): Minimum 75% (overall) in your All-India Senior School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (CISCE): Minimum 75% (overall) in your Indian School Certificate examination (Standard XII) State Board: Minimum 75% (overall) in your Higher Secondary Certificate (Standard XII) examination. International Baccalaureate: 30 points and above, with 15 points at Higher Level including a creative, humanities, and/or computing subject. A Levels: BBB including a creative, humanities and/or computing subject at A-level or equivalent Those who have studied in India from the Humanities or Commerce or Science streams can apply. Non-academic entry requirements All applicants will need to provide a portfolio of art work. The suitability of the portfolio submission for the intended course will form part of the assessment. English language requirement We accept several ways of meeting our English Language requirement. These include: IELTS Test, with scores of 6.5 overall (6.0 in reading, writing, speaking, and listening) other tests listed on our website an approved academic qualification having the required grade If you’ve been taught and assessed in English, including at a CBSE or CISCE school, for your final 4 years of high school, and have achieved at least 70% in English Language, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Refer to our English Language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements. Our academic admissions criteria Typical academic admissions criteria represent the usual level of academic attainment an applicant is expected to achieve prior to being admitted to that programme. These criteria are not intended to provide a guarantee that all applicants who satisfy the criteria may be offered a place, nor that all applicants to be made an offer will receive an identical offer to one another.

chunks.text: We offer competitive scholarships to talented students. We are now accepting registrations for a place at our campus in the Delhi NCR for the academic year starting August 2026. Potential students can understand more about our application process by looking at helpful guidance and advice on university admissions pages. To start the application process, register yourself. How to apply Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to that delivered to other University of Southampton students. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, undergraduates study for three years, and postgraduates study for one year.
chunks.title: Scholarships and Application Process for Delhi Campus

chunks.text: Can I apply for a scholarship or bursary? We welcome applications for a scholarship or bursary from any self-funding student who will be paying domestic Tuition Fees. Additional criteria, such as programme eligibility, are shown clearly on individual award pages. You must first make an application to study a specific programme before you make an application for a scholarship or bursary. Can I get an offer with predicted scores? We encourage students to apply as soon as possible so that they can receive a Conditional Offer based on predicted scores for their chosen programme. We can accept an application from a student using their predicted grades if we receive their final grades once they are available. Why is an Honours degree good for me? An Honours degree, shown with (Hons) in the programme title, mean that students will understand the fundamentals of their discipline and then build specialisation and perspective through optional modules. These fast-paced, intense programmes deliver essential learning in the first academic year and offer optional course modules, or electives, in the second year. Honours degrees are offered at University of Southampton campuses across the world, reflecting teaching and learning to a higher academic standard, meaning that an Honours degree would hold more value post graduation for future study and career development. How can I earn an Honours degree in just 3 years? The University Grants Commission (UGC) has mandated University of Southampton Delhi to make sure that programmes delivered in India are the same as those delivered at University of Southampton in the UK. Three years is the standard length of an undergraduate Honours degree in England where University of Southampton sets academic standards. This 3 year length reflects a trend towards accelerated learning where students see a return on their tuition fees as quickly as possible, whilst still maintaining high academic standards and the quality of the degree awarded. University of Southampton 3 year undergraduate degrees, and 1 year postgraduate degrees, are accepted widely internationally. Why is my degree award BSc (Hons) or BEng (Hons)? Degrees with a BA and BSc award do not represent a stream, but a broad categorisation that reflects student choice and leaning. Our BSc (Hons) and BEng (Hons) undergraduate degrees are oriented towards specialist, technical roles in industry with an analytical approach, not science subjects. What is an option? Our programmes are designed to make sure that students quickly understand the fundamentals of their discipline, and then build specialisation and perspective through electives, or optional, modules. These optional modules are typically offered in academic years 2 and 3, adding depth and breadth to core learning from previous years. As these options are often built around real-world trends, the specific choice of module available to students from year to year may vary.
chunks.title: Creative Computing Programme Frequently Asked Questions

chunks.text: Software Engineering BEng (Hons) Award BEng (Hons) Duration 3 years Course Type Full Time Start Date 24 August 2026 Choosing this programme Learn to develop reliable, complex and secure software systems. From mobile banking apps to aircraft autopilot controls, you’ll learn how to analyse software requirements, and then design, build and test a system that meets them. On this degree you’ll combine hands-on software development with a solid knowledge base of software engineering. Specialist modules will let you explore new developments in areas such as: robotics systems cyber security cloud application development Facilities Our computer lab offers facilities that span software projects, computing laboratory and cyber-security.
chunks.title: BEng (Hons) Software Engineering Programme Overview

chunks.text: What you will study The programme structure is designed to give you experience of core software technologies and techniques, while making it possible for you to work on your areas of specialised interest in depth. Year 1 overview The first year of the Software Engineering programme introduces you to the fundamental mathematics and theory that underpin the discipline, and gives hands-on experience of programming, computer hardware, computer networks and operating systems. You’ll study core topics such as: programming data management software modelling and design Year 2 overview The second year of the Software Engineering programme consolidates the material from the first year with an integrated software development module that runs throughout the year, and broadens out the curriculum to include functional programming, artificial intelligence, and formal specification and verification You’ll deepen your understanding of software engineering by studying topics, like: intelligent systems software design interaction design A group project will let you work within a team to design, create and test a software system. It will give you practical experience and deep exposure to teamwork and project management. Year 3 overview You’ll carry out an individual project in an area of software engineering that interests you. An academic, who is conducting research in that field, will provide support throughout. You’ll choose from a range of specialist Software Engineering modules, from across computer science and electronics. This will give you a deeper knowledge and let you explore topics such as: robotic systems machine learning cyber security computer vision Programme structure You’ll take core and compulsory modules in the first 2 years. In the third year you’ll complete an individual project and choose from a range of optional modules. The programmes are designed to ensure that students gain the fundamentals of their discipline and then add specialisation and perspective through available optional modules. The Programme Description Document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, information on programme modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. As a research-led University, we undertake a continuous review of our programmes to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. As a result, this programme may be revised during a student’s period of registration; however, any revision will be balanced against the requirement that the student should receive the educational service expected. Please read our Disclaimer to see why, when, and how changes may be made to a student’s programme. Semester abroad You will have the possibility to spend a semester at University of Southampton UK in Year 2, learning in a vibrant academic and cultural context. The selection of students for a semester abroad will depend on academic performance and a decision by programme lead.
chunks.title: Software Engineering Curriculum and Semester Abroad Option

Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to the BEng (Hons) Software Engineering degree delivered at University of Southampton in the UK.
