chunks.text:
Computer Science BSc (Hons)

Award BSc (Hons) Duration 3 years Course Type Full Time Start Date 24 August 2026

Choosing this programme

On this computer science degree, you’ll learn how to design and build software programs and systems. You’ll find out about the latest advances in areas like machine learning, cyber security and computer vision in this fast-moving industry and gain the knowledge to develop technologies that can make a difference to people’s lives. The curriculum ensures strong understanding of basics in mathematics, theory of computing and systems including computer architecture and computer networking. Pathways in Cyber Security, Data and Algorithms, Systems and Networks, Artificial Intelligence, and Applications continue from foundational levels into the third year, and potentially towards a specialist master’s degree. The world-leading undergraduate programme offers in-depth, comprehensive knowledge and aligns with the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) computing curriculum. You’ll study in state-of-the-art teaching labs with the latest equipment and high-quality displays. The content is equivalent to a BSc (Hons) Computer Science degree delivered to University of Southampton students in the UK.
chunks.title: Computer Science BSc (Hons) Programme Overview

chunks.text:
What you will study

A strong programming pathway runs through three years with Python as a first language, C as a second, Java as a third followed by Haskell as a fourth language. Through a mix of theoretical, design and practical subjects, you’ll learn about: basic computer science theory the ethical and legal frameworks which computer scientists and software engineers work within the practicalities of working with computers key techniques and application areas Year 1 overview You’ll study the logical and mathematical theory underpinning computer science. You’ll also gain an understanding of the fundamentals of computer hardware. As an introduction to software engineering, you’ll cover data structures and algorithms. You’ll also look at the principles of programming. Year 2 overview You’ll deepen your understanding of computer science by studying topics, such as artificial intelligence, communication protocols and the TCP/IP layered model. Year 3 overview A compulsory final year project is the capstone assessment for the three-year degree. Regular project work ensures teamwork, project planning, time management, presentation taking learning towards the successful completion of a brief. An individual project is a chance to conduct in-depth research in an area of computer science that interests you, under the supervision of an academic with expertise in your chosen field. Cybersecurity, applications and artificial intelligence pathways span diverse modules and you will get to choose optional modules on areas like: advanced databases machine learning technologies natural language processing security of cyber physical systems social computing techniques

Programme structure

This programme builds a comprehensive foundation in the discipline through inclusion of various modules as compulsory that are otherwise considered elective. Optional modules are geared to employability: they are attractive to students and well-received by industry. It includes research-led, contemporary compulsory modules on areas like formal specification and verification, the design of programming languages and interpreters and on artificial intelligence. The Programme Description Document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, information on programme modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. As a research-led University, we undertake a continuous review of our programmes to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. As a result, this programme may be revised during a student’s period of registration; however, any revision will be balanced against the requirement that the student should receive the educational service expected. Please read our Disclaimer to see why, when, and how changes may be made to a student’s programme.

Semester abroad

You will have the possibility to spend a semester at University of Southampton UK in Year 2, learning in a vibrant academic and cultural context. The selection of students for a semester abroad will depend on academic performance and a decision by programme lead.
chunks.title: Computer Science BSc (Hons) Curriculum and Structure

chunks.text:
Key information

We are mandated by the University Grants Commission (UGC) to deliver the equivalent programme and quality as we offer in the UK. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, the BSc (Hons) Computer Science degree has a duration of three years. View University of Southampton Delhi term dates. Dates mentioned are subject to change. Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to the BSc (Hons) Computer Science degree delivered at University of Southampton in the UK.
chunks.title: Key Information and Quality of Computer Science BSc (Hons)

chunks.text:
Your future career

We are a top 20 UK university for employability (QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022) and offer extensive support for today’s competitive and rapidly changing employment landscape. With this Computer Science degree, you can pursue programming, software development, IT project management, or information systems oversight. Recommendations of University of Southampton Industrial Advisory Board in the UK and partner companies feed into this course curriculum. It is a foundation for further study at master’s degree or PhD level anywhere in the world. Most of our graduates opt for computer science jobs in high-tech companies. Potential roles include: software engineer app developer web developer systems analyst

Careers services at University of Southampton Delhi

Our proprietary Southampton skills model is integrated into our curriculum. When you join us, you’ll be able to use our skills model to track, plan, and benefit your career development and progress. As well as support from the Careers and Employability team in Delhi, you’ll have access to our market-leading employability development offering. This includes: online resources regular workshops personalised career guidance and support through our MyCareer platform and e-mentoring With links to multinational and industrial organisations, we enable you to engage with employers and explore potential career paths. We also host regular alumni events in-person across major cities in India, allowing you to grow your own networks. Our support is ongoing, and once you graduate, you’ll be part of a global community of over 220,000 alumni that spans over 180 countries.
chunks.title: Computer Science BSc (Hons) Career Prospects and Employability

chunks.text:
Eligibility criteria

Students must meet the overall academic and mathematics scores for Grade 12 to be eligible for our programmes. Those who have studied academic disciplines in the Humanities or Commerce or Sciences including subjects such as Political Science or Business Studies can apply for the Programme. Mathematics in Class XII is a required subject. In order to be eligible for the programme, students must have:

Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE): Minimum 82% (overall) in your All-India Senior School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) with 85% in Standard XII Mathematics

Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (CISCE): Minimum 82% (overall) in your Indian School Certificate examination (Standard XII) with 85% in Standard XII Mathematics

State Board: Minimum 87% (overall) in your Higher Secondary Certificate (Standard XII) examination with 85% in Standard XII Mathematics

International Baccalaureate: 38 points and above, including 19 points at Higher Level, including 6 at Higher Level Mathematics (Analysis and Approaches) or 7 at Higher Level (Applications and Interpretations). Offers will be made on the individual Diploma Course subject(s) and the career-related study qualification. The CP core will not form part of the offer. Where there is a subject pre-requisite(s), applicants will be required to study the subject(s) at Higher Level in the Diploma course subject and/or take a specified unit in the career-related study qualification. Applicants may also be asked to achieve a specific grade in those elements. Please see the University of Southampton International Baccalaureate Career-Related Programme (IBCP) Statement for further information. Applicants are advised to contact the Admissions Office for more information.

A Levels: A*AA including mathematics (minimum grade A).

English language requirement

We accept several ways of meeting our English Language requirement. These include: IELTS Test, with scores of 6.5 overall (6.0 in reading, writing, speaking, and listening) other tests listed on our website an approved academic qualification having the required grade If you’ve been taught and assessed in English, including at a CBSE or CISCE school, for your final 4 years of high school, and have achieved at least 70% in English Language, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Refer to our English Language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements.

Our academic admissions criteria

Typical academic admissions criteria represent the usual level of academic attainment an applicant is expected to achieve prior to being admitted to that programme. These criteria are not intended to provide a guarantee that all applicants who satisfy the criteria may be offered a place, nor that all applicants to be made an offer will receive an identical offer to one another.
chunks.title: Computer Science BSc (Hons) Eligibility and English Language Requirements

chunks.text: Thank you for your interest in studying at University of Southampton’s Delhi campus. Use the form below to apply for undergraduate and postgraduate programmes starting from August 2026. You will not be able to save a partially completed application form; so please make sure that you have a strong internet connection and a quiet space to concentrate. The application form is best experienced on a desktop computer and requires 30-45 minutes to complete. When you have completed a page, the NEXT button will move you onwards. We encourage you to upload the documents listed below (if available). Doing so will help strengthen your application and give us a better understanding of your profile before we share our admission decision. There is a 2,000 INR application assessment fee which students would need to pay for us to process their application for August 2026. Need more help? For help completing the application form, or for more information about the status of your application, send an email to admissions@delhi.southampton.ac.uk. Our dedicated team of admissions specialists will reply as quickly as they can. We look forward to welcoming you to the campus in August 2026.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Campus Application Process and Fees

chunks.text: To apply for an Undergraduate Bachelor's programme, you need: Academic Certificates (certificates from Grade XII and Grade X) Students who are yet to complete their final year in school are required to provide Predicted Achievement i.e. the predicted score from your school. Please ensure that your predicted subject scores are specified in the document. Academic Transcripts (transcripts from Grade XII and Grade X) Proof of English Language proficiency You can see more guidance about English Language on our website. If you have been taught and assessed in English, including at a CBSE or CISCE school, for your final four years of high school, you may choose to use your English scores to meet the English Language requirement. Personal Statement (250-500 words) You can see more guidance about Personal Statements on our website. We suggest you keep a draft version ready, explaining your motivation to study your subject of interest at Southampton, and how you intend to pursue a career in the area. Letter of Reference Photo-Identity Card (such as Aadhaar card/passport)
chunks.title: Required Documents for Undergraduate Bachelor's Programme Application

chunks.text: To apply for a Postgraduate Master's programme, you need: Academic Certificates (Certificates from previous study) Students who are still to complete their final year in college, are required to provide Predicted Achievement i.e. the predicted score from your college. You will be required to provide the Final Certificates as soon as available. Academic Transcripts (transcripts from previous study) Proof of English Language proficiency You can see more guidance about English Language on our website. If you have successfully completed a degree taught and assessed in English up to two years preceding the date of first enrolment at the University of Southampton, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Personal Statement (250-500 words) You can see more guidance about Personal Statements on our website. We suggest you keep a draft version ready, explaining your motivation to study your subject of interest at Southampton, and how you intend to pursue a career in the area. Letter of Reference Photo-Identity Card (such as Adhaar card/passport) Resume or Curriculum Vitae (often known as a CV)
chunks.title: Required Documents for Postgraduate Master's Programme Application

chunks.text: International Management MSc Award MSc Duration 1 year Course Type Full Time Start Date 24 August 2026. Learn how a business operates and study key management concepts on our MSc International Management degree. The programme will give you insights into becoming an effective business manager in core management functions in our globalised workplace. It is open to graduates from a range of academic and professional backgrounds. The programme incorporates inter-disciplinary perspectives into management teaching, such as the application of technologies and sustainable business practices. You will learn: how organisations operate and are managed how to resolve issues through your understanding of management concepts analytical, communication and self-management skills The Business School is accredited to the Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business (AACSB) that signifies excellence in business education.
chunks.title: MSc International Management: Overview, Skills, and Accreditation

chunks.text: This is a full-time 1 year degree programme. The first 9 months of the programme comprises taught study, divided into 2 semesters. In semester 3 (the final 3 months) you’ll complete an individual research project for your dissertation. The course modules cover: management within a global context strategic management marketing accounting and control responsible leadership decision making operations management Programme structure The programme is built on a number of core and compulsory modules, that are designed to ensure that students gain a comprehensive understanding of the fundamentals of business. The programme includes modules such as: Managing within a global context: offered by the Department of Human Resource Management and Organisational Behaviour, this module explores advanced perspectives on management, leadership, power, culture and diversity. Career management in a digital age: developed by a globally recognised expert in the area of career management. Risk-taking and decision-making: this module emphasises how combining qualitative and quantitative thinking enhances effective decision-making in the face of risk and uncertainty. The Programme Description Document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, information on programme modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. As a research-led University, we undertake a continuous review of our programmes to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. As a result, this programme may be revised during a student’s period of registration; however, any revision will be balanced against the requirement that the student should receive the educational service expected. Please read our Disclaimer to see why, when, and how changes may be made to a student’s programme. Key information We are mandated by the University Grants Commission (UGC) to deliver the equivalent programme and quality as we offer in the UK. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, the MSc International Management degree has a duration of one year. View University of Southampton Delhi term dates. Dates mentioned are subject to change. Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to the MSc International Management degree delivered at University of Southampton in the UK.
chunks.title: International Management MSc: Modules, Structure, and UK Equivalence

chunks.text: Your future career We are a top 20 UK university for employability (QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022) and offer extensive support for today’s competitive and rapidly changing managment landscape. By the end of this Master’s in International Management, you’ll have essential skills for management careers in diverse organisations. Career opportunities Graduates commonly work in a range of organisations or sectors in business and industry, at government agencies, third sector organisations in disciplines such as finance, marketing and project management or as entrepreneurs at start-ups. Careers directly related to this course: auditor business analyst business development manager company secretary credit officer human resources officer project manager managing consultant marketing manager data analyst financial analyst public affairs manager Careers services at University of Southampton Delhi Our proprietary Southampton skills model is integrated into our curriculum. When you join us, you’ll be able to use our skills model to track, plan, and benefit your career development and progress. As well as support from the Careers and Employability team in Delhi, you’ll have access to our market-leading employability development offering. This includes: online resources regular workshops personalised career guidance and support through our MyCareer platform and e-mentoring With links to multinational and industrial organisations, we enable you to engage with employers and explore potential career paths. We also host regular alumni events in-person across major cities in India, allowing you to grow your own networks. Our support is ongoing, and once you graduate, you’ll be part of a global community of over 220,000 alumni that spans over 180 countries.
chunks.title: Career Prospects and Employability Support for Management Graduates

chunks.text: Eligibility criteria To apply for this programme, you’ll need an upper second-class degree, termed a 2:1 degree, or international equivalent in one of the following subjects: engineering humanities business mathematics physical sciences information systems management social sciences languages English language requirement We accept several ways of meeting our English Language requirement. These include: IELTS Test, with scores of 6.5 overall (6.0 in reading, writing, speaking, and listening) other tests listed on our website an approved academic qualification having the required grade If you’ve successfully completed a degree, taught and assessed in English, up to 2 years before the date of first enrolment at the University of Southampton, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Refer to our English Language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements. Our academic admissions criteria Typical academic admissions criteria represent the usual level of academic attainment an applicant is expected to achieve prior to being admitted to that programme. These criteria are not intended to provide a guarantee that all applicants who satisfy the criteria may be offered a place, nor that all applicants to be made an offer will receive an identical offer to one another.
chunks.title: International Management MSc Eligibility and English Language Requirements

For students starting in August 2026, the deposit is: 1,61,700 INR for India students The balance of the yearly tuition fee is paid in 3 instalments. 3,88,080 INR by Monday 07 September 2026 5,33,610 INR by Monday 25 January 2027 5,33,610 INR by Monday 19 April 2027 Fees for international students The fees for the year starting in August 2026 for international students are: 18,48,000 INR The tuition fee is paid in 4 instalments. For students starting in August 2026, the deposit is: 1,84,800 INR for international students If you are an international student, you’ll have to make 3 separate payments of: 4,33,520 INR by Monday 07 September 2026 6,09,840 INR by Monday 25 January 2027 6,09,840 INR by Monday 19 April 2027 We may adjust the fees in the future to account for inflation and changes in other costs. chunks.text: Economics MSc Award MSc Duration 1 year Course Type Full Time Start Date 24 August 2026 Gain in-depth knowledge of the concepts, methods and applications of modern microeconomics, macroeconomics and econometrics with a one-year master’s degree from an international university. The degree will prepare you for a career as an economist working in finance, consulting and policy or to continue research studies. The programme equips you to understand the theoretical foundations of economic analysis, work with economic models and apply economic analysis to a range of policy areas. You will develop research skills and learn to use a variety of research methodologies to test and evaluate economic models. You will look at economic theory and econometric methods across a range of topics and develop in-depth knowledge to carry out independent research. This will lead to a dissertation on a subject of your interest.
chunks.title: Economics MSc Program Introduction and Core Curriculum

chunks.text: What you will study This is a full-time 1 year degree programme. The programme structure comprises 2 semesters of taught study followed by a final semester of independent research for your dissertation in the final semester 3. Semester 1 builds sound working knowledge of concepts and methods used in economics and economic research at the postgraduate level. Semester 2 allows you to build on these foundations and deepen your understanding of advanced methods in economics as well as analysing questions and problems in specific areas of economics that reflect your interest. After completing the taught component, you will engage for three months in supervised independent research, yielding a master’s dissertation. Programme structure The programmes are designed to ensure that students gain the fundamentals of their discipline and then add specialisation and perspective through available optional modules. The Programme Description Document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, information on programme modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. As a research-led University, we undertake a continuous review of our programmes to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. As a result, this programme may be revised during a student’s period of registration; however, any revision will be balanced against the requirement that the student should receive the educational service expected. Key information We are mandated by the University Grants Commission (UGC) to deliver the equivalent programme and quality as we offer in the UK. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, the MSc Economics degree has a duration of one year. Dates mentioned are subject to change. Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to the MSc Economics degree delivered at University of Southampton in the UK.
chunks.title: Economics MSc Programme Structure, Semesters, and UK Equivalence

chunks.text: Your future career We are a top 20 UK university for employability (QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022) and offer extensive support for today’s competitive and rapidly changing financial landscape. The programme emphasises the application of evidence-based economic reasoning to policy questions, providing students with much-demanded skills as economists in financial or consulting firms, as well as in government or other institutions. Career opportunities Graduates commonly work in a range of organisations or sectors including banking and financial services, not-for-profit organisations, consultancies, Government departments and think tanks. Careers directly related to this course: Data analyst Economist Financial risk analyst Investment analyst Political risk analyst Statistician Wider career opportunities: Data scientist Diplomatic service Administrative service Management consultant Careers services at University of Southampton Delhi Our proprietary Southampton skills model is integrated into our curriculum. When you join us, you’ll be able to use our skills model to track, plan, and benefit your career development and progress. As well as support from the Careers and Employability team in Delhi, you’ll have access to our market-leading employability development offering. This includes: online resources regular workshops personalised career guidance and support through our MyCareer platform and e-mentoring With links to multinational and industrial organisations, we enable you to engage with employers and explore potential career paths. We also host regular alumni events in-person across major cities in India, allowing you to grow your own networks. Our support is ongoing, and once you graduate, you’ll be part of a global community of over 220,000 alumni that spans over 180 countries.
chunks.title: Economics MSc Graduate Career Paths and Employability Support

chunks.text: L.E.K. Scholar Award Award duration: 3 academic years Study period covered: Aug 2025 - Jun 2028 Funding type: Merit based scholarship Study Level: Undergraduate About the award The L.E.K. Scholar Award will be awarded to academically outstanding and suitably qualified applicants of the BSc (Hons) Economics and BSc (Hons) Accounting & Finance programmes. University of Southampton Delhi will offer scholarships to up to 12 undergraduate students enrolling on these programmes in August 2025. This awards will contribute towards tuition fees and will be offered to students who exceed the academic admission criteria of their chosen programme. Recipients of the L.E.K. Scholar Award will receive priority consideration for L.E.K. Consulting’s Summer Internship Programme (2026), offering a valuable opportunity for practical exposure and professional development, subject to performance and eligibility. The L.E.K. Scholar Awards will be conferred on an ongoing basis. What we will award Up to 12 scholarships for undergraduate students of ₹1.5 lakh each, per academic year, towards University of Southampton Delhi tuition fees in academic years 25/26, 26/27 and 27/28. How we make award decisions The L.E.K. Scholar Award is to be awarded to academically outstanding and suitably qualified applicants of the BSc Economics and BSc Accounting & Finance programmes. To be eligible, students must hold an unconditional offer to study BSc (Hons) Economics or BSc (Hons) Accounting at University of Southampton Delhi, starting August 2025. Making an application for this award No separate scholarship application is needed. The L.E.K. Scholar Award is awarded to academically outstanding and suitably qualified applicants of the BSc (Hons) Economics and BSc (Hons) Accounting & Finance programmes. You might also want to know What additional benefits are available to L.E.K. Scholars? In addition to the scholarship, recipients of the L.E.K. Scholar Award will receive priority consideration for L.E.K. Consulting’s Summer Internship Programme. This provides an excellent opportunity for hands-on experience and professional growth, subject to performance and eligibility. Who is eligible to apply for an L.E.K. Scholar Award? Students with an unconditional offer to study for BSc (Hons) Economics or BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance can be considered for an L.E.K. Scholar award. Why should I apply for an L.E.K. Scholar Award? This scholarship will represent a highly prestigious accolade for our top students, aligned with one of the world’s most respected strategy consulting firms and offering an outstanding pathway into top-tier global career opportunities.
chunks.title: L.E.K. Scholar Award for Economics and Accounting & Finance Students

chunks.text:
Cambridge Scholarship Award
Award duration: 1 academic year
Study period covered: Aug 2025 - Jun 2026
Funding type: Competitive scholarship
August 2025 intake: Applications for this award are now closed for August 2025

About the award
University of Southampton Delhi will offer up to four exclusive scholarships for Cambridge A-Level graduates in India applying for the August 2025 intake. These scholarships will contribute towards 1 year of tuition fees at University of Southampton Delhi in academic year 25/26. University will waive application fees for all Cambridge students applying between 01 May and 31 May 2025. Cambridge applicants will benefit from a priority application review through a dedicated evaluation team, ensuring an efficient and streamlined admissions process. The Cambridge Scholarship Awards will be announced in June/July 2025.

What we will award
Up to 4 exclusive scholarship awards of a flat contribution of 220,000 INR towards tuition fees due to University of Southampton Delhi in academic year 25/26.

Which programmes qualify for this award?
Students who enrol on one of these programmes could qualify for this award:
- Accounting and Finance BSc (Hons)
- Business Management BSc (Hons)
- Computer Science BSc (Hons)
- Economics BSc (Hons)

How we make award decisions
University’s Scholarship Committee will select recipients based on outstanding academic performance and excellence in A-Level results. The committee will make a final decision on this scholarship.

Making an application for this award
If you already have made an application to study, you can now make an application for this competitive scholarship. We welcome applications from all Cambridge students applying to any of our undergraduate programmes for the University of Southampton Delhi August 2025 intake. To be considered for this prestigious award, students must complete a scholarship application, showcasing their exceptional academic achievements and commitment to excellence.

You might also want to know
Is there an application fee waiver for Cambridge students applying to the University of Southampton Delhi?
Yes, all Cambridge students applying to any undergraduate programme at University of Southampton Delhi between 01 May and 31 May 2025 will have their application fees waived.

Who is eligible to apply for the Cambridge Scholarship Awards?
The Cambridge Scholarship Awards are open to all Cambridge A-Level graduates in India who are applying to selected undergraduate programmes at University of Southampton Delhi.
chunks.title: Cambridge Scholarship Award for A-Level Graduates at UoSD

chunks.text: Creative Computing BSc (Hons). Award: BSc (Hons). Duration: 3 years. Course Type: Full Time. Start Date: August 2026. The programme combines technology, creativity, design, and digital innovation. Students develop industry-ready programming skills while specialising in user experience design, physical fabrication, or project management. The curriculum focuses on creating immersive and engaging digital experiences by bringing together art and technology. Students gain extensive experience in team-based production and computational projects. The programme develops creative, technical, and intellectual strengths using industry-standard technologies while considering philosophical and ethical approaches. The degree is taught in English and is equivalent to the University of Southampton UK programme. The University is mandated by the UGC to deliver equivalent programme quality as offered in the United Kingdom.
chunks.title: Creative Computing BSc (Hons) | University of Southampton Delhi

chunks.text:
Future Talent Scholarship. Award duration: 1 academic year. Study period covered: Aug 2025 - Jun 2026. Funding type: Competitive scholarship. August 2025 intake: Applications for this award are now closed for August 2025.

About the award: We will offer a Future Talent Scholarship to up to 12 gifted students, selected across all University of Southampton Delhi programmes, in support of their academic studies from August 2025. This competitive scholarship will contribute towards 1 year of tuition fees at University of Southampton Delhi in academic year 25/26. Our Scholarship Committee will make their award decision based on the student’s impressive past performance and high standards of academic achievement. We will announce the first recipient of our Future Talent Scholarships in May/June 2025, with final recipients announced in July 2025, depending on the release dates of exam board results.

What we will award: Up to 12 individual awards of a flat contribution of 220,000 INR towards tuition fees due to University of Southampton Delhi in academic year 25/26.

Which programmes qualify for this award? Students who enrol on one of these programmes could qualify for this award:
- Accounting and Finance BSc (Hons)
- Business Management BSc (Hons)
- Computer Science BSc (Hons)
- Economics BSc (Hons)
- Finance MSc
- International Management MSc
chunks.title: Future Talent Scholarship: Overview, Value, and Eligible Programmes

chunks.text:
How we make award decisions: We aim to manage our scholarships and bursaries fairly, providing support for academically gifted students, and helping those who might otherwise struggle to study with us. We consider a range of criteria, including the academic achievements shown on an application to study in addition to individual scholarship and bursary applications. This information helps us to produce a shortlist of candidates for an award. We will invite those on the shortlist to meet with us to discuss their application. A scholarships panel will make a final decision on scholarship and bursary applications.

Making an application for this award: We cannot accept any further applications for this award for students wishing to start their programme in August 2025. We welcome applications for this competitive scholarship from any domestic student making an application to study any of our programmes in the University of Southampton Delhi August 2025 intake. Students who wish to be considered for this award must complete a specific scholarship application showing impressive past performance and high standards of academic achievement. This should signpost their potential for future excellence in their chosen area of study at University of Southampton Delhi.
chunks.title: Future Talent Scholarship Application and Award Decision Process

chunks.text:
You might also want to know:

How can I contribute to the community as a scholarship student? We would encourage - and expect - that the recipient of a scholarship or bursary will act as a Student Ambassador to promote University of Southampton Delhi while they study with us.

How would I receive my scholarship or bursary? There is no cash alternative to the waiver of Tuition Fees made under a scholarship or bursary. We will reduce the amount of Tuition Fees that you will pay by the value of the award.

Can I apply for a scholarship or bursary? We welcome applications for a scholarship or bursary from any self-funding student who will be paying domestic Tuition Fees. Additional criteria, such as programme eligibility, are shown clearly on individual award pages. You must first make an application to study a specific programme before you make an application for a scholarship or bursary.

What are the terms and conditions for scholarships? You can see our current terms and conditions for scholarships and bursaries here.
chunks.title: Scholarship Student Responsibilities and General Terms

chunks.text:
The Future Talent Bursary is offered for a duration of 3 academic years, covering the study period from August 2025 to June 2028. This merit bursary is for undergraduate students, with applications for the August 2025 intake now closed. Up to 8 undergraduate students will receive support for their academic studies starting August 2025. This bursary aims to financially assist talented students at the University of Southampton Delhi, contributing towards tuition fees for the three-year undergraduate programme. Student selection is based on an assessment of need and eligibility, ensuring a respectful process.

Successful applicants will receive up to 8 individual awards, each providing a flat contribution of no more than 660,000 INR towards tuition fees due to the University of Southampton Delhi over academic years 25/26, 26/27, and 27/28. Programmes qualifying for this award include: Accounting and Finance BSc (Hons), Business Management BSc (Hons), Computer Science BSc (Hons), and Economics BSc (Hons).
chunks.title: Future Talent Bursary: Overview and Eligible Programmes

chunks.text:
The University of Southampton Delhi manages scholarships and bursaries fairly, supporting academically gifted students and those facing financial challenges. Award decisions consider academic achievements from the study application and individual scholarship applications. A scholarships panel makes the final decision after shortlisting candidates. Applications for the August 2025 intake are closed. This merit bursary targets domestic undergraduate students whose financial circumstances would otherwise hinder their study at the University of Southampton Delhi, aligning with the university's Corporate Social Responsibility to improve life chances, promote social mobility, and increase student diversity.

Applicants considered for this award must submit a financial statement as part of their bursary application, which will be handled with sensitivity and confidentiality. The names of recipients of the Future Talent Bursary will not be publicly disclosed, with individual communication for successful applicants. To continue receiving the bursary in subsequent years, students must meet agreed performance and academic standards.
chunks.title: Future Talent Bursary: Decision Process and Application Details

chunks.text: As a scholarship or bursary recipient, students are encouraged and expected to act as a Student Ambassador to promote the University of Southampton Delhi. The scholarship or bursary award provides no cash alternative; instead, it reduces the amount of Tuition Fees payable by the student. Self-funding students paying domestic Tuition Fees are welcome to apply for a scholarship or bursary, provided they meet additional criteria such as programme eligibility. It is mandatory to first apply to study a specific programme before applying for a scholarship or bursary. A merit bursary is defined as financial aid for students who would otherwise be unable to fund their education, based on both financial ability and academic performance. The current terms and conditions for scholarships and bursaries are available on the university's website.
chunks.title: Future Talent Bursary: Frequently Asked Questions

chunks.text:
Undergraduate Delhi Presidential Scholarship
Award duration: 3 academic years
Study period covered: Aug 2025 - Jun 2028
Funding type: Competitive scholarship
Study Level: Undergraduate
August 2025 intake: Applications for this award are now closed for August 2025
About the award
To recognise and encourage academic excellence at University of Southampton Delhi we will offer a Delhi Presidential Scholarship to 1 undergraduate student from August 2025. This scholarship will fully fund the tuition fees for the 3 academic year duration of a University of Southampton Delhi undergraduate programme. Our Scholarship Committee will make their award decision based on an exceptional scholarship application, past academic performance and outstanding potential as a student and a graduate of University of Southampton Delhi. We will announce the recipient of the Undergraduate Delhi Presidential Scholarship in July 2025.
What we will award
A 100% waiver of the Tuition Fees due to University of Southampton Delhi over academic years 25/26, 26/27 and 27/28 to 1 domestic undergraduate student.
chunks.title: Undergraduate Delhi Presidential Scholarship: Overview and Benefits

chunks.text:
Which programmes qualify for this award? Students who enrol on one of these programmes could qualify for this award:-
Accounting and Finance BSc (Hons)
Business Management BSc (Hons)
Computer Science BSc (Hons)
Economics BSc (Hons)
How we make award decisions
We aim to manage our scholarships and bursaries fairly, providing support for academically gifted students, and helping those who might otherwise struggle to study with us. We consider a range of criteria, including the academic achievements shown on an application to study in addition to individual scholarship and bursary applications. This information helps us to produce a shortlist of candidates for an award. We will invite those on the shortlist to meet with us to discuss their application. A scholarships panel will make a final decision on scholarship and bursary applications.
Making an application for this award
We cannot accept any further applications for this award for students wishing to start their programme in August 2025. We welcome applications for this competitive scholarship from any domestic student making an application to study an undergraduate programme in the University of Southampton Delhi August 2025 intake. Students who wish to be considered for this award must complete a specific scholarship application demonstrating their appetite for the values, academic aspirations and community spirit of University of Southampton Delhi. To continue to benefit from the scholarship in subsequent years, students must meet agreed performance and academic standards as they progress from year to year.
chunks.title: Undergraduate Delhi Presidential Scholarship: Eligibility and Application

chunks.text: You might also want to know Will I lose my deposit if I get a full scholarship? If you are awarded a full scholarship - which waives 100% of Tuition Fees - we will refund the value of any Deposit that you may already have paid. How would I receive my scholarship or bursary? There is no cash alternative to the waiver of Tuition Fees made under a scholarship or bursary. We will reduce the amount of Tuition Fees that you will pay by the value of the award. How can I contribute to the community as a scholarship student? We would encourage - and expect - that the recipient of a scholarship or bursary will act as a Student Ambassador to promote University of Southampton Delhi while they study with us. Can I apply for a scholarship or bursary? We welcome applications for a scholarship or bursary from any self-funding student who will be paying domestic Tuition Fees. Additional criteria, such as programme eligibility, are shown clearly on individual award pages. You must first make an application to study a specific programme before you make an application for a scholarship or bursary. What are the terms and conditions for scholarships? You can see our current terms and conditions for scholarships and bursaries here.
chunks.title: FAQs and Terms for University of Southampton Delhi Scholarships

chunks.text:
The Postgraduate Delhi Presidential Scholarship offers a 1-academic-year award covering August 2025 to June 2026. This competitive scholarship is for postgraduate study, though applications for the August 2025 intake are now closed.

About the award: To recognise and encourage academic excellence at University of Southampton Delhi, a Delhi Presidential Scholarship will be awarded to one postgraduate student starting in August 2025. This scholarship fully funds the tuition fees for the 1-year duration of a University of Southampton Delhi postgraduate programme. The Scholarship Committee makes decisions based on an exceptional scholarship application, past academic performance, and outstanding potential. The recipient of the Postgraduate Delhi Presidential Scholarship will be announced in June 2025.

What we will award: A 100% waiver of the tuition fees due to University of Southampton Delhi in academic year 2025/2026 for one domestic postgraduate student.
chunks.title: Postgraduate Delhi Presidential Scholarship: Overview and Award Details

chunks.text:
Which programmes qualify for this award? Students who enrol on one of these programmes could qualify for this award:
- Finance MSc
- International Management MSc

How we make award decisions: We aim to manage our scholarships and bursaries fairly, providing support for academically gifted students and helping those who might otherwise struggle to study with us. We consider a range of criteria, including academic achievements demonstrated in the application to study, in addition to individual scholarship and bursary applications. This information helps us to produce a shortlist of candidates for an award. Those on the shortlist will be invited to meet with us to discuss their application. A scholarships panel will make a final decision on scholarship and bursary applications.
chunks.title: Postgraduate Delhi Scholarship: Eligible Programmes and Selection Criteria

chunks.text:
Making an application for this award: Applications are no longer accepted for students wishing to start their programme in August 2025. This competitive scholarship is open to any domestic student applying for a postgraduate programme in the University of Southampton Delhi August 2025 intake. Applicants must complete a specific scholarship application demonstrating their commitment to the values, academic aspirations, and community spirit of University of Southampton Delhi.

You might also want to know:
Will I lose my deposit if I get a full scholarship? If awarded a full scholarship, any deposit paid will be refunded.
How can I contribute to the community as a scholarship student? Recipients are encouraged and expected to act as Student Ambassadors for University of Southampton Delhi.
How would I receive my scholarship or bursary? There is no cash alternative; the award reduces the amount of Tuition Fees payable.
Can I apply for a scholarship or bursary? Applications are welcome from any self-funding student paying domestic Tuition Fees. Programme eligibility and other criteria are detailed on individual award pages. You must first apply to a specific programme before applying for a scholarship or bursary.
What are the terms and conditions for scholarships? Current terms and conditions are available here.
chunks.title: Applying for Postgraduate Delhi Presidential Scholarship and FAQ

chunks.text: How can I travel between the student hostel and campus? Perhaps the best way to get to campus in the morning from our student hostel, or back there again at night, is the private shuttle bus. This free service will run at regular intervals every weekday. When can I book a room at the student hostel? You can apply for a room at the student hostel when you have completed your application to study. We expect demand for rooms to be high, but you can express interest in accommodation for free, which which does not commit you to any contract. We will be in touch with those who have shown interest in accommodation to advise on availability and how to book a room.
chunks.title: UoSD Student Hostel Travel and Accommodation Booking

chunks.text:
Campus Open Day 6 December 2025
Event Status: Book Now
Date & Time: December 06, 2025 10:00 AM IST
Location: International Tech Park Gurgaon, Near Sec 59, Off, Golf Course Ext Rd, Gurugram, Haryana 122101, India
Recommended for: Students (and their families) who do not yet have an offer to study with us
See more and book Discover University of Southampton Delhi for the August 2026 intake! If you do not yet have an offer to study with us, join us for an open day. Experience a glimpse of life at University of Southampton Delhi, explore our facilities, meet members of our community, and get a sense of what it means to be part of a vibrant, globally connected learning environment. Don’t miss the chance to see where your learning journey could begin. We look forward to welcoming you!

Join a guided tour
Join a guided tour, seeing campus facilities such as classrooms, library, labs, sports facilities and social spaces. Visitors will be able to board our complementary shuttle bus to visit the nearby student accommodation.

Learn about graduate employment and enterprise
Learn about graduate employment and enterprise opportunities. Our Career Advancement Team will be on hand to show the wide range of employability, and further study opportunities, available after graduation.

Join an academic session
Experience learning and teaching the Southampton way and join an academic session.

Attend inspiring talks
Attend inspiring talks from senior staff and leaders explaining why the campus is a unique place to study.

Speak with student ambassadors
Speak with student ambassadors to get a truly personal view of the Southampton experience. Visitors will hear first-hand about life on campus and why our students choose to study with us.

Get advice from a Service Desk
Drop-in to get advice from a Service Desk, answering individual questions about academic content, getting an admissions offer, the student experience and employability after graduation.

Meet future classmates
Meet future classmates before enrolling in August, forming friendships now that will last for a lifetime.

Make an application
Prospective students will be able to make an application on the spot, receiving personalised support from the friendly Admissions team, ready to take them through every step of the process.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Campus Open Day 2025

chunks.text: Visitor arrival and registration: 10:00am Enjoy some refreshments as you sign in at Reception and decide what sessions you would like to attend on campus. Drop-ins: 10:00am A chance to talk our dedicated staff about and get answers to any detailed questions you may have. You will find expert guidance here on anything related to admissions, placement and careers, the library, and the amazing experience that awaits students. Staff here can answer questions on Admissions, Student Experience, Careers and Employability and Academics. Academic faculty meetings: by appointment until 1:00pm Talk to the world-leading academics who will deliver programmes from August. These short informal and individual sessions are your chance to ask questions about the programme content, the quality of academic teaching, and a way to get to know your faculty before term starts. We ask you to make an appointment in advance. Guided campus tour: 10:15am Join a 25-minute guided tour of the campus. We will show you our purpose built learning and teaching rooms, the first floor library, break-out and collaborative spaces, computing, financial and technical laboratories and our Student Hub. And you will see the impressive shared facilities outside the main campus building such as food courts, the health suite, shuttle bus station and sports facilities. Please assemble at Reception 10 minutes before your tour is scheduled to leave. Leadership talk: 10:45am We invite you to a 40-minute session to hear university leaders from the India and the UK explain why University of Southampton Delhi is a true landmark for Higher Education in India. You will also discover the distinctive style of teaching and learning, world-class research and support for enterprise and start up business. To complete the session, we will showcase the amazing careers that alumni have embarked upon when they have graduated from a University of Southampton programme. Please be seated by 10:40 am. Programme talks 12:00 noon Academic staff will talk about their programmes and experience in these separate sessions:-

chunks.text: Accounting and Finance: Money Talks Behavioural Economics Business Management Computer Science and Software Engineering Creative Computing

chunks.text: You might also want to know Can I visit the campus in person or book a tour? Our campus is located at International Tech Park Gurgaon, Near Sec 59, Off, Golf Course Ext Rd, Gurugram, Haryana 122101. The best way to see campus is to book a pre-planned tour from our events page. Should I wait to make my application to study? We encourage students to make their application to study at University of Southampton Delhi as soon as they can. This means we can issue an early Conditional Offer and that students will have the best the chance to meet their offer conditions. If demand is high, we may choose to close applications earlier than mentioned, but students with Offers to study would receive preferential treatment. How can I meet your team? You can meet us online, and in a selection of major cities in India. You can see when and where, by visiting the Events area of our website. chunks.text: Career advancement We are extremely proud of our employability support. By preparing students to be career-ready, as well as earn their globally ranked university degree, we help open up a world of employment and entrepreneurship once they graduate. World class careers and opportunity We have a strong reputation for producing top graduate talent as students start their employment journey with leading firms. Many will start their own businesses, with 42% expressing an interest in entrepreneurship. Our Successful Futures programme encourages students to reflect on their skills throughout their studies and through real-world experience. And partnerships with respected and well-known employers guarantee employability opportunities for students. Global 'Top 110' best university for employability Source: QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022 UK's #15 university for graduate prospects Source: Times and Sunday Times Good University Guide 2025 UK's #10 university targeted by top employers Source: The Graduate Market in 2023 by High Fliers Research
chunks.title: University's Employability Support and Global Rankings

chunks.text: Successful Futures Research shows that the more students plan their careers during their time at university, the more likely it is they will get a graduate-level job or move into further study. As a result, each programme at the university has employability awareness and development embedded through an approach that we call Successful Futures. The Successful Futures approach delivers a uniquely Southampton skills model through both an online self-assessment and a personalised employability and enterprise learner journey. Through it, among other, we focus on equal access to careers education and on preparing students for lifelong learning by empowering them to identify, reflect on and develop their employability and enterprise skills. Our skills model The University of Southampton skills model comprises 14 skills which have been identified by research with employers, academic colleagues, students and graduates as key skills graduate recruiters want. All our degree programmes have been mapped against the skills model.Each student undertakes a skills self-assessment online at start of their journey with the university and on an annual basis thereafter. This digital resource was developed using global recruitment data and is designed to encourage students to explore career ideas, consider their career choices, reflect on skills gained and signpost opportunities for skills development. We believe that if students have awareness of what skills employers want for their career choice, which skills they are developing in their course and where they can find opportunities to develop skills further, they will be better prepared for a graduate recruitment market that places increasing emphasis on skills rather than simply on qualifications.
chunks.title: Successful Futures Programme and Skills Model Overview

chunks.text: A structured approach for better outcomes We support students to:-use the Skills Self-Assessment to identify and reflect on employability skills they are developing throughout their programme of study.identify, and plan how to acquire additional skills and experience they may want or need to help them meet their career aspirations.undertake the work experience, international semester option and enterprise support to prepare for their future.successfully articulate their skills to graduate recruiters or in pitching for funding as a start-up.use intensive job coaching to be fully prepared for guaranteed interviews with top graduate recruiters.So that when engaging with employers students will:-have already worked on industry problems via live briefs, increasing their commercial awareness.have had real world experience from internships, gaining understanding of what is needed in the workplace.have gained international experience, gaining greater awareness of other cultures and marketsbe fully prepared for recruitment interviews, helping employers identify the best fit for their needs.be better able to clearly articulate their skills to graduate recruiters or in pitching for funding as a start-up.
chunks.title: Structured Student Career Support and Outcomes

chunks.text: Plan your career with E-Mentoring Southampton E-Mentoring Southampton connects you with more than 2,500 industry mentors around the world for expert career advice and support in finding your first graduate job. All E-Mentors are Southampton alumni and supporters, who are waiting to hear from you and help you with your career questions.Ask Southampton graduates questions about their career and receive real-world answers from the experts.Connect with industry mentors working for companies and sectors you’re interested in.Build a professional network with a global community of University of Southampton alumni.Plan your career with the support and expertise of our mentor community.
chunks.title: E-Mentoring Southampton for Career Guidance

chunks.text: A tradition of success in India and worldwide University of Southampton has a long and proud tradition of seeing graduates start successful careers in India and worldwide locations, with globally recognised companies in a variety of market sectors. We have been proud to empower our graduates to join these top names over recent years:-Aerospace, transport and automotiveAirbus - The world's leading helicopter and airliner manufacturer.Alstom - French multinational rolling stock manufacturer which operates worldwide in rail transport markets.Rolls-Royce - Multinational aerospace and defense company that designs, manufactures, and distributes power systems for aviation and other industries.Toyota - Japanese multinational automotive manufacturer.Financial services and bankingAmerican Express - American bank holding company and multinational financial services corporation that specializes in payment cards.First Abu Dhabi Bank - Largest bank in the United Arab Emirates.JP Morgan - Global financial services firm, offering a wide range of services including investment banking, asset management, and private banking.Metlife - One of the world's leading financial services companies, providing insurance, annuities, employee benefits and asset management.

chunks.text: Employment partnerships Our curricula incorporate global perspectives through case studies, projects, and literature. Internships, placements and career guidance are extensive, recognising their importance to students and families. We seek the same qualities in all our campuses regardless of location - academic excellence, intellectual curiosity, strong ethical values, and a willingness to tackle global challenges. We value students who have an international outlook and an open mind, since we are shaping future global leaders. chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Campus Open Day Introduction

chunks.text: Visitor arrival and registration - 10:30 am Enjoy some refreshments as you sign in at Reception and decide what sessions you would like to attend on campus. Academic faculty meet and greet - 10:30 am - 11:15 am Talk to the world-leading academics who will deliver programmes from August. These short informal and individual sessions are your chance to ask questions about the programme content, the quality of academic teaching, and a way to get to know your faculty before term starts. There is no need to book, but we would ask you to wait a few minutes if an academic is talking to another visitor when you arrive. Student support and 'spot offer' helpdesks - 10:30 am - 11:15 am A chance to talk our dedicated support staff about and get answers to any detailed questions you may have. You will find expert guidance here on anything related to admissions, placement and careers, the library, and the amazing experience that awaits students. And when you are ready to make your application, you could receive a provisional ‘spot offer’ on the day letting you know you have a place in August when you meet academic and eligibility conditions. University leadership formal welcome - 11:15 am - 12:00 pm We invite you to a 40-minute session to hear university leaders from the India and the UK explain why University of Southampton Delhi is a true landmark for Higher Education in India. You will also discover the distinctive style of teaching and learning, world-class research and support for enterprise and start up business. To complete the session, we will showcase the amazing careers that alumni have embarked upon when they have graduated from a University of Southampton programme.
chunks.title: Early Morning Sessions and University Leadership Welcome

chunks.text: Academic faculty meet and greet - 12:00 pm - 2:30 pm Talk to the world-leading academics who will deliver programmes from August. These short informal and individual sessions are your chance to ask questions about the programme content, the quality of academic teaching, and a way to get to know your faculty before term starts. There is no need to book, but we would ask you to wait a few minutes if an academic is talking to another visitor when you arrive. Student support and 'spot offer' helpdesks - 12:00 pm - 2:30 pm A chance to talk our dedicated support staff about and get answers to any detailed questions you may have. You will find expert guidance here on anything related to admissions, placement and careers, the library, and the amazing experience that awaits students. And when you are ready to make your application, you could receive a provisional ‘spot offer’ on the day letting you know you have a place in August when you meet academic and eligibility conditions. Academic taster sessions - 12:00 pm - 12:30 pm Take a 25-minute academic taster and experience the unique teaching style that makes University of Southampton a world-leader. Sessions are delivered by the same academic faculty members who will teach programmes when term starts in August 2025. Visitors can choose between ‘Behavioural Economics’ lead by Dr Chitrakalpa Sen (Head of Economics) and ‘Money Talks – Why Finance and Accounting Matter in every career’ lead by Dr Mohammed Anam Akhtar (Head of Business Education). Guided campus tour - 12:00 pm - 12:30 pm Join a 25 minute guided tour of the campus. We will show you our purpose built learning and teaching rooms, the first floor library, break-out and collaborative spaces, computing, financial and technical laboratories and our Student Hub. And you will see the impressive, shared facilities outside the main campus building such as food courts, the health suite, shuttle bus station and sports facilities. Academic taster sessions - 12:30 pm - 1:00 pm Take a 25 minute academic taster and experience the unique teaching style that makes University of Southampton a world-leader. Sessions are delivered by the same academic faculty members who will teach programmes when term starts in August 2025. Visitors can choose between ‘Design Thinking’ lead by Dr Aparna Pasumarthy (Lecturer in Business) and ‘Human Machine Interaction’ lead by Dr Vaibhav Gandhi (Head of Computer Science). Guided campus tour - 12:30 pm - 1:00 pm Join a 25 minute guided tour of the campus. We will show you our purpose built learning and teaching rooms, the first floor library, break-out and collaborative spaces, computing, financial and technical laboratories and our Student Hub. And you will see the impressive shared facilities outside the main campus building such as food courts, the health suite, shuttle bus station and sports facilities. Guided campus tour - 1:00 pm - 1:30 pm Join a 25 minute guided tour of the campus. We will show you our purpose built learning and teaching rooms, the first floor library, break-out and collaborative spaces, computing, financial and technical laboratories and our Student Hub. And you will see the impressive shared facilities outside the main campus building such as food courts, the health suite, shuttle bus station and sports facilities.
chunks.title: Open Day Academic Taster Sessions and Campus Facility Tours

chunks.text: Student hostel accommodation guided visit - 1:30 pm - 2:30 pm A 55 minute tour of our exclusive student accommodation hostel only 6km from campus. Hop on a free shuttlebus from campus and see what your daily journey will be like to your new ‘home from home’ in the peaceful Suncity residential community. You will see amazing room options, shared community spaces and dining facilities. We will bring you back to campus on the shuttlebus after the guided visit ends. Event closes - 2:30 pm Have a safe trip home!
chunks.title: Student Accommodation Tour and Open Day Conclusion

chunks.text: Welcome to the university. We are excited to welcome our first students to the university. Complete these steps to join our community and start your studies. We hope that you are as excited as we are for you to become part of the only Global Top 100 university here in India. As part of our first cohort, you have shown true spirit, incredible tenacity and a genuine confidence in your future. You are one of the first students in the country with the courage to transform your future with a new style of education. You can be proud to be remembered in history as an educational trailblazer. We want to make your journey to University of Southampton Delhi as smooth as possible. Over the next few weeks, we will ask you to complete a number of important steps to join our community: -Set up IT account between 20-22 August 2025. Verify your documents between 22-24 August 2025. Check in to student hostel from 23 August 2025 onwards. Enrol at the university between 27-29 August 2025. Collect your student ID card on Friday 29 August 2025. Student induction and support week from 25-29 August 2025. Careers and employer week from 01-05 September 2025. Pay tuition fees by 08 September 2025. We will provide more detail on these steps over the next few days, and our expert staff will be on hand to guide you through them. It is important that you complete these tasks before teaching starts on campus on 08 September 2025.
chunks.title: Welcome to University of Southampton Delhi's First Cohort

chunks.text: Set up IT account You must set up your IT account to access the university's IT systems. Verify your documents You should have original documents verified so that we can check you meet the requirements of your offer letter. Check in to student hostel If you have booked accommodation, you must check in before you travel to the accommodation or move into your room. Enrol at the university You must officially enrol to join the university as a student. Collect your student ID card You must have a student ID card to access campus and identify you as a member of the student community. Student induction and support week Prepare to 'hit the ground running' with support and advice from professional and academic teams. Sessions take place on campus from 25-29 August 2025. Careers and employer week Get advice from employers and our careers team on being career-ready while you study. Sessions take place on campus from 01-05 September 2025. Pay tuition fees You must pay the next instalment of your tuition fees before the academic term starts. Academic term starts Seminars and lectures start from Monday 08 September 2025. Check your timetable so you know where and when.
chunks.title: Essential Tasks and Events for New University Students

chunks.text: Offer Holder event 19 July 3.30pm-7.00pm Event Status: Completed Date & Time: July 19, 2025 3:30 PM IST Location: International Tech Park Gurgaon, Near Sec 59, Off, Golf Course Ext Rd, Gurugram, Haryana 122101, India Recommended for: Students with an offer to study with us (and their families) Book now Discover University of Southampton Delhi for the August 2025 intake! If you are an offer holder, or are already committed to studying with us, this special event will prepare you for August. Join us from 3.30pm to 7.00pm. Experience a glimpse of life at University of Southampton Delhi, explore our facilities, meet members of our community, and get a sense of what it means to be part of a vibrant, globally connected learning environment. Don’t miss the chance to see where your learning journey could begin. We look forward to welcoming you! Visitor arrival and registration - 3:30 pm Enjoy some refreshments as you sign in at Reception and decide what sessions you would like to attend on campus. Academic faculty meet and greet - 3:30 pm - 4:00 pm Talk to the world-leading academics who will deliver programmes from August. These short informal and individual sessions are your chance to ask questions about the programme content, the quality of academic teaching, and a way to get to know your faculty before term starts. There is no need to book, but we would ask you to wait a few minutes if an academic is talking to another visitor when you arrive. Student support, finance and accommodation helpdesks - 3:30 pm - 4:00 pm A chance to talk our dedicated support staff about and get answers to any detailed questions you may have. You will find expert guidance here on anything related to admissions, placement and careers, the library, and the amazing experience that awaits students. Our finance team will be on hand if you still have a tuition fee deposit to pay or you wish to secure accommodation at the student hostel. University leadership formal welcome - 4:00 pm - 4:45 pm We invite you to a 40-minute session to hear university leaders from the India and the UK explain why University of Southampton Delhi is a true landmark for Higher Education in India. You will also discover the distinctive style of teaching and learning, world-class research and support for enterprise and start up business. To complete the session, we will showcase the amazing careers that alumni have embarked upon when they have graduated from a University of Southampton programme.
chunks.title: University of Southampton Delhi Offer Holder Event Overview & Welcome

chunks.text: Academic taster sessions - 4:45 pm - 5:15 pm Take a 25 minute academic taster and experience the unique teaching style that makes University of Southampton a world-leader. Sessions are delivered by the same academic faculty members who will teach programmes when term starts in August 2025. Visitors can choose between ‘Behavioural Economics’ lead by Dr Chitrakalpa Sen (Head of Economics) and ‘Money Talks – Why Finance and Accounting Matter in every career’ lead by Dr Mohammed Anam Akhtar (Head of Business Education). Academic taster sessions - 5:15 pm - 5:45 pm Take a 25 minute academic taster and experience the unique teaching style that makes University of Southampton a world-leader. Sessions are delivered by the same academic faculty members who will teach programmes when term starts in August 2025. Visitors can choose between ‘Design Thinking’ lead by Dr Aparna Pasumarthy (Lecturer in Business) and ‘Human Machine Interaction’ lead by Dr Vaibhav Gandhi (Head of Computer Science). Academic faculty meet and greet - 4:45 pm - 7:00 pm Talk to the world-leading academics who will deliver programmes from August. These short informal and individual sessions are your chance to ask questions about the programme content, the quality of academic teaching, and a way to get to know your faculty before term starts. There is no need to book, but we would ask you to wait a few minutes if an academic is talking to another visitor when you arrive. Student support, finance and accommodation helpdesks - 4:45 pm - 7:00 pm A chance to talk our dedicated support staff about and get answers to any detailed questions you may have. You will find expert guidance here on anything related to admissions, placement and careers, the library, and the amazing experience that awaits students. Our finance team will be on hand if you still have a tuition fee deposit to pay or you wish to secure accommodation at the student hostel.

chunks.text: Guided campus tour - 5:45 pm - 6:15 pm Join a 25 minute guided tour of the campus. We will show you our purpose built learning and teaching rooms, the first floor library, break-out and collaborative spaces, computing, financial and technical laboratories and our Student Hub. And you will see the impressive shared facilities outside the main campus building such as food courts, the health suite, shuttle bus station and sports facilities Student hostel accommodation guided visit - 5:45 pm - 6:45 pm A 55 minute tour of our exclusive student accommodation hostel only 6km from campus. Hop on a free shuttlebus from campus and see what your daily journey will be like to your new ‘home from home’ in the peaceful Suncity residential community. You will see amazing room options, shared community spaces and dining facilities. We will bring you back to campus on the shuttlebus after the guided visit ends. Networking and High Tea - 6:15 pm - 7:00 pm A great mixer session with academic faculty, other offer holders and support staff. Reflect on the day, ask any last-minute questions and enjoy some refreshments before you leave the campus. Event closes - 7:00 pm Have a safe trip home! International Tech Park Gurgaon, Near Sec 59, Off, Golf Course Ext Rd, Gurugram, Haryana 122101, India

chunks.text: "Careers in International Management" online event Event Status: Completed Date & Time: July 11, 2025 7:00 PM IST Recommended for: Offer holders and potential applicants Book now Many students will look for an international career when they complete their programme. University of Southampton graduates are ready to compete globally for the best jobs with the skills, experience and a confidence that comes from studying at an internationally recognised institution. Dr Aparna Pasumarthy, Lecturer in Business, will explain how learning and teaching on both MSc International Management and BSc (Hons) Business Management programmes uniquely equips graduates for international work. And you will have chance to hear directly from Veenu Jain, a University of Southampton graduate from MSc Management. Veenu can show how the right programme, hard work and the right approach to securing a great career pays dividends. This online event is scheduled for Friday 11 July, from 7.00 PM to 8.00 PM IST.
chunks.title: Online Event: Careers in International Management
